92 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
ANT, GIANT
WORKER SOLDIER QUEEN
FREQUENCY Rare Very rare Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d100 1/5 the number of workers 1
SIZE Small Medium Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 180ft 180ft Can’t move
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17] 3 [17] 4 [16]
HIT DICE 2 3 10
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d6 piercing) 1 bite (2d4 piercing & 3d4 
poison) None
SENSES Standard Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE 10% 10% 100%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1) Low (5-7)
MORALE 55 60 N/A
LOOT Nil Nil Cache 3 (x3) and Cache 5
EXPERIENCE 30 +1/hp 50 +2/hp 700 +13/hp
Giant ants are a dangerous threat to dungeon adventurers, but 
they can also be encountered above ground.
The population of a giant ant colony is determined by rolling for 
the number of workers (1d100). There will also be one soldier 
for each five workers (round fractions up).
The queen can’t move or fight, and lives at the heart of the col -
ony. It takes twelve workers to carry her, moving at 30ft. If the 
queen’s chamber is invaded, twelve workers will try to carry her 
to safety while the rest of the colony (workers and soldiers alike) 
attacks the intruders.
If the queen is killed, the entire colony will be confused (as the 
7th level druidic spell) for 1d6 rounds. They will then leave the 
colony in search of a new one.
Immunities and Resistances
Many-legged. A giant ant cannot be knocked prone, overborne, 
or grappled.
Special Features
Climber. Giant ants can move on walls and ceilings as if they 
were floors.
Special Attacks
Melee Attack: Poison (Soldiers only).  Anyone bitten by a soldier 
ant will take 3d4 hit points of damage from poison in addition 
to the piercing damage unless they succeed at a saving throw 
vs poison, in which case the poison damage is 1d4 hit points.
Unarmed Combat. Giant ants cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics.
Languages
None.

APE
GORILLA CARNIVOROUS GIANT
FREQUENCY Rare Very rare Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 3d6 2d4 1
SIZE Medium Large Gigantic
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 120ft 120ft 180ft

GORILLA
Wild gorillas live in remote places and usually avoid conflict.
Special Attacks
Mangle. When both of the gorilla’s fist attacks make a success -
ful hit against the same target, the gorilla automatically inflicts 
an additional 1d6 points of blunt damage from mangling the 
target. The target is not held or immobilised by this, and can 
continue to act normally in combat.
Ranged Attack. Gorillas can throw rocks for 1d6 blunt damage. 
The range increment is 20ft (-2 beyond 20ft, -4 beyond 40ft).
Unarmed Combat.  Gorillas can choose to overbear instead of 
making a regular melee attack. If not fended off, they score an 
unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Over-
bear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/-0.
Languages
Usually none. In some campaigns there may exist colonies of 
Awakened Apes that speak either their own language or Com -
mon, wear simple clothes, and use simple tools and weapons.
CARNIVOROUS APE
OSRIC’s source literature has a tradition of aggressive ape-like 
creatures, such as the White Apes of Barsoom or Thak in Robert 
E. Howard’s Rogues in the House . In OSRIC, certain humanoids, 
and particularly hobgoblins, form alliances with carnivorous 
apes and share lairs with them.
Special Attacks
Mangle. When both of the carnivorous ape’s fist attacks make 
a successful hit against the same target, the carnivorous ape 
automatically inflicts an additional 1d8 points of blunt damage 
from mangling the target. The target is not held or immobilised 
by this, and can continue to act normally in combat.
Ranged Attack. Carnivorous apes can throw rocks for 1d8 blunt 
damage. The range increment is 20ft (-2 beyond 20ft, -4 be -
yond 40ft).
Unarmed Combat. Carnivorous apes can choose to overbear in-
stead of making a regular melee attack. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +3/-3. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2.
Languages
The carnivorous ape doesn’t speak any languages, but may un-
derstand a few simple words in a human or humanoid language.
GIANT APE
The giant ape is enormous: King Kong scale.
Special Attacks
Trample. On its move, as a charge, or as part of a move into 
combat, the giant ape can tread on any creature smaller than 
size Huge. If the smaller creature can move and isn’t prone, 
then it can immediately (during the giant ape’s turn) choose 
to move to an unoccupied space within 5ft of it. This 5ft move 
doesn’t cost the smaller creature any of its movement. If the 
===== PAGE 95 =====
94 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
smaller creature is prone, can’t move for any other reason, or 
if there’s no unoccupied space within 5ft of it, then the smaller 
creature takes 3d6+12 blunt damage as the giant ape treads 
on it. This doesn’t count as one of the giant ape’s attacks; it is 
just part of movement.
Melee Attack: Grasp and Squeeze. 
Grasp: When one of the giant ape’s fists attacks hits a target, 
and the giant ape’s attack roll is 4 or more higher than the min-
imum it would need to hit the creature, the target is grasped if 
its size is large or smaller. The grasp ends when the creature 
escapes, when either the giant ape or the grasped creature is 
killed, or when something moves the grasped creature such 
that it’s no longer within 5ft of the giant ape.
• While it’s grasped, the seized creature can’t attack, move, or 
use its arms and legs. When the giant ape moves, the grasped 
creature moves with it. The giant ape’s movement speed isn’t 
affected by seizing creatures. Each giant ape can only grasp 
one creature in each fist at a time, and two or more giant apes 
can’t grasp the same creature.
• A creature that’s grasped can try to escape on its  initiative. 
It attempts a Major Test of Strength. If the creature passes, 
then it escapes the giant ape. It can then choose to move up 
to its speed away from the giant ape, but it can’t take any 
other action that round. If it fails, then it remains grasped and 
can’t take any other action that round.
• Other creatures can help the grasped creature to escape by 
attempting a Major Test of Strength. If they pass, they wrestle 
the creature away from the giant ape, so the creature is no 
longer grasped. They can’t take any other action that round. If 
they fail, then the grasped creature remains grasped and the 
other creature can’t take any other action that round.
Squeeze: If the giant ape has grasped a creature, then on its 
initiative, instead of one fist attack, the giant ape can squeeze 
the creature it’s grasped. The creature takes 3d6+12 points 
of blunt damage. The giant ape can then choose whether to 
keep the creature grasped, or to drop it, ending the grasp. 
A dropped creature takes 3d6 blunt damage from the fall. It 
must then attempt a saving throw vs death, and must add its 
Dexterity saving throw modifier to this saving throw. If it fails, 
it lands prone.
Range Attack: Throw Tree. If the giant ape doesn’t have any crea-
tures grasped, then instead of all its attacks, it can throw a 
nearby tree or boulder. It can uproot a tree or lift a boulder as 
part of this action. The giant ape then throws the tree or boul-
der at a point within 180ft of it that it can see. Each creature 
within 5ft of that point (either scale) must attempt a saving 
throw vs death and must add its Dexterity saving throw mod -
ifier to this saving throw. If it fails this saving throw, it takes 
2d6+12 points of blunt damage.
Stomp. Instead of using its bite attack in combat, the giant ape 
can choose to stomp. When it does so, it jumps with both feet, 
and lands, making the ground shake. Creatures within 10ft of 
the giant ape must attempt a saving throw vs death, and must 
add their Dexterity saving throw modifier to this saving throw. 
If they’re flying, they automatically pass this saving throw. If 
they fail, they fall prone (they don’t die; death is just the saving 
throw category used).
Unarmed Combat. Giant apes cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics.
Languages
The giant ape doesn’t speak any languages, but may under -
stand a few simple words in a human or humanoid language.
AURUMVORAX
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 90ft; 30ft burrowing
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 12
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (2d4 piercing)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 105
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 2,250 +16/hp
Also known as the “golden gorger” for its beautiful fur coat, the 
aurumvorax is a long-bodied, eight-legged carnivore. It lives in 
plains or woods. Though as small as a badger, the creature is 
very dense and heavy, and weighs as much as a bear.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune (gas and poison):  The aurumvorax is immune to gases 
and poison.
Resistant (blunt and fire): The creature takes only half damage 
from blunt weapons and fire damage (round down). 
Many-legged. This creature cannot be knocked prone, over -
borne, or grappled.
Special Features
This small creature is very heavy (600 pounds).
Special Attacks
Melee Attack: Jaw Lock and Rake. 
Jaw Lock: When the aurumvorax’s bite hits, it locks its jaws in the 
target’s flesh. (If you are using a grid, it is in the target’s square). 
If the target moves, it has to pull the aurumvorax with it, and 
since the aurumvorax weighs 600 pounds, this is unlikely. Until 
the jaw lock ends, the aurumvorax can’t bite or lock onto an -
===== PAGE 96 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 95
other target. On its initiative, the aurumvorax can choose to 
release its target (in a grid, it moves to an unoccupied space 
within 5ft) and can take another action. 
• A creature the aurumvorax is locked onto can try to escape 
on its  initiative. It attempts a Major Test of Strength. If the 
creature succeeds at the test, it takes 2d4 piercing damage 
as it pulls the aurumvorax off itself. The jaw lock ends, and 
the creature can then choose to move up to its speed away 
from the aurumvorax, but it can’t take any other action that 
round. If it fails, then the aurumvorax remains locked on and 
its target can’t take any other action that round.
• Other creatures can help the creature locked onto to escape 
by attempting a Major Test of Strength. If they pass, they 
wrestle the creature away from the aurumvorax, so the au -
rumvorax is no longer locked on. The creature rescuing can’t 
take any other action that round. If the creature rescuing fails, 
then the aurumvorax remains locked on and the creature res-
cuing can’t take any other action that round.
Rake: If the aurumvorax is locked onto a creature, then instead 
of attacking, it can rake the creature it’s locked onto. When 
it does so, the aurumvorax’s target automatically takes 2d4 
piercing damage from the aurumvorax’s jaws, and the aurum-
vorax rakes it, attacking with 2d4 of its claws. Each claw inflicts 
1d6 slashing damage (a successful to-hit roll is required).
Unarmed Combat. Aurumvoraxes cannot be overborne or grap-
pled, and do not employ these tactics.
Languages
None.
AXE BEAK (PHORUSRHACID)

These ostrich-like birds run fast, and attack prey with their im -
mensely strong beaks. They are usually found in Lost World 
types of settings, but some cultures or monsters may have 
learned how to domesticate them as mounts for battle.
Unarmed Combat.  Axe beaks do not initiate unarmed combat 
unless they are trained mounts, and cannot be grappled. If not 
fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit 
Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-
2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -/-.
AZER
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful neutral
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 2+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 spear (1d6+2 piercing and 1d3 fire)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 60
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 40 +3/hp
Azer are elementals from the elemental plane of fire. They look 
like dwarfs with flames for hair and brass-coloured skin. In that 
plane of existence, azers may be encountered in much larger 
numbers than shown here.

===== PAGE 97 =====
96 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Fire. The azer ignores fire damage.
Resistant to Poison. When the azer takes poison damage, this 
damage is halved. Round fractions down.
Vulnerable to Cold. When the azer takes cold damage, this dam-
age is doubled.
Special Features
Burning. When you hit the azer with a melee attack while stand-
ing within 5ft of it, you take 1d3 fire damage. 
Glowing. The azer sheds light in a 40ft radius.
Special Attacks
Melee Attack: Burning.  When the azer hits you with a melee at -
tack, you take an extra 1d3 fire damage.
Ranged Attack. 1 spear (1d6+2 piercing); 30ft range increment (-2 
beyond 30ft, -4 beyond 60ft).
Unarmed Combat. Azers can choose to overbear or grapple in -
stead of making a regular melee attack. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with an 18+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+6/-14. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grappling Re -
sult Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
Languages
Azer have their own language, and also speak Ignan, the lingua 
franca of the Elemental Plane of Fire.
B.
BABBLER
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft 
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 5
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d6 slashing), 1 bite (1d8 
piercing)
SENSES Keen-eared (detect invisible 5%)
LAIR CHANCE 15%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 70
LOOT Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 100 +5/hp
Babblers are intelligent, lizard-like creatures that resemble 
a small allosaurus with long arms. They are usually found in 
swamps, fens, and marshes. Babblers particularly like the taste 
of human flesh.
Babblers share their habitat with lizardfolk and may be distant-
ly related to them. Some sages think they’re former lizardfolk 
transformed by a curse. In any event, they are sometimes found 
accompanying lizardfolk raiding parties, and sometimes found 
eating lizardfolk. Since no one can understand the language of 
babblers, there’s no way of knowing the motivations involved.
Immunities and Resistances
Hold Breath.  This creature can go for up to an hour between 
breaths.
Special Features
Swamp Stealth. While motionless in marsh or swamp terrain, the 
babbler has a 30% chance to become completely invisible, as 
if hiding in shadows.
Unarmed Combat. Babblers can choose to overbear instead of 
making a regular melee attack, but cannot grapple or be grap-
pled. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 
1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/
DEF +2/-2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -/-.
Languages
Babblers vocalise constantly, unless they’re asleep or hiding. 
They emit a constant stream of noises that sound like language, 
but magic doesn’t translate the sounds, and nobody has ever 
learned their tongue. They never speak intelligibly. Babblers can 
usually understand the languages of lizardfolk, although they 
can’t speak it.

Baboons are communal, omnivorous creatures found in warm-
er climes. They live in troops. Baboons sometimes enter houses 
to steal food, or raid farms to take goats or swine. They tend to 
flee from serious resistance. 
Unarmed Combat. Baboons can choose to overbear instead of 
making a regular melee attack, and can be grappled. If not 
fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit 
Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2. 
Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.
BADGER
ORDINARY GIANT

(1d6)
SENSES Keen (detect 
invisible 5%)
Keen (detect 
invisible 5%)
LAIR CHANCE 10% 10%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4) Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 55 60
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 30 +1/hp 50 +2/hp
Swift-moving burrowers, badgers will defend their territory to 
the death. They mate for life, and when more than one is indi -
cated, the encounter will be with a family. Adult badger pelts 
will usually bring 1d3×10 gold each, and juvenile pelts are worth 
one-half normal.
The giant badger is simply a larger version of the regular bad -
ger. They are very rare, even in badger-rich areas, but may be 
encountered in prehistoric “lost world” environments. Their 
pelts are worth 1d3 x20 gold each. 
Unarmed Combat.  Badgers cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics.
Language
Depending on the world setting, some unusual badgers (both 
ordinary and giant) may be related to the fey, in which case they 
can speak common and/or fey.
BANSHEE (GROANING SPIRIT)
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 7
MELEE ATTACKS 1 touch (1d8 cold)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect invisible 5%
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 80
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 665 +8/hp
The banshee is often said to be the ghost of an evil elven wom-
an, although many have been encountered who don’t really ap-
pear to fit this description. These spirits are found in remote 
countryside and other lonely natural places. They are mostly in-
corporeal, and float just above the ground, but they cannot fly.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Banshees are turned as type 13 Undead.
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. The banshee is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage, unless the weapon causing 
that damage is magical.
Immunities. This creature is immune to mind control (includ -
ing charm) and hold spells, sleep, gases, poison, disease, cold 
damage, electrical damage, temporary damage, and subdual 
damage.
Vulnerability to Exorcism. An exorcism spell immediately kills the 
creature.
Special Features
Fearful Appearance. The mere sight of a banshee will cause fear 
(as the level 3 illusionist spell) unless the viewer saves vs spells 
(one saving throw, not an ongoing series). Fighting the crea -
ture without looking at it incurs a to-hit penalty of -4.
Special Attacks
Wail (1/day). The banshee can wail only when it is dark. All crea-
tures within 30ft of the banshee when it wails must save ver -
sus spells or die.
Unarmed Combat. Banshees cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics.

98 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS

The barracuda is a fearsome, very fast aquatic predator. The 
natural habitat of this fish is warm salt water. It prefers vulnera-
ble or wounded prey, especially those smaller than itself.
BASILISK
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 6
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d10 piercing)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 75
LOOT Hoard 6
EXPERIENCE 1,000 +8/hp
The basilisk is an eight-legged, reptilian creature with baleful 
eyes and a deadly gaze.
Immunities and Resistances
Reflected Gaze.  The basilisk is vulnerable to its own reflected 
gaze. If confronted with a reflective surface, it must make a 
saving throw vs petrifaction (with a bonus of +4) or be turned 
to stone.
Special Features
Petrifying Gaze. Any creature meeting the gaze of a basilisk must 
successfully save vs petrifaction or instantly be turned to stone 
(save each time the gaze is met). The gaze effects of the basilisk 
extend into both the astral and ethereal planes. This applies 
to anyone who is generally in front of the basilisk, and not us -
ing an indirect method of sight such as a mirror. Attacking the 
creature by looking at a reflection incurs a to-hit penalty of -2. 
Attacking blindly incurs a to-hit penalty of -4.
Unarmed Combat.  Basilisks cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics.

 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 99
ORDINARY BATS
This listing includes all types of normal bats. They will only at -
tack when trapped, seeking to escape. When startled, they will 
swarm, causing confusion, extinguishing torches, etc. 
Immunities and Resistances
Echolocator. Sight isn’t this creature’s primary sense. Silence 
affects it in the same way that darkness affects sighted crea -
tures. Invisible opponents are detected and may be attacked 
without any penalty. 
Special Attacks
Bat Swarm.  If more than 5 bats are startled and swarm, they 
cause confusion, extinguish torches, and can interrupt spell 
casting. Every 5 bats in the swarm occupies an area of about 
10x10ft. Any person in the area must make a saving throw 
vs. petrifaction or be affected in some way by the bats, in the 
following order: (1) an open flame carried by the character is 
extinguished, (2) if the character is not carrying an open flame 
but is casting a spell, the spell is lost, (3) if the character is nei-
ther carrying an open flame nor casting a spell, the character is 
confused and cannot take actions for 1d4 rounds.
GIANT BATS
These are giant versions of carnivorous ordinary bats. Their 
bodies grow up to 3ft long, and they can have wingspans of up 
to 6ft. 
Immunities and Resistances
Echolocator. Sight isn’t this creature’s primary sense. Silence 
affects it in the same way that darkness affects sighted crea -
tures. Invisible opponents are detected and may be attacked 
without any penalty.
Special Features
Highly Manoeuvrable. Users of missile weapons will suffer a -3 
“to hit” penalty unless the attacker’s dexterity is 13 or higher. 
Giant bats must land on their intended victims to attack, losing 
this benefit when they do so.
Special Attacks
Disease. There is a 1% chance that anyone bitten will contract 
rabies, or some similar blood-borne infection.
BAT, MOBAT
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d8
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 30ft; 150ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18] or 10 [10]
HIT DICE 4-6
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (2d4)
SENSES Echolocation
LAIR CHANCE 15%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 65, 70 or 75
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE
4HD: 135 +3/hp
5HD: 190 +4/hp
6HD: 300 +6/hp
The very large mobat is found in warm regions where a plentiful 
supply of warm-blooded prey is available. These evil creatures 
may work with other evil species of any kind that can commu -
nicate with them.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise Attack. Mobats surprise opponents on a 1-3 (on 1d6).
Immunities and Resistances
Echolocator. Sight isn’t this creature’s primary sense. Silence 
affects it in the same way that darkness affects sighted crea -
tures.
Special Features
Armour Class. When mobats are airborne their AC is 2 [18], but 
when grounded or attacking their AC is 10 [10].
Special Attacks
Shriek. Mobats can emit a terrifying shriek. If a saving throw 
vs paralysis is failed, the victims (all within a 20ft radius) are 
forced to cover their ears, and are thus rendered defenceless. 
The mobat can do this in any round, but cannot shriek and 
attack in the same round.
Language
Mobats do not have a language other than the neutral evil align-
ment speech. They can (and do) cooperate with other evil crea-
tures—on a disorganised basis if there is no communication, 
or in more organised fashion if they can communicate by align-
ment tongue or by spells.
===== PAGE 101 =====

BATRACHIAN
BATRACHIAN TADPOLE
FREQUENCY Rare Very Rare

SIZE Medium Small

ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] (4 [16] for advanced types) 8 [12]
HIT DICE 1 1d2  hit points
MELEE ATTACKS 1 spear (1d6 + poison) or 1 bite 
(1d4+1) and 2 claws (1d2) 1 bite (1d2)
SENSES Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE 20% 100%
INTELLIGENCE Low to average (5-10) Animal (1)
MORALE 50 50

Individual 1, 2, 4 each; in lair, Cache 
3 (x5) and 10% chance of any 2 
magic items
Nil
EXPERIENCE 18 +1/hp 1 +0/hp
Batrachians are humanoid frog-people of several different varieties. The more 
advanced batrachian species/cultures use javelins, spears (or harpoons) and 
armour. 
There is a 25% chance that batrachians will have 2d4 giant frogs (75%) or poi -
sonous giant frogs (25%) in their lair. Advanced batrachians with poisonous gi-
ant frogs available may (50% chance) have anointed their spears or harpoons 
with poison.
Batrachian tadpoles spend most of their time in water, and usually dwell in a 
large, central pool, but they can emerge and fight if threatened.
Rumours persist of batrachian ghouls, which may be under a tribal priest or 
witch-crafter’s command.
If 30 or more batrachians are encountered at once, there will be 1d6 batra -
chian leaders with 2 HD. These larger batrachians have a strength bonus of 
+1 to damage.
Batrachians can have witch-priests (maximum of 5th level) or witch-crafters 
(maximum of 5th/4th level).
Immunities and Resistances
Hold Breath. This creature can go for up to an hour between breaths.
Special Features
Ambush. Batrachians are well-camouflaged and 
can remain motionless for long periods. They 
are also cold-blooded and hence hard to see 
with infravision. As a result, they surprise oppo-
nents on a 1-3, and on a 1-5 if hopping.
Clumsy. If not armed with a long weapon like a 
spear or harpoon, a batrachian will always lose 
initiative due to its clumsy movements on land.
Special Attacks
Hop. A hop counts as a charge, with an additional 
+1 “to hit” bonus with a weapon. A batrachian 
making a hop attack with a spear or harpoon 
inflicts double damage. 
Ranged Attack. Adult batrachians can attack with 
javelins. 1 javelin (1d6 piercing). Range incre -
ment 20ft (-2 at 21+ft, -4 at 41+ft).
Unarmed Combat.  Batrachians can choose to 
overbear or grapple instead of making a regu -
lar melee attack. If not fended off, they score an 
unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20 (6+ if wearing 
armour). Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2. Overbear 
Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.

BLACK BEAR
Of all bears, the black bear is the least aggressive. If attacked, 
they will defend themselves and their cubs. The black bear is 
omnivorous and possesses good hearing and smell, but has 
weak vision.
Special Attacks
Melee Attack: Bear Hug. Any claw attack with a to-hit roll of 18 or 
more results in the target being hugged for an additional 2d4 
hit points.
Unarmed Combat. Black bears cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics. 
BROWN BEAR
The brown bear, of which the grizzly is the most famous, is an 
aggressive strain of bear. Like black bears, they are omnivorous 
and possess good hearing and smell, but have weak vision. 
Immunities and Resistances
Death Stamina. If reduced to zero hit points, the brown bear will 
continue to fight until either 1d4 rounds have elapsed or it is 
reduced to -9 hit points.
Special Attacks
Melee Attack: Bear Hug. Any claw attack with a to-hit roll of 18 or 
more results in the target being hugged for an additional 2d6 
hit points.
Unarmed Combat. Brown bears may attempt to overbear a lone 
opponent, and can (technically) grapple and be grappled. If not 
fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit 
Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +5/-5. 
Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2.

CAVE BEAR
The cave bear, a giant relic of past ages, is particularly carnivo -
rous, in contrast to its more omnivorous relatives. 
Immunities and Resistances
Death Stamina. If reduced to zero hit  points, the cave bear will 
continue to fight until either 1d4 rounds have elapsed or it is 
reduced to -9 hit points.
Special Attacks
Melee Attack: Bear Hug. Any claw attack with a to-hit roll of 18 or 
more results in the target being hugged for an additional 2d8 
hit points.
POLAR BEAR
Polar bears have white fur and are excellent swimmers. They do 
not have a bear hug attack.
Immunities and Resistances
Death Stamina. If reduced to zero hit  points, the polar bear will 
continue to fight until either 1d4+1 rounds have elapsed or it 
is reduced to -13 hit points.

BEE, GIANT (HONEYBEE)
WORKER SOLDIER
FREQUENCY Rare Very Rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1d10 (20d10 in lair) 1 (3d6 in lair)
SIZE Medium Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 90ft; 300ft flying 120ft; 300ft flying
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] 5 [15]
HIT DICE 3+1 4+2
MELEE ATTACKS 1 sting (1d3 + 
poison)
1 sting (1d4 + 
poison)
SENSES Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE 20% 90%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4) Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 60 65
LOOT See below See below
EXPERIENCE 100 +4/hp 150 +5/hp
Giant honeybees live in hives. These lairs will contain non-com-
batant queens (10 HD but no effective attack) and 2d3 non-com-
batant drones (2 HD and no effective attack). Giant honeybees 
will flee from smoke or fire unless their lair is threatened.
Giant bee hives will always contain honey worth 10d10 gold 
pieces. There is a 15% chance that a hive will contain royal jel -
ly. If it does, the royal jelly can be fashioned into 2d3 potions 
of extra healing by a magic-user of 7th or higher level—or a 
magic-user of 12th level or higher may make it into an unguent 
which preserves the appearance of youth. Regular application 
of this unguent for 1 year makes the user appear 2d3 years 
younger. Such an unguent is typically worth 3d6x1,000 gp to 
wealthy nobles.
Special Attacks
One Poison Sting. A giant bee can only sting once per encoun -
ter; it has a 25% chance of dying after stinging, otherwise the 
stinger will slowly regrow. The sting is poisonous; if it hits, the 
target must make a saving throw vs. poison or take additional 
points of poison damage. For worker bees the saving throw is 
made with no penalty and the additional damage is 2d4. For 
soldier bees, the saving throw is made at a -1 penalty and the 
additional damage is 5d4.
BEE, GIANT (BUMBLEBEE)
BUMBLEBEE QUEEN
FREQUENCY Rare Rare
NO.

SIZE Medium Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral

Giant bumblebees live in nests rather than hives. These lairs 
will contain combatant queens and 2d3 non-combatant drones 
(2 HD and no effective attack). Giant bumblebees will flee from 
smoke or fire unless their lair is threatened.
Giant bumblebee nests will always contain honey worth 10d10 
gold pieces. There is a 15% chance that a hive will contain royal 
jelly. If it does, the royal jelly can be fashioned into 2d3 potions 
of extra healing by a magic-user of 7th or higher level—or a 
magic-user of 12th level or higher may make it into an unguent 
which preserves the appearance of youth. Regular application 
of this unguent for 1 year makes the user appear 2d3 years 
younger. Such an unguent is typically worth 3d6x1,000 gp to 
wealthy nobles.
Special Attacks
Bumblebee Sting. A giant bumblebee can only sting once per en-
counter; it has a 25% chance of dying after stinging, otherwise 
the stinger will slowly regrow. Queens do not die from stinging, 
but still lose the stinger. The sting is poisonous; if it hits, the 
target must make a saving throw vs. poison with a -1 penalty 
or take 5d4 additional points of poison damage.

Unarmed Combat. Giant beetles cannot be overborne or grap -
pled, and do not employ these tactics. 
GIANT FIRE BEETLE
With their glowing red glands, one above each eye and one on 
the back of the abdomen, these nocturnal beetles are much 
sought after by adventurers. Fire beetles can be found both 
above and below ground, feeding on decaying matter like all 
beetles.
Special Features
The glands will continue to glow for 1d6 days after removal, illu-
minating a 10ft radius without fail.
GIANT BOMBARDIER BEETLE
Frequently found in forested regions, this beetle primarily scav-
enges for food above ground. It will collect decaying matter into 
a large heap and there lay its eggs.
Special Attacks
Noxious Gas. When attacked it has a 50% chance to release an 
8ft cube of noxious gas. The gas will cause 3d4 hp of damage 
and the sound of its release will stun (20% chance) and deafen 
(20%) all within a cloud (5ft radius beginning directly in front of 
the beetle). The stun effect will prevent action for 2d4 rounds 
and the deafness will persist for 2d6 rounds. The beetle must 
“recharge” for 1 round before releasing gas again, and cannot 
release gas more than twice in 8 hours. 
GIANT WATER BEETLE
Hunting by scent and vibration, and dwelling in fresh water at 
least 30ft deep, the water beetle is a powerful threat to the un -
wary adventurer. Omnivorous and insatiable, the water beetle 
will consume whatever it can get its powerful mandibles around.
Special Features
Hold Breath.  This creature can go for up to an hour between 
breaths.
Unusual Senses. Giant water beetles automatically detect invis -
ible opponents because their primary senses are scent and 
vibration.

104 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
GIANT BORING BEETLE
Giant boring beetles may be found in enormous dead trees, 
but are usually encountered in tunnel complexes where there 
is sufficient rotting wood and moisture to support the fungal 
cultures they eat. There are rumours that some have developed 
a “hive-mind” similar to ants and when threatened they may dis-
play surprising group intelligence in the defence of their home.
GIANT DEATH WATCH BEETLE
Giant Death Watch beetles live in temperate areas and some -
times underground. They disguise themselves by sticking rub -
bish, branches and detritus to their carapaces with their saliva. 
Immunities and Resistances
Many-legged. This creature cannot be knocked prone, over -
borne, or grappled.
Special Attacks
Death Vibration. These giant beetles are feared for making ter -
rible, deadly bass vibrations by scraping their hind legs—when 
a beetle does this, all within 30ft of the creature must save vs 
death or die. Even those who pass their save take 4d6 hp dam -
age. After using this special attack, the Death Watch beetle must 
wait at least 12 turns before using it again.
GIANT RHINOCEROS BEETLE
Usually encountered in the tropics, these massive creatures 
(roughly 12ft long, plus another 6ft of horn) live on the fruit and 
vegetation they crush as they roam about.
Immunities and Resistances
Many-legged. This creature cannot be knocked prone, over -
borne, or grappled.
GIANT STAG BEETLE
Giant stag beetles live in woodlands near farmland, preferring 
for food the young shoots of cultivated grains. For this reason, 
they can often plague regions and even cause famine should 
enough of them descend upon an area. They attack with a bite 
and with their two horns.
Immunities and Resistances
Many-legged. This creature cannot be knocked prone, over -
borne, or grappled.
BEHIR
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED Encountered: 1d2
SIZE Huge
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 12
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (2d4) or bite plus 6 claws (1d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect invisible 5%
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 105
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE 2,750 +16/hp
A behir is a particularly nasty subterranean beast with a croc -
odile-like head on a 40ft long, snake-like body with twelve sets 
of legs.
Since behirs swallow prey whole, the beast’s stomach will con -
tain 10d4 gems (60% chance), 1d8 jewellery (30% chance), and 
a misc. magic item that can survive its digestive juices (10% 
chance).
Immunities and Resistances
Immunities. The behir is immune to electricity damage and poi -
son.
Many-legged. This creature cannot be knocked prone, over -
borne, or grappled, and does not employ unarmed combat tac-
tics.
Special Features
Climber. This creature can move on walls and ceilings as if they 
were floors, but moves at half speed when doing so.
Special Attacks
Lightning Breath.  Every 10 rounds a behir can also breathe a 
20ft lightning bolt that does 4d6+24 damage unless a save vs 
breath is made for half damage. 
Swallow Whole. On a bite attack that rolls a natural 20, the behir 
swallows the victim. If the victim is not rescued, they will die 
at the end of 6 rounds spent in the creature’s stomach. It 
is possible for the victim to cut their way out (the inside of 
the behir is armour class 10 [10]). For each round the victim 
is inside, subtract 1 from their damage that they inflict. This 
penalty is cumulative for each round, so after four rounds the 
damage inflicted is at -4.
Bite and Claw. The behir’s first attack is a bite, but if it hits with 
this attack it can coil around the victim and bring its claws into 
play. On the second round after a successful bite, the behir 
===== PAGE 106 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 105
can make an additional 6 claw attacks against the victim. The 
victim is held in place, but can continue to act (other than mov-
ing); the behir’s attacks do not shift to automatic damage; it 
must still make to-hit rolls to inflict damage, although it can 
now make a lot more attacks.
Language
Behirs only speak the alignment tongue of neutral evil.
BIRD OF PREY
NORMAL GIANT
FREQUENCY Common Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d20 1
SIZE Small Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 30ft; 360ft flying 
(AA:IV)
30ft; 360ft flying 
(AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1hp) and 2 
claws (1d2)
1 bite (2d6) and 2 
claws (1d6)
SENSES Keen Keen
LAIR CHANCE 20% 20%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE 50 65
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 15 +1/hp 105 +3/hp
NORMAL BIRDS OF PREY
Birds of prey are found across the known world in many shapes, 
forms and colours. They are distinguished from one another by 
plumage, size, beak shape, body length, wing size and talon 
type, as well as by dietary habits. Most are two or three feet 
long, weigh up to about three pounds and have a wingspan of 
around four or five feet.
Eagles, hawks and falcons are all examples of birds of prey. 
Falcons are generally the smallest of the three, tending to be 
around a foot in length and having a wingspan of two to three 
feet. Hawks are usually somewhat larger, typically being around 
two feet long and having a wingspan of four to five feet. Eagles 
can be considerably larger, sometimes exceeding even three 
feet in length and having a wingspan of anywhere from five to 
eight feet. The largest types may weigh up to twelve pounds. 
Special Attacks
Diving Attack (Normal). A diving attack must be made from the air 
at a distance of 120ft or more. It acts as a charge, and grants a 
+2 “to-hit” bonus and double damage from their talons.
GIANT BIRDS OF PREY
There is a 50% chance that randomly-encountered giant birds 
of prey are of a highly-intelligent variety (such as Tolkien’s giant 
eagles). These are similar enough to their animal-type relatives 
to share the rest of the physical statistics, but have higher intel-
ligence, possibly their own culture, and may be related to the 
fey. Otherwise, the giant bird of prey is just a giant version of a 
normal bird of prey.
Special Attacks
Diving Attack (Giant). A diving attack must be made from the air 
at a distance of 60ft or more. It acts as a charge, and grants a 
+4 “to-hit” bonus and double damage from their talons.
Languages
Intelligent giant birds of prey are likely to understand the com -
mon tongue, and may be able to speak it.
BLINDHEIM
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 4+2
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d8 piercing)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 70
LOOT Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 110 +5/hp
Blindheims are yellowish, frog-like monsters found in subterra-
nean environments. They are usually bipedal, but move on all 
fours in rough terrain. 
Special Attacks
Blinding Light. These yellowish, frog-like bipeds can project twin 
beams of incredibly bright light from their eyes. Anyone who 
comes within 30ft of the blindheim must save vs aimed magic 
items or be temporarily blinded (for 1d8+12 turns). The saving 
throw is at -3 if the approaching character has infravision. A 
character who is not blinded may attack the blindheim at a 
penalty of -2 “to hit” (if they are avoiding looking at the crea -
ture) or as normal (if they are for some reason immune to 
bright light).

Unarmed Combat. Blindheims do not employ unarmed combat 
but can be grappled or overborne. If not fended off, they score 
an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-6. 
Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF -1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: 
ATK/DEF -2/+2.
BLINK DOG
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 4d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful good
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d6 piercing)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 65
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 175 +5/hp
Blink dogs are medium-sized dogs, dark brown with white high-
lights. They are intelligent, as smart as the average human. 
Blink dogs are so called because they can use a limited form of 
teleportation, called a “blink,” in a somewhat random fashion 
and at random intervals.
Coupled with their pack hunting tactics, blink dogs are fear -
some opponents. If the pack takes more than 25% losses all will 
simultaneously blink out and not return. There is a long-stand -
ing hatred between blink dogs and phase cats (coeurls) and the 
two will attack each other on sight. Blinking is a natural ability 
for these animals and they will never materialise in an occupied 
space or within a solid object.
If a blink dog lair is found there is a 60% chance of 3d4 pups. 
These younglings fight as 1 HD monsters and inflict 1d2 points 
of damage per attack, though they are far more likely to flee us-
ing their teleportation ability. A captured pup will make a loyal 
companion to a good aligned human, or, it can be sold for 1,500 
gp (± 100–600 gp). 
Special Attacks
Blink. In combat (and if needed, in other situations) blink dogs 
use a semi-random, short-range teleportation, called a “blink.” 
Each round, roll to see if the blink dog uses this ability: roll 
1d20, and if the result is 12 or more, the blink dog teleports to 
attack. A second roll of the 1d20 reveals where the dog reap -
pears: 01–15 = behind (rear attack at +2), 16–18 = non-shielded 
or right flank (defender cannot use shield in this round), 19 = 
shielded or left flank (no benefit), 20 = front (no benefit). The 
blink dog blinks and attacks in the same round, gaining any 
bonuses based on its position relative to the target.
Unarmed Combat. Blink dogs cannot be grappled or overborne, 
but might overbear a lone opponent. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +1/na. Grappling Re -
sult Mods: ATK/DEF na/na.
Language
Blink dogs communicate amongst themselves via their high-
pitched barks and low growls. They do not speak or understand 
Common.

WILD BOAR
Boars are wild pigs with tusks. They are extremely aggressive and 
quite dangerous. The first two boars encountered will automati-
cally be adults; each additional boar indicated has a 75% chance 
of being a sounder (boar piglet). Sounders will flee from attackers, 
and are non-combatants. 
Special Features
Die Hard. Adult boars will fight for 1d4+1 rounds after reaching 
0 hit points, or until -7 hit points are accumulated.
Unarmed Combat. Wild boar cannot be grappled or overborne, 
but might overbear a lone opponent. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+10/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/na. Grappling Re-
sult Mods: ATK/DEF na/na.
===== PAGE 108 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 107
GIANT BOAR
Ancient ancestor to the modern boar, the giant boar is even 
more aggressive. They have a smaller proportion of sounders in 
a group: additional boars after the first two (automatic adults) 
have only a 25% chance of being sounders (as opposed to 75% 
for normal boars). Also, giant boar sounders do not flee; they are 
treated as ordinary adult wild boars (without the die hard ability).
Special Features
Die Hard (Giant Boar). Adult giant boars will fight for 1d4 rounds 
after reaching 0 hit  points, or until -11 hit  points are accumu -
lated.
Unarmed Combat. Wild boar cannot be grappled or overborne, 
but might overbear a lone opponent. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+10/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +3/na. Grappling Re-
sult Mods: ATK/DEF na/na.
BOAR, WARTHOG
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d6
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 2 (2d4/2d4)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 60
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 50 +2/hp
Tropical relatives of the common boar, warthogs will only attack 
if threatened or cornered. If more than two are indicated, they 
will consist of a mated pair and young. Young warthogs have 2 hit 
dice rather than 3, but are otherwise the same as adults.
Special Features
Die Hard. All warthogs will fight for 1–2 rounds below 0 hit points, 
or to -6 hp.
Unarmed Combat. Warthogs cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics. 
BROWNIE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 4d4
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Lawful good
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 1d4 hp
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d3)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE High (13-14)
MORALE 50
LOOT Cache 1, 2, and 3
EXPERIENCE 50 +1/hp
Found in sylvan woodland, often along with fauns, unicorns 
and similar creatures, brownies are shy and retiring. They can 
sometimes be persuaded to help good-aligned creatures. They 
are skilled craftsmen, adept at making and repairing ordinary 
things, and are highly magical.
Surprise and Initiative
Cannot be Surprised. Thanks to their excellent senses and habit-
ual alertness, brownies cannot be surprised.
Immunities and Resistances
Improved Saving Throw.  Brownies are inherently lucky and 
strongly defended against magic, which means they make all 
saving throws as follows: Aimed magic items 11, Breath weap-
ons 13, Death/Paralysis/ Poison 7, Petrifaction/Polymorph 10, 
Spells 12.
As Familiar. If a brownie is a familiar, it confers a +2 bonus on all 
saving throws to its character ally. 
Special Features
Spell-like Abilities. Brownies can cast each of the following spells, 
without material components:
At will: Brownies may become invisible at will, provided they re-
main still while doing so.
1/day: confusion, continual light, dancing lights, dimension door, 
mending, mirror image, protection from evil, and ventriloquism. 
Unarmed Combat.  Brownies do not employ unarmed combat 
but can be grappled or overborne. If not fended off, they score 
an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-
8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.
===== PAGE 109 =====
108 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Language
Brownies speak their own tongue, as well as elven and halfling. 
They also speak sylvan, the language of other fey creatures such 
as spites, nymphs and dryads.
BUGBEAR
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 6d6 (9d6 in lair)
SIZE Large (7ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 3+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (2d4 or by weapon)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Low to average (5-10)
MORALE 65
LOOT Individual 1, 2, 3, 4 (each); in 
lair, Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 135 +4/hp
Bugbears are related to goblins, although they are much larger 
and shaggier than true goblins, with large heads and a gift for 
stealthiness. They can live both above and below ground, and 
do not have penalties for fighting in sunlight. 
Bugbears use a variety of weapons usually gathered from fall -
en opponents. These include axes, morning stars, hammers, 
swords, spears and missile weapons. Bugbear hunting parties 
(12+ creatures) always have a leader who fights as a 4 Hit Dice 
creature. (AC 4 [16], 22–25 hp, and do 2d8+1 damage).
Within the lair there will be a chieftain and sub-chief. The chief 
is also a 4 Hit Dice creature with the following stats: (AC 3 [17], 
28–30 hp, 2d8+2 damage). The sub-chief has the same stats as 
a leader (see above).
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Bugbears surprise opponents on a 1–3 on 1d6.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack. Bugbears armed with ranged weapons may use 
them as humans do.
Unarmed Combat. Bugbears can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
12+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-2. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +2/-2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.
Language
Bugbears speak hobgoblin and goblin as well as chaotic evil and 
their own form of speech.
BULETTE
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 140ft; 30ft burrowing
ARMOUR CLASS -2 [22] (see below)
HIT DICE 9
MELEE ATTACKS 3 (3d6/3d6/4d12)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 0%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE N/A (fights to the death)
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 2,000 +12/hp
Bulettes are sometimes called “land sharks” because of their 
habit of burrowing while the crests of their backs break the 
surface. Like sharks, they are huge, vicious, and always hungry. 
They seem to be someone’s experiment gone wrong: a turtle 
crossed with an armadillo with just a touch of demon thrown 
in. Their favourite food is horse but they will eat just about any-
thing that moves. The only kind of meat they don’t seem to like 
is dwarf or elf. They have been known to dig halflings right out 
of their holes.
Being fearless, a bulette will attack anything that moves on 
sight. They have been known to attack an armed party to get 
at the horses. When they attack, they use their vicious bite and 
their two front claws. 
Their plates are highly prized by armourers. They can be fash -
ioned into +1 or +2 shields.

===== PAGE 110 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 109
Special Features
Sectioned Armour Class. Bulettes are well armoured AC -2 [22], 
but they have a vulnerable spot. When fighting, the bulette 
raises one of its back plates as a warning crest (part of the rea-
son it is often described as a “land shark”. The area underneath 
the crest is unprotected, AC 6 [14]. However, an attacker must 
be behind the bulette in order to attack the unarmoured spot.
Special Attacks
8ft jump. When cornered or seriously wounded, a bulette will 
attempt to leap 8ft vertically and land directly on top of their 
victim, clawing with all four feet.
Unarmed Combat.  Bulettes cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics. 
C
CAMEL
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d12
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 210ft dromedary / 180ft bactrian
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d4)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 60
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 50 +2/hp
An ornery beast of burden, the camel comes in two types, both 
of which have the same stats in game terms. The dromedary has 
only one hump and prefers warm climates. The bactrian camel 
has two humps, is adaptable to a wider range of temperatures 
and environments, and moves 30ft more slowly.
Regardless of the type, all camels can be domesticated, can car-
ry immense loads (up to 6,000 gp). Between 4 and 6 thousand 
gp, speed is reduced to half. 
Special Attacks
Spit. Their usual attack is a bite, but they may also spit. Spitting 
requires no to-hit roll, and has a 25% chance to blind the oppo-
nent for 1d3 rounds (no saving throw).
CARBUNCLE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Chaotic neutral
MOVE 30ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS None
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 50
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE 5 +1/hp
Carbuncles are adorable minor agents of Chaos. They seem to 
be curious little animals like armadillos, and they feed on leaves 
and small insects. Set in the carbuncle’s head above its eyes is a 
large ruby (randomly-determined value of at least 500 gp). If the 
beast dies, any ruby attached to it shatters and cannot be re -
constituted—but it can voluntarily give up its gem, in which case 
the gem will re-grow over a period of several months (re-roll the 
gem’s value each time it re-grows). The carbuncle will not nor -
mally give up its gem unless charmed or otherwise enchanted.
Language
Carbuncles are empathic and communicate via a minor form 
of telepathy. They will approach and seek to join a party of hu -
mans or demi-humans and then try to spread discord by means 
of lies, bearing false witness, or betraying the party’s presence 
to nearby monsters.
CARCASS CREEPER
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d6
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17] or 7 [13] (see below)
HIT DICE 3+1
MELEE ATTACKS 8 (paralysis)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 50%
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A (fights to the death)
LOOT Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 105 +3/hp
===== PAGE 111 =====
110 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
A fearsome creature that roams around in caves and dungeons, 
the carcass creeper has a body resembling a caterpillar, with an 
armoured head that sprouts 8 tentacles. It requires a steady 
supply of fresh corpses in which to lay its eggs.
The armoured head belies a tender, vulnerable body so the 
creeper will rely on its speed and many paralysing tentacles to 
protect itself as it secures prey. Each of the tentacles has a 2ft 
reach, although larger specimens are not unknown.
If a carcass creeper has paralysed all nearby enemies, it will be-
gin biting them, inflicting 1d2 hit  points of damage per round 
(the carcass creeper’s mouth is not usable for a bite in actual 
melee combat).
Special Features
Sectioned Armour Class. The carcass creeper has a well-ar -
moured head, which is AC 3 [17]. Attacks from the sides or 
rear may reach the softer, less-protected portion of the beast, 
which is only AC 7 [13].
Special Attacks
Paralysing Tentacles. Anyone hit by a tentacle must make a sav -
ing throw vs. paralysis or be paralysed for 3d4 turns.
Unarmed Combat.  Carcass creepers cannot be overborne or 
grappled, and do not employ these tactics. 
CARYATID COLUMN
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d12
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 5
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (2d4)
SENSES Special
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A (fights to the death)
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 110 +3/hp
Caryatid columns share many similarities to stone golems, being 
created in much the same way. These constructs resemble intri-
cately-carved columns in the shape of a person, usually in pairs. 
The most common type is maidens, however they can resemble 
practically anyone. In their dormant state they are almost indis-
tinguishable from a normal statue. The only indication of their 
true nature is a faint outline of sword held in one hand. They 
are created as guardians and will animate only when triggered 
by a certain event. Their trigger must be something simple like 
someone entering a room, lifting a chest, or opening a door. 
Once animated they will do their utmost to prevent the trigger 
from continuing. When their task is complete they will return to 
their previous place and form.
The caryatid column’s main purpose is not combat, but if 
pressed they will use their swords and attack. 
Immunities and Resistances
Saving Throw Bonus.  Caryatid columns make all saving throws 
with a +4 bonus.
Immunities. Caryatid columns are immune to blindness (and 
invisibility), psychic damage, charm, domination, sleep, gases, 
poison, and disease.
Resistances. Normal weapons do half damage and magical 
weapons score full damage, but without their additional mag -
ical effects. 
Special Features
Any weapon, including magic weapons, that hits a caryatid 
column has a 25% chance of breaking. For each plus that the 
weapon has reduces the chance of breaking by 5%; consider 
any magical weapons without any pluses as a +1.
Unarmed Combat.  Caryatid Columns cannot be overborne or 
grappled, and do not employ these tactics. 
CAT
DOMESTIC WILD
FREQUENCY Common Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1 or 2d6 1 or 1d4+1
SIZE Small Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 50ft 180ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] 5 [15]
HIT DICE 1d2 hp 1d6 hp
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d2-1) 3 (1/1/1d2)
SENSES Keen Keen
LAIR CHANCE 75% 5%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE 50 50
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 3 +1/hp 10 +1/hp
Agile and proud, cats are found everywhere. Because of their 
size, domestic cats have very ineffective attacks. When a black 
cat is found as a magic-user’s familiar, the cat confers excellent 
hearing and night vision on its master or mistress.
See also, Lynx, Giant.
===== PAGE 112 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 111
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Cats are only surprised on a 1 (of a d6), and they sur-
prise opponents on a 1–3 (of a d6).
Special Attacks
Rear Claws.  A cat exceeding its needed “to hit” score by 4 or 
more can rake with its rear claws as well as its front, so roll the 
listed damage twice.
Unarmed Combat. Cats cannot be overborne or grappled, and 
do not employ these tactics. 
CATERWAUL
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 180ft or 240ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] (see below)
HIT DICE 4+2
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d4) and 1 bite (1d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 70
LOOT Individual 5, Cache 4, 5 and 7
EXPERIENCE 400 +5/hp
A caterwaul is a bipedal feline predator that stalks darkened 
caves waiting for prey to walk past. These unusual creatures re-
semble black panthers walking upright. On their hind legs they 
move at 180ft, but they can drop to all fours and sprint very fast 
(240ft) for short distances. 
What makes these creatures unique is their unusually high dex-
terity. This will vary from individual to individual. Roll d100 and 
check the chart below.
D100 AC BONUS ATTACKS/ROUND
01–30 0 [0] 1/1
31–60 -1 [+1] 3/2
61–90 -2 [+2] 3/2
91–93 -3 [+3] 2/1
94–97 -4 [+4] 2/1
98 -5 [+5] 2/1
99 -6 [+6] 5/2
00 -7 [+7] 5/2
The attacks per round works much the same way as for fighters, 
referring to a full attack routine (claw, claw, bite) rather than 
individual attacks.
Surprise and Initiative
Caterwauls can only be surprised (for one segment) on a roll of 
1 on a d10.
Special Features
Thief-Type Abilities. Climb 95%, Move quietly 75%, Hide 75%.
Special Attacks
Screech. When a caterwaul attacks for the first time in a com-
bat, it lets out a distinctive screech, which inflicts 1d8 hit points 
of damage to all within 60ft. This may also summon wandering 
monsters.
Unarmed Combat. Caterwauls do not employ unarmed combat 
but can be grappled. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear 
Results Mods: ATK/DEF na/na. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+0/+0.

CATTLE
BISON (BUFFALO) BULL CATTLE
FREQUENCY Uncommon Common Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 4d6 1, plus 50% chance of 3d6 cattle 20d10 
SIZE Large Large Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 150ft 150ft 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17] or 7 [13] 7 [13] 7 [13]
HIT DICE 5 4 3
MELEE ATTACKS 2 horns (1d8 piercing) 2 horns (1d6 piercing) 1 head butt (1d6 blunt)
SENSES Standard Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil Nil Nil
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE 70 65 60
LOOT Nil Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 110 +4/hp 75 +3/hp 50 +2/hp
Unarmed Combat. Cattle cannot be overborne or grappled, and 
do not employ these tactics (they are folded into their trample 
or charge attacks). 
BUFFALO
Native to tropical and subtropical plains, the buffalo will usually 
attack if approached within 60ft. Any attack by one may result 
in the whole herd charging. 
50% of all buffalo species are non-aggressive and will flee from 
threats unless cornered.
Special Features
Sectioned Armour Class. A bison’s head is AC 3 [17]. Only those to 
the side or rear can attack the body’s AC 7 [13].
Special Attacks
When charging, the buffalo will do 3d6 impact damage plus 1d4 
trampling damage. The charge must cover a minimum of 40ft.
BULL
This is an encounter with a wild bull, massive in size and very 
aggressive. If someone moves to within 80ft, it is likely to charge 
(75%), even without apparent provocation. 
Special Attacks
The charge, if either horn hits, inflicts 4d4 points of blunt dam -
age upon impact, in addition to any damage from the horns. 
Any creature smaller than huge is knocked prone. The charge 
must traverse a minimum of 30ft in order to be effective.
CATTLE
These are smaller bulls and cows, either encountered without 
a lead bull or as part of a lead bull’s herd. If there is any inter -
action with the herd, it has a 25% chance of stampeding, and 
a stampede has a 25% chance to head toward the characters, 
becoming a special attack.
Special Attacks
Stampedes will cause characters to be trampled by 2d4 ani -
mals, and take 1d4 damage per animal.
CENTAUR
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 4d6
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral good or Chaotic good
MOVE 180ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15] or 4 [16]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 2 hooves (1d6) and weapon
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 65
LOOT Individual 4 and Cache 3 each; Hoard 
4, 9, and Cache 6 in lair
EXPERIENCE 75 +3/hp
===== PAGE 114 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 113
Centaurs have the body of a horse, but have the upper torso, 
arms and head of a human in place of the horse’s neck and head. 
Antipathetic toward humans, yet friendly toward elves and their 
kin, centaurs prefer unfrequented glades, pastures and wood -
lands. A centaur band is armed with: 50% hardwood clubs (2d4 
damage), 25% composite and/or longbows with the remaining 
centaurs armed with lance and shield (thus, AC 4 [16])—this last 
group being the leaders. All centaurs are capable of attacking 
with melee weapons and two hoof attacks each melee round.
If upon rare occasion encountered in their lair, there will be an 
additional 3d6 (total 7d6 encountered) fighting centaurs, with 
5d6 young. Non-fighting (young) centaurs possess only 1d3 hit  
points. 
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack. Centaurs may use ranged weapons as humans 
can.
Unarmed Combat. Centaurs cannot be overborne or grappled, 
but they may overbear. If not fended off, they score an un -
armed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/na. Over-
bear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +5/na. Grappling Result Mods: 
ATK/DEF na/na.
CENTIPEDE, GIANT
BIG BIGGER BIGGEST
FREQUENCY Uncommon Common Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 5d6 2d12 1d4
SIZE Small Small Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 210ft 150ft 180ft
ARMOUR CLASS 9 [11] 9 [11] 5 [15]
HIT DICE 1 hp 1 to 2 hp 3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (poison only) 1 bite (poison only) 1 bite (1d3 piercing + poison)
SENSES Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil Nil Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0) Non- (0) Non- (0)
MORALE N/A N/A N/A
LOOT Nil Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 31 +1/hp 30 +1/hp 125 +3/hp
Giant centipedes can be encountered in three sizes.
Immunities and Resistances
Many-legged. This creature cannot be knocked prone, over -
borne, or grappled.
Special Features
Climber. This creature can move on walls and ceilings as if they 
were floors.
Special Attacks
Poison Bite (Big).  Weak venom (save at +4) that only does 4d4 
damage if the saving throw fails.
Poison Bite (Bigger). Weak venom (save at +4), but the venom is 
lethal if the saving throw fails.
Poison Bite (Biggest). Relatively powerful venom (no adjustment 
to saving throw). Death is the result of a failed save, but a suc-
cessful save still results in 1d8 acid damage. 
Unarmed Combat.  Giant centipedes cannot be overborne or 
grappled, and do not employ these tactics.

CHIMERA
CHIMERA GORGIMERA
FREQUENCY Rare Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4 1
SIZE Large Large
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft; 180ft flying (AA:II) 120ft; 150ft flying (AA:II)
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16] 3 [17]
HIT DICE 9 10+1
MELEE ATTACKS 2 lion claws (1d4), 2 goat horns (1d3), 1 lion bite 
(2d6), 1 dragon bite (3d4)
2 lion claws (1d4), 1 gorgon-gore (2d4), 1 lion bite 
(2d6), 1 dragon bite (3d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 40% 30%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4) Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 90 95
LOOT Hoard 6 Hoard 6
EXPERIENCE 1,300 +12/hp 2,250 +14/hp
CHIMERA
A chimera has the hind quarters of a goat, the forequarters of a 
lion, the wings of a dragon and 3 heads, one each of the afore -
mentioned creatures. The creature may make up to 6 natural 
attacks per round, two with clawed lion paws, one with each of 
the goat head’s 2 horns, the mighty bite of the lion’s maw and 
another powerful bite by the dragon.
Special Attacks
Breath Weapon. The chimera’s dragon head is capable of breath-
ing fire to a 60ft distance, inflicting 3d8 points of damage. Nor-
mal saving throws vs breath weapon apply (save for half dam-
age).
GORGIMERA
The gorgimera looks much like a chimera, but it has gorgon’s 
head rather than a goat’s head beside the lion and dragon 
heads.
Special Attacks
Breath Weapons. The gorgon head breathes a cloud of petrifying 
gas in a cone 30ft long and 10ft radius at the base (save or be 
turned to stone). The dragon head is capable of breathing fire 
to a 60ft distance, inflicting 3d8 points of damage (save for half 
damage).
Unarmed Combat.  Chimeras and gorgimeras cannot be over -
borne or grappled, and do not employ these tactics. 
Language
Chimeric is an imperfect form of the red dragon tongue, and is 
spoken by both chimeras and gorgimeras. It is also often used 
as a code by those familiar with the language.
COCKATRICE
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d6
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft; 180ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 5 [15]
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d3 + petrifaction)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 70
LOOT Hoard 4
EXPERIENCE 170 +4/hp
These creatures have the head, legs and wings of a rooster, 
while the body is that of a serpent. Its wings are functional and 
allow it to fly. The creature is believed to be from a rooster’s egg 
hatched by a serpent.
Although the beast is capable of inflicting light wounds with its 
beak, the mere touch of the cockatrice is capable of turning any 
creature to stone. 
Special Attacks
Petrifaction. If the cockatrice hits with beak or claws, the touched 
creature must roll a successful saving throw vs petrifaction or 
be instantly turned to stone. The power of this touch is capable 
of affecting astral and ethereal beings.
Unarmed Combat.  Cockatrices cannot be overborne or grap -
pled, and do not employ these tactics. 
===== PAGE 116 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 115
COFFER CORPSE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 8 [12]
HIT DICE 2 
MELEE ATTACKS 1 claw (1d6 + strangle) or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 80%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE N/A (fights to the death)
LOOT Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 30 +2/hp
A coffer corpse resembles a zombie, seeming nothing more 
than a rotting, fetid corpse. They are the bodies of the dead who 
are left behind, never given a proper burial, their souls never 
finding rest.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Coffer corpses are turned as type 7 Undead.
Immune to Mundane Weapons.  The coffer corpse is immune to 
piercing, blunt, and slashing damage, unless the weapon caus-
ing that damage is magical.
Immunities. This creature is immune to mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells, sleep, gases, poison, disease, cold dam-
age, temporary damage, and subdual damage.
Special Features
If a coffer corpse is hit for 6 or more points in a single round of 
combat by a normal weapon (to which they are immune) they 
will fall down, only to rise up the next round. All who witness 
this horror must save vs fear. Those who fail the saving throw 
will panic and run away for 2 rounds, with a 60% chance (re -
duced by 5% per level) to drop all items they are holding.
Special Attacks
Strangle. A coffer corpse has a 75% chance to attack with its bare 
hands to strangle rather than attacking with a weapon. If they 
hit, they have grabbed their target by the throat, causing 1d6 
damage. Each successive round thereafter they cause an addi-
tional 1d6 damage as they are strangling their victim, with no 
additional roll needed. They will continue choking until they 
are destroyed or their victim is dead.
Unarmed Combat. Coffer corpses cannot be overborne or grap-
pled (it is theoretically possible, but they are immune to tem -
porary damage), and do not employ these tactics. 
COUATL
FREQUENCY Very Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful good
MOVE 60ft; 180ft flying (AA:VI)
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 9
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d3+ poison) and 1 constrict 
(2d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect invisible 15%
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Genius (17-18)
MORALE 90
LOOT Hoard 2 and 9
EXPERIENCE 2,000 +12/hp
Couatl are a species of brightly feathered serpents with wings. 
They are extremely intelligent spellcasters. Couatl are rarely en-
countered, usually in remote tropical environments. They are 
often treated as gods by lesser beings nearby.
Special Features
Spellcasting. Roll 1d100 to determine the couatl’s spell-casting 
type. 01-45: 5th level magic-user, 46-80: 7th level cleric, 81-00: 
5th level magic-user and 7th level cleric.
Ethereality. Couatl can shift into the ethereal plane once per day. 
In this form they become invisible even while attacking, and 
can only be damaged by magic weapons (and spells). Howev -
er, they cannot make physical attacks while in ethereal form. 
This is usually used to escape through walls rather than as a 
combat tactic.
Special Attacks
Poison Bite. If the couatl’s bite hits, the victim must save vs poi-
son or die.
Constrict. A couatl can also constrict with its body. A successful 
attack means the couatl has grabbed its victim, causing 2d4 
damage, with an additional 2d4 damage each round until one 
or the other is dead.
Unarmed Combat.  Couatls cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics. 

===== PAGE 117 =====
116 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
CRABMAN
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d6
SIZE Large (9ft)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 90ft; 60ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 2 pincers (1d4)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Low to average (5-10)
MORALE 60
LOOT Individual 2
EXPERIENCE 40 +2/hp
Crabmen are amphibious humanoids. Each has a tough red -
dish-brown exoskeleton and pincers in place of hands. They 
have smaller, rudimentary “arms” that can work together to 
manipulate very light tools.
Crabmen lair in caves along the seashore and are general -
ly peaceful creatures, though they are often forced to defend 
themselves from raids by sahuagin and other marine predators.
These creatures prize silver very highly and will attack, on sight, 
anyone openly carrying silver objects or items which appear to 
be made of silver. Another oddity of the species is that they oc-
casionally undergo some sort of group frenzy, forming a band 
of 1d10+30 individuals to march inland, raiding any settlements 
they find. 
Unarmed Combat. Crabmen can be overborne or grappled, and 
they may overbear. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-10. Overbear Re-
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
na/-2.
Language
Crabmen speak their own language, which appears to be an an-
cient offshoot of the Neutral alignment tongue.
CROCODILE
NORMAL GIANT
FREQUENCY Common Rare
SIZE Large Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral

1 bite (2d4 piercing) 
and 1 tail (1d12 
blunt)
1 bite (3d6 piercing) 
and 1 tail (2d10

MORALE 60 80

Crocodiles may be found in shallow fresh or salt water. They lie 
in ambush along river banks, leaping up from concealment to 
eat just about anything that comes in reach. Cold is their ene -
my, cutting their movement speed in half.
Giant Crocodiles are normally restricted to prehistoric or salt water 
environments.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Crocodiles of both varieties surprise opponents on a 
roll of 1–3 on a d6.
Unarmed Combat.  Crocodiles of either type cannot be over -
borne or grappled, and do not employ these tactics.

Unarmed Combat.  Giant crustaceans cannot be overborne or 
grappled, and do not employ these tactics. 
GIANT CRAB
These creatures are only found near water (fresh or salt). They 
will typically rush their prey from a place of concealment, having 
“scouted” the area with their eyes (which are on stalks).
Surprise and Initiative
Giant crabs achieve surprise on 1–4 of 1d6.
Immunities and Resistances
Many-legged. This creature cannot be knocked prone.
GIANT CRAYFISH
Giant crayfish are only encountered near fresh water. They will 
typically rush their prey from a place of concealment, but they 
do not have the advantage of the giant crab’s eyestalks.
Surprise and Initiative
Giant crayfish achieve surprise on 1–3 of 1d6.
Immunities and Resistances
Many-legged. This creature cannot be knocked prone.
CRYPT THING
FREQUENCY Very Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 6
MELEE ATTACKS 1 clawed hand (1d8 slashing)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 100%
INTELLIGENCE Very
MORALE 75
LOOT Cache 12
EXPERIENCE 160 +4/hp
A crypt thing appears as a cloaked skeleton, but contrary to ap-
pearances, is not undead and cannot be turned. They reside 
in their lairs and never venture forth. A crypt thing will never 
attack first, they will always let the party attack before they do. 
If they are not molested they will leave the adventurers alone. 
If they are attacked then they will respond by activating their 
teleport ability. 
A crypt thing can also defend itself with its clawlike hands. 
Immunities and Resistances
Cannot be Turned (Not Undead).  Despite appearances, crypt 
things are not undead and thus cannot be turned.
Immune to Mundane Weapons.  The crypt thing is immune to 
piercing, blunt, and slashing damage, unless the weapon caus-
ing that damage is magical.
Special Features
Teleport Enemies.  Anyone attempting to attack a crypt thing 
must (for the first attack only) make a saving throw vs spells or 
instantly be teleported in a random direction. Each individual 
who fails must roll d100. Those who roll 01–20 are teleported 
from 100 to 1,000ft north. Those who roll 21–40 are teleported 
100–1,000ft south. Those who roll 41–60 are teleported 100–
1,000ft east. Those who roll 61–80 are teleported 100–1,000ft 
west. Those who roll 81–90 are teleported one dungeon level 
up. Those who roll 91–00 are teleported one dungeon level 
down.
Those who make the saving throw can continue with their at -
tack. 
This is an advanced form of teleportation. Individuals will arrive 
safely, not in solid areas like floors or walls.
Unarmed Combat. Crypt things can be overborne or grappled, 
although they do not use these tactics themselves. If not fend-
ed off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: 
ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF na/-1. Grap -
pling Result Mods: ATK/DEF na/+0.
===== PAGE 119 =====
118 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Language
Crypt things speak common. If questioned about the disappear-
ance of any comrades, they will say they were disintegrated.
CYCLOPS
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Large (20ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 13
MELEE ATTACKS 1 club (6d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect invisible 10%
LAIR CHANCE 80%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 110
LOOT Hoard 5, Cache 5
EXPERIENCE 3,300 +17/hp
Cyclopes are extremely tall, ugly humanoids, each with a single 
eye centred under its slightly drooping brow. These oafish, an -
tisocial creatures prefer to inhabit lonesome environs, such as 
out–of–the–way, otherwise deserted islands.
In addition to their single club attack, cyclopes may attack by 
hurling large boulders up to a distance of 60ft.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack. A cyclops can throw rocks like other giants, for 
4d10 blunt damage, with a range increment of 20ft (-2 at 40ft, 
-4 at 60ft).
Unarmed Combat. A cyclops can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ 
on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +4/-4. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.

DAKON
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 6d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful neutral
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 1+1
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d10 slashing)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 50%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 55
LOOT Hoard 5
EXPERIENCE 25 +2/hp
Dakons are intelligent apes resembling gorillas. They are peace-
ful, fighting only in defence or to regain treasure stolen from 
them. These apes are likely to be settle anywhere, but avoid 
large expanses of water. They are on good terms with lawful 
humans and demi-humans and are able to speak the common 
tongue, but they have a deep mistrust of humanoids and shun 
them.

===== PAGE 120 =====
