Special Attacks
To-Hit Bonus: Dakons attack with a +2 to-hit bonus due to their 
incredible strength and the knife-edged sharpness of their 
claws.
Unarmed Combat. Dakons can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
Language
Dakons speak the common tongue.
DARK CREEPER
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1 (or 20d4)
SIZE Small (4ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic neutral
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20] (or 8 [12]; see below
HIT DICE 1+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 dagger (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 120ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 55
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE 50 +2/hp
Dark creepers are short, spindly humanoids with pale skin who 
like to wrap themselves in loose, dark clothing, leaving little ex -
posed save their eyes and hands. These beings are in some way 
a manifestation of the eldritch powers of darkness, although 
they are not individually powerful. Dark creepers detest sun -
light and avoid any light as best they can, dwelling alone in dun-
geons or profane villages even deeper underground. These vil -
lages are always governed by a dark stalker.
Solitary dark creepers tend to carry their treasure with them, 
concealed within the many loose folds of their dark clothing. 
There is a 20% chance a dark creeper will carry a magic dagger, 
15% chance of a magic ring, and 10% chance of either 1d4 gems 
of random value or 1d3 items of random jewellery. Lair trea -
sure probabilities for a lone dark creeper are 50% for a mag -
ic dagger, 10% for a magic ring, 30% for gems or jewellery. To 
generate treasure for a dark creeper village, multiply individual 
treasure chances by the number of creepers, adding 5d20 pp 
and 5d12 gp.
Special Features
Thief Abilities. Dark creepers have all the skills of a 4th level thief.
Spell-Like Abilities.
3/day: Detect magic and create darkness. 
In both cases, duration is 1 hour, and the effect is in a 50ft radius 
around the target point. Any magical sources of light have a 20% 
chance to avoid the effects of darkness upon that item only. If 
an item is affected, it will resume normal function after 1 hour.
Death Fire. When a creeper dies, its body magically immolates 
in a fiercely hot blaze of disgusting purple and green flames. 
Metal items, including magical items of metal, carried as part 
of the creeper’s treasure hoard have an 80% of surviving this 
magical fire undamaged. Any magic item damaged by the 
creeper’s death fire will lose its enchantment.
Deadly in Darkness:  In darkness, the dark creeper’s armour 
class is 0 [20]. In light, the armour class is 8 [12].
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack. Dark creepers can attack with ranged weapons 
as humans can.
Unarmed Combat. Dark creepers do not make grappling or over-
bearing attacks, but can be attacked in this way. If not fended 
off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: 
ATK/DEF +6/-6. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2. Grap -
pling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.
DARK STALKER
FREQUENCY Very Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1 (see below)
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic neutral
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20] (or 8 [12]; see below
HIT DICE 2+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 short sword (1d6)
SENSES Infravision 120ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 60
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE 200 +3/hp
Dark stalkers are the leaders of the dark creepers, though it 
is not known whether they are dark creepers altered through 
some means either genetic or magical, or if they are a breed 
apart. What is known for certain is the dark stalker is much tall-
er than its dark creeper followers and one will be found as the 
leader of any dark creeper village. In an area settled by at least 
25 solitary dark creepers there is a 90% chance there will be a 
dark stalker in the area, increased by 2% for every 5 additional 
creepers. A dark stalker is rarely encountered away from their 
clan, but if such an encounter occurs the stalker is probably on 
===== PAGE 121 =====
120 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
some mysterious personal mission and will not engage in com-
bat unless forced to do so.
Dark stalkers carry their treasure in their robes like dark creep-
ers: 30% chance of magic short sword, 10% of magical rings 
(worn if possible), 10% chance of either 2d4 gems or 1d2 items 
of jewellery to be determined randomly by GM.
Special Features
Thief Abilities. Dark stalkers have all the skills of a 4th level thief.
Spell-Like Abilities.
3/day: Detect magic and create darkness. 
In both cases, duration is 1 hour, and the effect is in a 50ft 
radius around the target point. Any magical sources of light 
have a 20% chance to avoid the effects of darkness upon 
that item only. If an item is affected, it will resume normal 
function after 1 hour.
2/day: wall of fog
Death Fire. When a dark stalker dies, it explodes in a sickly blue 
3d8 fireball blast with a radius of 10ft. Magic items carried by 
the stalker have a 75% chance of surviving the blast in usable 
condition.
Deadly in Darkness: In darkness, the dark stalker’s armour class 
is 0 [20]. In light, the armour class is 8 [12].
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack. Dark stalkers can attack with ranged weapons as 
humans can.
Unarmed Combat.  Dark stalkers can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-6. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
DEMONS
Demonologists have identified several classes of demon. Each 
class of demon possesses a variety of powerful, supernatural 
abilities. Those abilities common to all demons are noted be -
low. For further information on both the classified and unclassi-
fied demons, see the individual demon listings. Note that most 
demons do not actually fall into the six listed categories.
Many demons possess the following magical abilities: infravision 
(as the 3rd level magic-user spell), teleport (with no chance of 
error) (as the 5th level magic-user spell), darkness and gate (as 
the 9th level magic-user spell). See individual listings for details.
Demons can freely travel between their own home planes and 
Tartarus, Pandemonium and Hades. They may also travel the 
Astral Plane at will. However, they are only able to enter the 
Prime Material Plane if summoned with a spell ( conjuration, 
gate, wish, etc.) or certain magical items.
Demons are all extremely and decidedly chaotic evil (see “Align-
ment”). Demonic society is highly competitive and always fa -
vours the strong over the weak.
In combat, demons are fearless and the less intelligent among 
them will attack mindlessly until slain.
They are immune to subdual attacks and may simultaneously 
attack as many opponents as their abilities allow.
Being supremely chaotic, demons will never willingly serve an -
other. If compelled into service through magical or other means, 
they will always seek a way to either kill or enslave their master, 
or at the very least, subvert the terms of their summoning. While 
demons may be summoned by higher-level PCs, they will prove 
highly difficult to control. Thaumaturgic circles are proof against 
demons, but require increasingly complex preparation and ma-
terials for the more powerful sorts. Often summoning will entail 
either a threat of punishment or a reward. These should be con-
sidered carefully by the GM during play and should always carry 
an element of risk for the summoner.
Demons are repulsed by good artifacts or other powerful holy 
items.
On the Prime Material Plane, demons may be turned by clerics 
of levels 8+ and paladins of levels 11+.

===== PAGE 122 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 121
Summary of Resistances:
ATTACK FORM DAMAGE ROLLED
Acid Full damage
Cold Half damage
Electricity (lightning bolt) Half damage
Fire (dragon breath, fireball) Half damage
Gas, poisonous (cloudkill) Half damage
Iron Weapons Full damage
CLASS A DEMON (VROCK)
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3 or 1d6 (in the Abyss)
SIZE Large (9ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft; 180ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 8
MELEE ATTACKS 2 talons (1d4), 2 claws (1d8) 
and 1 bite (1d6)
SENSES Infravision 120ft
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 85
LOOT Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 1,275 +10/hp
Vrock, considered one of the weakest of demonkind, look like a 
hideous cross between a vulture and a humanoid. Unlike stron-
ger demons, they are vulnerable to non-magical weapons.
Vrock are particularly stupid, and cannot normally be bargained 
with. They love the sight of precious gems and jewellery, howev-
er, and also enjoy feasting on the flesh of humans.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-
son (including gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: Darkness, 5ft radius, detect invisibility (objects only, oth-
erwise as per the 2nd level magic-user spell), gate in another 
vrock (10% chance of success), or telekinesis (as the 5th level 
magic-user spell, up to 200 lbs).
Unarmed Combat. Vrock demons can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +3/-3. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+3/-3.
CLASS B DEMON (HEZROU)
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3 or 1d6 (in the Abyss)
SIZE Large (8ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 60ft; 120ft hopping
ARMOUR CLASS -2 [22]
HIT DICE 9
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d3) and 1 bite (4d4)
SENSES Infravision 120ft
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 90
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 2,000 +12/hp
Slightly shorter than the vrock, the hezrou resemble loathsome 
toads with humanoid arms. They are vulnerable to normal me -
lee and missile weapons.
Hezrou and vrock will gladly fight each other without hesitation, 
and they both share a love of human flesh.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (55%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 55% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-
son (including gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: Darkness, 15ft radius, cause fear (as the 4th level mag-
ic-user spell), levitate (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), detect 
invisibility (objects only, otherwise as per the 2nd level mag -
ic-user spell), telekinesis (as per the 5th level magic-user spell, 
up to 300 lbs), or gate (as the 9th level magic-user spell) an -
other hezrou (20% chance of success).
Unarmed Combat. Hezrou-type demons can make grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+2/-2.

CLASS D DEMON (NALFESHNEE AND OTHERS)
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3 or 1d6 (in the Abyss)
SIZE Large (11ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft; 120ft flying (AA:II)
ARMOUR CLASS -1 [21]
HIT DICE 11
MELEE ATTACKS 1 claw (1d4) and 1 bite (2d4)
SENSES Infravision 120ft, Detect invisible 15%
LAIR CHANCE 15%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 100
LOOT Hoard 5
EXPERIENCE 3,000 +16/hp
Particularly malevolent demons, the class D have the upper 
body of an ape and the cloven-hoofed lower body (and head) of 
a boar. They have rather small, feathered wings as well, which 
seem undersized compared to their corpulent bodies. 
Certain class D demons have their own secret names, which 
makes it 90% certain they will answer a summons if it is spoken. 
Conjurers should be prepared to make impressive promises of 
treasure, magical items or living sacrifices when summoning a 
class D demon, especially by name. It should also be remem -
bered that these demons particularly enjoy feasting on human 
blood and meat.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (65%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 65% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-
son (including gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: Darkness, 10ft radius, improved phantasmal force  (as 
the 2nd level illusionist spell), fear (as the 4th level magic-user 
spell), levitate (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), detect magic 
(as the 1st level magic-user spell), comprehend languages (as 
the 1st level magic-user spell), dispel magic (as the 3rd level 
magic-user spell), polymorph self (as the 4th level magic-user 
spell), telekinesis (as the 5th level magic-user spell, up to 500 
lbs), project image  (as the 6th level magic-user spell), use a 
===== PAGE 124 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 123
symbol of fear  or discord (as the 8th level magic-user spell), 
and gate (as the 9th level magic-user spell) another class A to 
D demon (random class, 60% chance of success).

Strength Bonus. These demons have a +2 bonus on to-hit rolls.
Unarmed Combat. Nalfeshnee demons can make grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-6. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +5/-5. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+2/-2.
CLASS E DEMON (MARILITH AND OTHERS)
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3 or 1d6 (in the Abyss)
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS -7 [27]
HIT DICE 7+7
MELEE ATTACKS 6 arms (2d4) and 1 constriction (1d6)

INTELLIGENCE High (13-14)
MORALE 90
LOOT Hoard 7

Infamous even among demonkind for their cruel and ill-tem -
pered nature, the marilith are invariably female. From the waist 
up they appear to be a human female with six arms and skin 
tones ranging from deep violet to a putrescent green. Below 
the waist however, they have the coiling body of a large serpent. 
In melee they prefer to wield a variety of barbed and hooked 
swords and battle axes or simply constrict their prey with their 
powerful serpentine tail.
All class E demons have personal and secret names which may 
be used to summon and bargain with them, much like class D 
demons. 
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (80%).  Any magic spell or effect has an 80% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.

ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-

morph self  (as the 4th level magic-user spell), project image  
(as the 6th level magic-user spell), or gate (as the 9th level 
magic-user spell) another demon (50% chance of success).

is summoned:

CLASS F DEMON (BALOR AND OTHERS)
FREQUENCY Rare

Reportedly only six of this class of demon exist, each with its 
own secret name. In combat they wield massive +1 swords and 
a cat-o-nine-tails whip which they employ to drag their victims 
into the flames that they continually immolate themselves in.

Summoning class F demons requires significant offerings or fu-
ture promises of service or sacrifice. If negotiation is successful, 
they might be persuaded to aid the summoner or associated 
group for a while. In any case, these demons will always try to 
bully and intimidate their masters in an effort to usurp leader -
ship, which they crave. Many other chaotic evil monsters and 
demons are attracted to the aura of charismatic evil that sur -
rounds class F demons, so they are often found in the forefront 
of evil hordes.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (75%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 75% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-
son (including gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: Darkness, 10ft radius, fear (as the 4th level magic-us -
er spell), detect magic (as the 1st level magic-user spell), read 
magic (as the 1st level magic-user spell), comprehend languag-
es (as the 1st level magic-user spell), detect invisibility (objects 
only, otherwise as the 2nd level magic-user spell), pyrotech-
nics (as the 2nd level magic-user spell ), dispel magic (as the 
3rd level magic-user spell), suggestion (as the 3rd level mag -
ic-user spell), telekinesis (as the 5th level magic-user spell, up 
to 600 lbs), use a symbol of fear, discord, sleep, or stunning (as 
the 8th level magic-user spell), and gate (as the 9th level mag-
ic-user spell) another demon of class C (80% chance) or class 
D (20% chance) with a 70% chance of success.
Special Attacks
Whip. Each round, there is a 4 in 6 chance the Balor will use 
its fiery whip. Victims who are hit and fail a saving throw vs 
spells are burnt by the flames and suffer 4d6 additional points 
of damage.
Unarmed Combat.  Balor demons can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +3/-3. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+2/-2.
ALUU-DEMON (HALF-DEMON)
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil (See below)
MOVE 120ft; 120ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 6+1d6  hit points
MELEE ATTACKS 1 weapon (weapon + strength bonus) 
or draining touch
SENSES Infravision 120ft
LAIR CHANCE 15%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12) to genius (17-18)
MORALE 75
LOOT Cache 5, 6, 7
EXPERIENCE 4,050 +14/hp
Aluu-demons are one of several types of “tieflings,” a common 
term for those with fiends of the lower planes in their ances -
try. The two tiefling monster types represented in this book 
(aluu-demons and caumbions) are too powerful to be used as a 
character ancestry.
Most aluu-demons are the result of union between a succubus 
or incubus with a human. Their individual appearances vary, 
but their vestigial horns and small leathery bat-like wings de -
note their demonic heritage. Most favour their demonic parent 
and revel in chaotic evil. A minority (20%) harbour less demonia-
cal inclinations. Nevertheless, they may never be lawful or good.
Aluu-demons have a natural base armour class of 5 [15]. Wear-
ing armour gives them no additional benefit, although rings, 
magic items and spells can improve their AC. 
Fully 25% of all aluu-demons have genius level intelligence (17-
18). These aluu-demons are able to memorise and cast spells 
as a 1st to 12th level magic-user (1d12 to determine level). Spell 
casting abilities are in addition to their other innate abilities not-
ed above, and the aluu-demon has none of the armour/weapon 
restrictions of the magic-user class.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (30%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 30% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Non-Iron Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune 
to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons that are 
not made of iron, unless the weapon causing that damage has 
a magical bonus of +1 or more. Most metal weapons are made 
from iron (steel).
===== PAGE 126 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 125
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
1/day: dimension door (as the 4th level magic-user spell)
3/day (as Level 12 Caster): charm person (as the 1st level mag-
ic-user spell), ESP (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), poly-
morph self (humanoid shapes only, otherwise as the 4th level 
magic-user spell), and suggestion (as the 3rd level magic-user 
spell).
Special Attacks
Energy Drain.  Much like succubi/incubi, aluu-demons are able 
to drain a victim’s life energy with a touch (a “to hit” roll is re-
quired in combat). Each touch drains 1d8 hit points from the 
victim and adds 1d4 hit points to the aluu-demon.
Unarmed Combat.  Aluu-demons can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks, but usually don’t. If not fended off, they score 
an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-
8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
BABAU
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3 or 1d6 (in the Abyss)
SIZE Medium (7ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS -3 [23]
HIT DICE 7+14
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d4+1) and 1 bite (2d4) or 1 
weapon (+7 damage)
SENSES Infravision 120ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Very
MORALE 95
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 2,000 +12/hp
Babau are also known by demonologists as horned demons 
or bone demons. They are around 7ft tall and look like skele -
tons covered in a leathery black skin. The babau’s feet, taloned 
hands and head are grossly oversized, nearly the same as a hill 
giant’s. From the base of its skull protrudes a hooked horn.
These demons are known for their cunning intelligence and 
strength (19). In combat, they prefer to use any sort of weapon-
ry if it is to their advantage. 
Babau are despised by class A, B and C demons. Class D de -
mons especially hate bands of babau, and hunt them for food.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. The babau is immune to piercing, 
blunt, and slashing damage, unless the weapon causing that 
damage is magical.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-
son (including gases).
Ichor Reduces Weapon Damage by Half.  In the heat of battle, ba -
bau demons secrete a slimy rust coloured ichor that reduces 
damage from melee weapons, as blows slide off their bodies. 
Any damage from a melee weapon inflicts only half the dam -
age indicated.
Vulnerability to Iron.  Iron weapons inflict +2 additional damage 
on babau demons (although the weapon must be magical to 
hit).
Special Features
Thief Abilities.  Babau have the thieving abilities of a 9th level 
Thief.
Spell-Like Abilities
At Will: Darkness, 5ft radius
1/day (as 14th level caster):  fear (touch only, otherwise as 
the 4th level magic-user spell), levitate (as the 2nd level mag-
ic-user spell), fly (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), dispel 
magic (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), polymorph self (as 
the 4th level magic-user spell), heat metal (as the 2nd level 
druid spell), and gate (as the 9th level magic-user spell) in 
another babau (25% chance of success).
Enfeebling Gaze.  Anyone up to 20ft away gazing into the eyes 
of a babau must save vs spells or suffer the effects of a ray of 
enfeeblement (as the 2nd level magic-user spell). This applies to 
anyone who is generally in front of the demon and looking at 
it without using an indirect method of sight such as a mirror. 
Attacking the creature by looking at a reflection incurs a to-hit 
penalty of -2. Attacking blindly incurs a to-hit penalty of -4.
Unarmed Combat. Babau demons can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+1/-1.

CAUMBION
MINOR NOBLE
FREQUENCY Very rare Very rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1 1
SIZE Medium (6-7ft) Medium (7-8ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil Chaotic evil
MOVE 150ft 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] (base) 2 [18] (base)
HIT DICE 5 to 8 (1d4+4) 9 to 16 (1d8+8)
MELEE ATTACKS 2 (By weapon type) 2 (By weapon type)
SENSES Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 10% 50%
INTELLIGENCE Average to 
exceptional (8-16) Genius (17-18)
MORALE 70 to 85 90 to 125
LOOT Cache 5, 6, 7 Cache 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
EXPERIENCE 1,275 +10/hp 4,410 +15/hp
Caumbions are the offspring of a major demon and a human 
(cf. aluu-demon). As such, each is slightly different depending 
upon parentage. Generally, caumbions are strong and heavily–
built. They will usually have several other demonic characteris -
tics as well, such as vestigial horns, barbs, or scaled skin.
Caumbions may become clerics, attaining a maximum level 
equal to their hit dice, magic-users (maximum of 5th level), or 
thieves or assassins (up to a maximum of their hit dice in ability 
level). The caumbion has the abilities of the class, none of the 
armour/weapon restrictions, but does not gain extra hit points 
from the class. If the caumbion has the abilities of a character 
class, add an extra 15 points of XP per hit point to the monster’s 
experience point value.
MINOR CAUMBIONS
Minor Caumbions have a natural base armour class of 6 [14]. 
Wearing mundane armours cannot improve this. Magical ar -
mours or other protections add only their bonus, unless the 
armour is superior to AC 6 [14], in which case the full benefit is 
accrued (the caumbion’s base AC is ignored, and the AC of the 
armour is used instead).
The GM may choose to add class abilities or special abilities to a 
minor caumbion to generate an interesting foe if the caumbion 
is the main villain and has minions; caumbions are highly indi -
vidual. In higher level adventures where the caumbion is just 
one of many enemies, this usually isn’t worth the extra com -
plexity.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance:  Minor caumbions have 5%–20% magic re -
sistance depending on their hit dice. 5HD=5%, 6HD=10%, 
7HD=15%, and 8HD=20%.
Unarmed Combat.  Minor Caumbions can make grappling or 
overbearing attacks, but only overbearing is commonly used 
against sole opponents. If not fended off, they score an un -
armed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Over -
bear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/-0.
NOBLE CAUMBIONS
Noble Caumbions are the children of very powerful demons, 
such as demon princes and the like (“nobility” refers to their 
power, for their behaviour is usually anything but noble). They 
are likely to be the main villain in an adventure, so the GM will 
probably choose to add abilities from one of the four possible 
classes (see above).
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance: Noble caumbions have 25%–40% magic resis -
tance depending on their hit dice. 9-10HD=25%, 11-12HD=30%, 
13-14HD=35%, 15-16HD=40%.
Unarmed Combat.  Noble Caumbions can make grappling or 
overbearing attacks, but only overbearing is commonly used 
against sole opponents. If not fended off, they score an un -
armed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Over -
bear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2. Grappling Result Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/-0.
DRETCH
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d4 or 5d4 (in the Abyss)
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d4) and 1 bite(1d4+1)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 15%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 65
LOOT Individual 1, 2, 3, 4
EXPERIENCE 175 +4/hp
Dretch are the weakest of native demonkind (manes, as con -
demned souls, are weaker). Their appearance is almost comical: 
a plump body with thin, gangly arms and legs. Their squat heads 
are bald and they have a slobbering, stupid visage. Dretch usu -
ally attack in hordes, using tooth and claw in blind abandon, 
although if they consider themselves threatened they will first 
try to open a gate for reinforcements. 
===== PAGE 128 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 127
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (30%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 30% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-
son (including gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: Darkness, 5ft radius, scare (as the 2nd level magic-user 
spell), telekinesis (as the 5th level magic-user spell, up to 50 
lbs) or attempt to gate (as the 9th level magic-user spell) a 
class A demon (15% chance of success).
1/day: stinking cloud  (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), and 
teleport without fail (as the 5th level magic-user spell, but with 
no chance of error). 
Unarmed Combat. Dretch demons can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks, but ordinarily don’t. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+6/-6. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF -1/+1. Grappling Result

EKIVU (FLY DEMON) (KHASME)
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3 or 1d6 (in the Abyss)
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 50ft; 210ft flying (AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS -1 [21]
HIT DICE 7+2
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (2d4) and 1 bite (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 85
LOOT Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 1,275 +10/hp
Ekivu resemble a hideous crossbreed between a giant fly and 
a human. Their hind legs are insect-like and their fore-limbs 
resemble human arms, but with bony, sharp claws. Like flies, 
their bodies are covered in blueish-black chitin with bristling 
hair. Their heads are vaguely human, but with bulbous, faceted 
eyes like those of a fly. Their mouths are ringed with sharp teeth 
and their long noses are actually a sharp proboscis for drawing 
blood from their victims.
Ekivu have a long standing hatred of most other classes of de -
mons, especially classes A and B. Ekivu have managed to en -
slave a number of shub, which they use for their own nefarious 
ends.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (40%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 40% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Poison Immunity. These demons are immune to poison and poi-
son damage (including gases).
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: Darkness, 5ft radius, detect good (as the 1st level cler -
ic spell), detect invisibility (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), 
telekinesis (as the 5th level magic-user spell, up to 150 lbs), 
fear (touch only, otherwise as the 4th level magic-user spell) 
or gate (as the 9th level magic-user spell) another ekivu (15% 
chance of success).
Special Attacks
Drone. When not using one of their other special abilities, ek -
ivu constantly make an insidious buzzing drone which has 
the power to lull listeners into a comatose state. The ekivu 
can drone while engaged in melee combat. If a save vs spells 
(unlisted categories) is not made, the victim will sleep for 2d4 
hours or until the demon has drawn 1d4 hp of their blood.
Unarmed Combat. Ekivu demons cannot be overborne or grap -
pled, and do not employ these tactics. 
MANIS
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 4d4
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Chaotic Evil
MOVE 30ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d2 slashing) and 1 bite (1d4 
piercing)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 50%
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 18 +1/hp
A manis (plural: manes) is the form of a soul condemned to the 
abyss. They are considered demons, but do not have all the 
powers associated with demonkind. They are rare in the ma -
terial planes. Manes will attack anything that is not a demon. 
The form of a manis is bipedal and humanoid, but manes have 
vacant eyes, sharp teeth and claws, and tails.
===== PAGE 129 =====
128 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Mundane Weapons.  A manis is immune to piercing, 
blunt, and slashing damage, unless the weapon causing that 
damage is magical.
Immunities. This creature is immune to mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells, sleep, gases, poison, disease, cold dam-
age, temporary damage, and subdual damage.
Cannot Be Killed. A manis that is killed re-forms after one day. 
They are only truly destroyed when a demon lord chooses to 
feed on them.
Unarmed Combat.  Manes can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks, but ordinarily don’t. If not fended off, they score an 
unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2. Over-
bear Results Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2. Grappling Result Mods: 
ATK/DEF -2/+2.
QUASIT
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Tiny
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d2) and 1 bite (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 60
LOOT Cache 3 (x3)
EXPERIENCE 325 +3/hp
Quasits are larval fiends that have been shaped by demon lords 
for service as familiars to chaotic evil magic-users and occasion-
ally as servants to other powerful chaotic evil individuals. 
When serving as a familiar, quasits endow the magic-user with 
several abilities. If the magic-user is within a mile of their qu -
asit familiar, a constant full-sensory telepathic communication 
operates through the quasit. The master also gains 1 level of 
ability as a magic-user within this radius. If the quasit is within 
25ft of its master, it imparts its 25% magic resistance and also 
its regeneration abilities. If either master or quasit moves out of 
range, the special abilities are lost. In addition to these abilities, 
once per week a quasit is able to contact the Abyss in order to 
help gain some necessary advice or information. This operates 
as the 5th level cleric spell commune, and up to 6 questions are 
permitted.
Despite their rather low intelligence, quasits are quite crafty 
and cunning creatures. They are also used as tools by demon 
lords, who might communicate through them. Quasits are gen-
erally tasked with helping their masters to spread evil and cha-
os. In particular, they are often used to pervert lawful evil souls 
to chaos, which earns them rewards on their home plane of the 
Abyss. Ultimately, when its master dies, the quasit is charged 
with bringing the soul back to the Abyss. If the quasit has served 
well, it may be rewarded by being changed into a class A or class 
B demon. If not, they will be punished through becoming a soul 
worm, or even a quasit again.
If the quasit dies, the master immediately loses 4 levels (dura -
tion 1 month).
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (25%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 25% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Immunities. Quasits are immune to cold, fire, and electrical dam-
age.
Saving Throw (Spells). Quasits make saving throws against spells 
with a target number of 13.
Special Features
Regeneration. Quasits regenerate damage at a rate of 1 hp per 
round.
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: detect good (as the 1st level cleric spell), detect magic (as 
the 1st level cleric spell), invisibility (as the 2nd level magic-us-
er spell), polymorph self (each quasit has 2 forms into which it 
can polymorph, chosen by the GM from frog, bat, giant cen -
tipede, or wolf.
1/day: cause fear (as the 4th level magic-user spell) in a 30ft 
radius.
Special Attacks
In its natural form, a quasit attacks with its teeth and claws, 
which inflict a burning irritation that subtracts 1 from the vic -
tim’s dexterity unless a save vs poison is made. This penalty is 
cumulative. The irritation remains for 2d6 melee rounds.
Unarmed Combat.  Quasits can make grappling or overbear -
ing attacks, but ordinarily don’t. If not fended off, they score 
an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-
10. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF -4/+4. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF -4/+4.

SHUB (RUTTERKITH)
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4 or 3d4 (in the Abyss)
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 1 [19]
HIT DICE 5+1
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d6+1) or 1 weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 70
LOOT Individual 3 and 4 each; Cache 1 and 
3 in lair
EXPERIENCE 425 +6/hp
Shub resemble humanoids, but with hunched backs, twisted 
arms and legs, and a strange, loping gait. Their pointed skulls 
are bald and misshapen.
While physically among the weakest of demonkind, the shub 
are a vile, vengeful sort. They are the continual target of abuse 
by other classes of demon, and the shub are quick to respond 
in kind if given the opportunity. Shub particularly enjoy bullying 
the dretch, whom they despise as lumbering and stupid.
In combat, shub prefer to wield a variety of strange, wick -
ed-looking pole arms, saw-toothed swords, and bizarre atlatls 
and throwing knives.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (40%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 40% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-
son (including gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: darkness (5ft radius), fear (touch only, otherwise as the 
4th level magic-user spell), fly (as the 3rd level magic-user 
spell), telekinesis (as the 5th level magic-user spell, up to 100 
lbs), attempt to gate in an ekivu (15% chance of success).
1/day: teleport (without fail, otherwise as the 5th level mag -
ic-user spell).
Unarmed Combat.  Shub demons can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks, but ordinarily don’t. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
SUCCUBUS/INCUBUS
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft; 180ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 6
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d3)
SENSES Infravision 120ft
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 75
LOOT Hoard 9, Cache 3
EXPERIENCE 2,100 +6/hp
These solitary demons are called a succubus (if female) and an 
incubus (if male). In their true forms they resemble stunning -
ly attractive human beings, with leathery bat wings sprouting 
from their backs. 
If they take a liking to a particular victim, a succubus/incubus 
may mate with a human, producing an aluu-demon (see listing 
for further details).
In the Abyss, the succubi and incubi reign over lesser demons 
through intimidation and trickery. They also prize treasure, es -
pecially gems and jewellery.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Magic Resistance (70%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 70% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-
son (including gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: darkness (5ft radius), become ethereal (as per the 
magic item oil of etherealness ), charm person (as the 1st lev -
el magic-user spell), ESP (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), 
clairaudience (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), suggestion 
(as the 3rd level magic-user spell), polymorph self (humanoid 
shapes only, otherwise as the 4th level magic-user spell), or 
gate (as the 9th level magic-user spell) a class D demon (70%) 
or a class E demon (30%) with a 40% chance of success.
1/day: teleport (without fail, otherwise as the 5th level mag -
ic-user spell).
===== PAGE 131 =====
130 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Special Attacks
Energy-Draining Kiss.  A succubus or incubus feeds by draining 
energy from charmed or unconscious victims by a kiss. Each 
kiss, to a maximum of one per day, drains one level from the 
victim. Captives of a succubus or incubus do not survive for 
long.
Unarmed Combat.  Succubi and incubi can make grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and often do (their AC makes it difficult 
to fend them off). If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
UDUK (BAR-IGURA)
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3 or 1d6 (in the Abyss)
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft; 150ft in trees
ARMOUR CLASS -3 [23]
HIT DICE 6+6
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d6) and 1 bite (2d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 80
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 1,725 +10/hp
The uduk, or the “jumping demon”, looks much like an orang–
utan with a horribly twisted face. Its hands and feet are taloned 
and have 6 digits each. The uduk can move on foot along the 
ground, but they excel at swinging through areas of dense foli -
age. They can also jump up to 40 feet in a single bound.
They have a natural ability to change their colouration to match 
their surroundings, which takes one melee round. 
Uduk do not like the company of other demons, and generally 
are found only with others of their ilk. If other kinds of demons 
are present, uduk will bully weaker demons in order to under -
mine their superiors.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (45%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 45% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Damage Resistances (all half damage): Cold, Electricity, Fire, Poi-
son (including gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: darkness (10ft radius), fear (as the 4th level magic-user 
spell), create water (as the 1st level cleric spell), detect illusion 
(as the 1st level Illusionist spell), detect invisibility (objects only, 
otherwise as the 2nd level magic-user spell), dispel magic (as 
the 3rd level magic-user spell), entangle (as the 1st level druid 
spell), plant growth (as the 3rd level druid spell), telekinesis (as 
the 5th level magic-user spell, up to 250 lbs), or gate in anoth-
er uduk (25% chance of success). 
2/day: change self (as the 1st level illusionist spell), invisibility 
(as the 2nd level magic-user spell), and spectral force (as the 
3rd level Illusionist spell).
Unarmed Combat.  Bar-Igura demons can make grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-6. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+0/+0.
DEVILS
The primary inhabitants of the planes of Hell are the devils. 
They are the champions of lawful evil, and are the implacable 
foes of chaotic demonkind and all servants of good.
Devil society is organised into a strict hierarchy, which is never 
compromised for fear of retribution from the archdevils, who 
rule the planes of Hell. Nevertheless, squabbling and rivalries 
are common amongst devils. Amongst the archdevils them -
selves there also exists a hierarchy of barons, dukes, marquis -
es, and princes—cause for considerable rivalry and jealousy.
All devils can move between the various planes of Hell, although 
this usually requires permission from the archdevil who rules the 
particular plane. Devils may also move at will to Gehenna, Hades, 
and Acheron. Similarly, they can also travel to the Astral Plane, 
but this is rare. Devils are not allowed to enter the other planes 
(e.g. the Prime Material) without a proper summoning, a gate 
spell, or the invocation of their secret name (if they have one).
All devils of a certain rank possess special, magical abilities 
which may vary according to individual type. They can use these 
abilities at will one at a time. These abilities are: animate dead 
(as the 5th level magic-user spell), charm person (as the 1st level 
magic-user spell), fear (effect varies, as the 4th level magic-user 
spell), know alignment (as the 2nd level cleric spell), spectral force 
(as the 3rd level illusionist spell), suggestion (as the 3rd level 
magic-user spell), teleport (no chance of error, otherwise as the 
5th level magic-user spell), and summoning other devils (varies, 
as the 3rd level magic-user spell monster summoning I.
All devils have infravision and may understand and communi -
cate in any language.
Only the material form of a devil may be killed. To actually slay 
a devil one must do so in the planes of Hell or a neighbouring 
lower plane. If its material form is slain, a devil must return to 
its home plane in Hell for nine decades of servitude as a lemure 
before it will resume its former status.
===== PAGE 132 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 131
Once combat is joined with devils, they can never be subdued. 
In dire circumstances the major devils and archdevils may be 
willing to negotiate. Lesser devils will madly fight to the death. 
If it is to their advantage, devils can split their attacks among 2 
or more targets.
Devils are exceptionally conscious of any kind of laws or con -
tracts binding them to certain behaviour, and are profoundly 
adept at exploiting hidden loopholes. Negotiating with them 
can be quite tedious as every minute detail and exigency must 
be properly accounted for.
Just as when summoning a demon, proper inscription of a mag-
ical circle of protection is necessary for the conjurer’s safety 
when attempting to summon a devil. These circles are type-spe-
cific, with more powerful devils requiring proportionately more 
complex and expensive circles. Devils are also repulsed by good 
artifacts.
Devils are susceptible to attack as noted in the table below;
ATTACK FORM DAMAGE ROLLED
Acid Full damage
Cold Half damage
Electricity (lightning bolt) Full damage
Fire (dragon breath, fireball) None
Gas, poisonous (cloudkill) Half damage
Iron weapons No additional damage
Magic missile Full damage
 Poison Full damage
Silver Full damage (according to 
normal weapon type)
ASSAGIM (NIPHERIBU, NUPERIBBO)
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 9 [11]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d2) or 1 weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 50
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 18 +1/hp
These are the lowest of all the devils, and they are found in the 
millions throughout every plane of Hell. Assagim are the un -
fortunate souls of those not evil enough to be incarnated as 
lemures. They are totally senseless—having neither eyes, ears, 
or mouth. 
Assagim resemble bony, featureless humanoids with two crude 
arms ending in claws. In combat, they attack with their two 
sharp claws. If organised by an archdevil into an army, they are 
sometimes armed with crude clubs or maces which they use to 
blindly batter their opponents en masse. If slain in the service of 
evil, assagim will usually (99%) reincarnate within one day as an-
other assagim. A small percentage (1%) become lemures; and 
within another 10 millennia, they might again be reincarnated 
as one of the lesser devils.
Immunities and Resistances
Immunities. This creature is immune to fire damage and im -
mune to any condition that assumes the presence of senses 
such as hearing or sight.
Damage Resistances  (all half damage): Cold, Poison (including 
gases).
Special Features
Regeneration (Native Plane Only).  These creatures regenerate at 
a rate of 1hp/round if they are in Hell or one of the other lower 
planes of existence. This ability is lost in the material planes.
Unarmed Combat. Assagim can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.

132 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Language
Assagim (Nipheribu) are telepathically sensitive to other devils, 
and can receive commands from most other types of devil ex -
cept lemures.
BARBED DEVIL
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2 or 3d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 8
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (2d4) and 1 tail (3d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 50%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 85
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 1,425 +10/hp
These devils are quite common on the 3rd and 4th planes of 
Hell. They are typically used as guards and sentries, a task at 
which they excel. Any trespassers will be quickly imprisoned in 
one of their many iron cells to await torture.
Barbed devils do not need any hand-held weapons. They are 
quite proficient at using their hard, sharply-barbed claws and 
tail to beat their victims into submission. 
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (35%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 35% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immunities. This creature is immune to fire damage.
Damage Resistances  (all half damage): Cold, Poison (including 
gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: animate dead (as the 5th level magic-user spell), charm 
person (as the 1st level magic-user spell), fear (effect varies, 
as the 4th level magic-user spell), know alignment (as the 2nd 
level cleric spell), spectral force  (as the 3rd level illusionist 
spell), suggestion (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), teleport 
(no chance of error, otherwise as the 5th level magic-user 
spell), pyrotechnics (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), produce 
flame (as the 2nd level druid spell), hold person  (as the 3rd 
level magic-user spell), or summon an additional barbed devil 
to their aid (30% chance of success).
Special Attacks
Fear. Whenever a barbed devil hits an opponent, they cause fear 
(as the 4th level magic-user spell) if the victim fails a saving 
throw against spells.
Unarmed Combat.  Barbed devils can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.

Bearded devils make their home on the 3rd plane of Hell. They 
are renowned for both their bloodthirstiness and their mali -
cious cruelty. For this reason, they are often seen serving as 
shock troops in the legions of Hell. Bearded devils do not value 
treasure, although they may be influenced by offers of warm 
meat.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (45%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 45% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immunities. This creature is immune to fire damage.
Damage Resistances  (all half damage): Cold, Poison (including 
gases).

Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: animate dead (as the 5th level magic-user spell), charm 
person (as the 1st level magic-user spell), fear (effect varies, as 
the 4th level magic-user spell), know alignment (as the 2nd lev-
el cleric spell), spectral force (as the 3rd level illusionist spell), 
suggestion (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), teleport (no 
chance of error, otherwise as the 5th level magic-user spell), 
affect normal fires (as the 1st level magic-user spell), command 
(as the 1st level cleric spell), fear (touch only, otherwise as the 
===== PAGE 134 =====

4th level magic-user spell), produce flame (as the 2nd level 
druid spell), or summon another bearded devil (35% chance 
of success).
Special Attacks
Glaive. In combat, bearded devils prefer to use a wickedly 
barbed and hooked glaive, which can entangle an opponent 
intent on escape (1d3 damage plus the victim is immobilised 
and cannot attack. A successful Minor Test of Strength check is 
required to escape from the glaive.
Beards. If unarmed, these devils will grab victims in their claws 
and use their bristly beards to scrape and burn. If they manage 
to hit with both claws, they will also score maximum damage 
with their beards (8 hp). Any victim struck for maximum dam -
age from these “beard-burns” must make a save vs poison or be 
afflicted with a painful, itching rash (-1 dexterity every 4 rounds; 
duration 3d8 turns).
Unarmed Combat. Bearded devils can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks, although they usually don’t. If not fended off, 
they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/
DEF +10/-10. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grap -
pling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
BONE DEVIL
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2 or 2d4
SIZE Large (9ft)
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS -1 [21]
HIT DICE 9
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bone hook (3d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 90
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 1,000 +10/hp
Bone devils make their lair on the 5th plane of Hell. They are 
cruel, and enjoy torturing those weaker than themselves. They 
resemble large skeletons, covered in leathery white flesh, with 
a segmented tail like a scorpion. Unlike most devils, bone devils 
prefer the frigid climate of their home plane. 
Bone devils carry no treasure. They cannot be bribed with coins 
or gems, but they consider frozen humanoid flesh a delicacy.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (20%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 20% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immunities. This creature is immune to fire damage.
Damage Resistances  (all half damage): Cold, Poison (including 
gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: animate dead (as the 5th level magic-user spell), charm 
person (as the 1st level magic-user spell), fear (effect varies, 
as the 4th level magic-user spell), know alignment (as the 2nd 
level cleric spell), spectral force  (as the 3rd level illusionist 
spell), suggestion (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), teleport 
(no chance of error, otherwise as the 5th level magic-user 
spell), fear (5ft radius, otherwise as the 4th level magic-user 
spell), phantasmal force  (as the 1st level illusionist spell), fly 
(as the 3rd level magic-user spell), invisibility (as the 2nd level 
magic-user spell), detect invisibility (as the 2nd level magic-us-
er spell), or summon another bone devil (40% chance of suc -
cess). 
1/day: wall of ice (as the 4th level magic-user spell).
Special Attacks
Hook. Bone devils wield a large bone hook to snag their oppo -
nents. If they score a hit, there is a 50% chance the victim will 
be immobilised and cannot attack. A successful Minor Test of 
Strength check is required to escape from the hook. 
Tail Sting. If a victim is caught in the devil’s hook, the bone devil 
can then sting with their scorpion-like tails (2d4 damage, save 
vs poison or lose 1d4 strength for 10 rounds).
Unarmed Combat. Bone devils can make grappling or overbear-
ing attacks, although they usually don’t. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+10/-10. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Grappling Re-
sult Mods: ATK/DEF +3/-3.

These winged humanoids usually appear female, although a 
few (10%) are male. Erinyes dwell on the 2nd Plane of Hell, and 
are tasked with retrieving evil souls from the Prime Material 
Plane for their diabolical masters.
Erinyes are unrelenting pursuers and prefer to capture their vic-
tims alive and return them to Hell for punishment. On occasion, 
erinyes may be willing to bargain, but they are quite crafty and 
will always try to tempt the bargainer into committing a great 
sin.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (30%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 30% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immunities. This creature is immune to fire damage.
Damage Resistances  (all half damage): Cold, Poison (including 
gases).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: animate dead (as the 5th level magic-user spell), c harm 
person (as the 1st level magic-user spell), fear (effect varies, as 
the 4th level magic-user spell), know alignment (as the 2nd level 
cleric spell), spectral force (as the 3rd level illusionist spell), sug-
gestion (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), teleport (no chance 
of error, otherwise as the 5th level magic-user spell), fear (gaze, 
otherwise as the 4th level magic-user spell), detect invisibility (as 
the 2nd level magic-user spell), locate object (as the 2nd level 
magic-user spell), invisibility (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), 
polymorph self (as the 4th level magic-user spell), produce flame 
(as the 2nd level druid spell), or summon another erinyes to their 
aid (25% chance of success).
Special Attacks
Poison Dagger. In melee, erinyes use a magical dagger which is 
poisonous and causes horribly painful wounds (save vs poison 
or pass out for 1d6 rounds). 
Ropes of Entanglement. These devils also carry ropes of entangle-
ment, which they use to bind their prisoners.
Unarmed Combat. Erinyes devils can be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, although they don’t use these tactics. If 
not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. 
Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF 
+1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
HELLCAT (DEVILCAT)
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 7+2
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d4+1) and 1 bite (2d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 85
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 1,000 +10/hp
Hellcats are a minor type of devil, often seen serving their in -
fernal masters in the nine planes of Hell. Rarely, hellcats also 
travel to the Prime Material Plane to serve as a familiar for an 
important evil spell caster.
Hellcats encountered on the Prime Material Plane who are not 
already serving as a familiar may attach themselves to a suitable 
lawful evil character. They prefer to serve clerics, but will also at-
tach themselves to a magic-user or illusionist. If a stronger lawful 
evil character is present, hellcats will willingly switch to the new 
master, even betraying or attacking their former master in the 
process. In any case, a hellcat is only allowed to remain on the 
Prime Material Plane for a year and day. At the end of this period 
they must return to Hell. Sometimes they may later travel to the 
Prime Material Plane again, but will not necessarily seek out their 
old master.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (20%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 20% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Other Immunities.  Hellcats are immune to the effects of all 
charm, sleep, hold and suggestion spells. 
Special Features
Invisible in Light. In normal lighting, hellcats are invisible. In total 
darkness however, they can be dimly seen as a glowing red -
dish aura resembling a large feline. 
Unarmed Combat.  Hellcats cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics. 
===== PAGE 136 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 135
HORNED DEVIL
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2 or 1d4+1
SIZE Large (9ft)
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 90ft; 180ft flying (AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS -5 [25]
HIT DICE 5+5

SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 55%
INTELLIGENCE High (13-14)
MORALE 75
LOOT Hoard 9
EXPERIENCE 1,320 +6/hp
Horned devils are inhabitants of the 6th and 7th planes of Hell. 
Although considered to be greater devils, they are weaker than 
either ice devils or pit fiends, whom they despise. Horned devils 
have their own personal names which can be used to summon 
and control them.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Immunities. This creature is immune to fire damage.
Damage Resistances  (all half damage): Cold, Poison (including 
gases).
Special Features
Fear Aura. Horned devils constantly emanate fear (as the 4th lev-
el magic-user spell) in a 5ft radius. 
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: animate dead (as the 5th level magic-user spell), charm 
person (as the 1st level magic-user spell), fear (effect varies, as 
the 4th level magic-user spell), know alignment (as the 2nd lev-
el cleric spell), spectral force (as the 3rd level illusionist spell), 
suggestion (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), teleport (no 
chance of error, otherwise as the 5th level magic-user spell), 
ESP (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), detect magic  (as the 
1st level magic-user spell), phantasmal force  (as the 1st lev -
el illusionist spell), pyrotechnics (as the 2nd level magic-user 
spell), produce flame (as the 2nd level druid spell) or summon 
another horned devil (50% chance of success).
1/day: wall of fire that inflicts 3d8 hit points of fire damage.
Special Attacks
Melee Weapons. In combat, horned devils typically use either a 
barbed fork (75%) for 2d6 damage or a jagged whip (25%) that 
inflicts 1d4 damage plus the victim is stunned 1d4 rounds—
save vs spells for unlisted categories to avoid).
Wounding Tail. As part of their normal attack, these devils can 
attack with their sharply-tipped tails, which cause weeping 
wounds (1d3 damage plus 1 hp of damage per turn until the 
wound is bound or cured).
Unarmed Combat. Horned devils can be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, although they usually do not engage in 
grappling. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-6. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +3/-3. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +3/-3.
ICE DEVIL (GREATER DEVIL)

Ice devils resemble upright remorhaz with humanoid arms and 
legs, and knotted, barbed tails. They dominate the 8th plane 
of Hell, which serves as their frozen home. They are also very 
strong (18.76 strength) and enjoy torturing their victims before 
killing them. Ice devils also have secret personal names which 
can be used to summon them, if the name is somehow known 
to the conjurer.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (55%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 55% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane and +1 Weapons.  This creature is immune 
to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and un-
armed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a 
magical bonus of +2 or more.
Immunities. This creature is immune to fire damage.
Damage Resistances  (all half damage): Cold, Poison (including 
gases).

Regeneration. If injured, the ice devil regenerates 1 hp per round.

Special Attacks
Magic Spear. Some ice devils (25% chance) wield long, magical 
spears which they can use to impale and freeze their victims. 
The spear inflicts 2d6 piercing damage, and if a victim is hit 
they must make a saving throw vs. paralysation or be reduced 
to half their normal move (duration 3d6 rounds). The effect 
can only reduce a victim to one-quarter move, after which ad -
ditional attacks have no further effect.
Unarmed Combat. Ice devils can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, although they usually do not engage in 
grappling. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Results Mods:

IMP (LESSER DEVIL)
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Tiny
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 60ft; 180ft flying (AA:V)
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 2+2
MELEE ATTACKS Tail (1d4 + poison)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 60
LOOT Cache 1
EXPERIENCE 275 +3/hp
Imps are formed from soul worms, created by archdevils to 
spread evil through service to a lawful evil priest or sorcerer. 
They are very rarely encountered on the Prime Material Plane, 
but are commonly seen in the lower planes. They have only av-
erage intelligence, but in their role as familiars they are able to 
rely on the knowledge of their archdevil master.
When serving as a familiar to an evil magic-user or cleric, the 
Imp imparts the following benefits to its master: Constant 
full-sensory telepathic contact between the Imp and the master 
up to 1 mile in range. If the master is within 25ft of the imp, the 
former gains the Imp’s 25% magic resistance and its regenera-
tion ability (1 hp/round). If the master is within 1 mile of the Imp, 
the former gains an additional level of ability. However, if the 
imp is killed, the master immediately loses four ability levels.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (25%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 25% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Non-Silver Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +1 or more, or is a silver weapon.
Other Immunities. Imps are also immune to cold, fire, and elec -
trical damage. 
Special Features
Polymorph. Imps are created with the innate ability to polymorph 
self. The GM should choose two of the following forms (giant 
rat, goat, large spider, raven). In their polymorphed form, imps 
are limited to that animal’s natural attacks. 
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: detect good (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), detect 
magic (as the 1st level magic-user spell), and invisibility (as the 
2nd level magic-user spell). 
1/day: Once per day, an imp can cast a suggestion (as the 3rd 
level magic-user spell).
1/Week: Once per week it can commune with the lower planes 
(6 questions maximum, otherwise as the 5th level cleric spell).
Special Attacks
Poison Sting.  In their natural form, imps attack with their poi -
son–tipped tail stingers. Any victim struck must save vs poison 
or die.
Unarmed Combat. Imps can be targeted with grappling or over -
bearing attacks, although they do not use these tactics. If not 
fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit 
Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF -4/+4. 
Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -3/+3.
===== PAGE 138 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 137
LEMURE (LEAST DEVIL)
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 5d6
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 30ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 pseudopod (1d3)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 100%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 60
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 65 +3/hp
Lemures are the primal form of evil souls damned to spend 
eternity in Hell. They are only vaguely humanoid, with crude 
arms and heads. Due to their ongoing torment, lemures are 
quite insane and will attack any non-devils coming close to 
them. Certain lemures (5%) are chosen by archdevils to form 
wraiths, spectres, and other ethereal undead.
Lemures cannot normally be destroyed, except by blessed or 
holy items or weapons. 
Immunities and Resistances
Immunities. Lemures are immune to all forms of sleep or charm 
spells, and to fire damage.
Damage Resistances  (all half damage): Cold, Poison (including 
gases).
Special Features
Regeneration. Lemures regenerate 1 hit point per round unless 
the damage is caused by holy water or a weapon that has had 
a bless spell cast upon it. 
Unarmed Combat.  Lemures are too squishy/oozy to be over -
borne or grappled, and do not employ these tactics.

The manalishi, also known as “scaly devils” and “abyssai,” are 
quite common on the upper planes of Hell. Most manalishi 
serve the five-headed dragon queen of Hell.
Manalishi are humanoid-shaped with horns, bat-like wings, 
a long spiked tail, and vaguely draconic features. Their scaled 
hide varies, as there are five distinct breeds of manalishi: black, 
blue, green, red, and white.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance.  See summary stats for individual levels of 
magic resistance.
Damage Resistances  (all half damage): Cold, Poison (including 
gases).
===== PAGE 139 =====
138 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Special Features
Regeneration. Manalishi regenerate at a rate of 1 hit point per 
round unless the damage was caused by holy water, a silver 
weapon, or a blessed weapon.
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: change self (as the 1st level illusionist spell), command 
(as the 1st level cleric spell), produce flame (as the 2nd level 
druid spell), pyrotechnics (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), 
or scare (as the 2nd level magic-user spell). 
1/day: Once per day, a manalishi can attempt to summon an -
other manalishi (20% chance of success).
Unarmed Combat. Varies by type.
SHAITAN (GREATER DEVIL; STYX FIEND)
FREQUENCY Very Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 60ft; 150ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS -1 [21]
HIT DICE 6+6
MELEE ATTACKS 1 touch (2d4 + imprisonment)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 60%
INTELLIGENCE High (13-14)
MORALE 80
LOOT Cache 3 and 4
EXPERIENCE 1,275+ 8/hp
These devils resemble humanoids with large, bulbous heads 
and small, evil features. Their wings are black with silver tips.
Shaitan dwell on the 5th plane of Hell, where they serve the 
archdevil, Geryon. Their primary task is to acquire souls for 
their master. Occasionally, they will travel to the Prime Material 
plane on a mission of murder and mayhem. 
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Non-Silver Mundane Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +1 or more, or is a silver weapon.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: animate dead (as the 5th level magic-user spell), charm 
person (as the 1st level magic-user spell), fear (effect varies, as 
the 4th level magic-user spell), know alignment (as the 2nd lev-
el cleric spell), spectral force (as the 3rd level illusionist spell), 
suggestion (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), teleport (no 
chance of error, otherwise as the 5th level magic-user spell).
Dispelling. Dispelling a shaitan on the Prime Material plane 
requires pronouncement of a holy word (the 7th level cleric 
spell).
Special Attacks
Imprisonment. In combat, these devils will attempt to touch their 
victim (2d4 damage) and thereby cast an imprisonment spell 
(as the 9th level magic-user spell).
Unarmed Combat. Shaitan devils can be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, although they do not use these tactics. 
If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. 
Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF 
+1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
SPIKED DEVIL (LEAST DEVIL; SPINED FIEND)
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d4 or 5d4
SIZE Small (4ft)
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 60ft; 180ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 3+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 weapon and/or 2 claws (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 65
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 240 +4/hp
Spiked devils are small and weak compared to the other dev -
ils, but are amongst the most commonly seen types. They have 
scaly reddish skin and bat-like wings. A row of dark spines runs 
from their forehead to the base of their tail. Both their hands 
and feet have sharp claws.
In melee, they usually attack with a trident, fork, or similar pole 
arm. When flying and attacking a target on the ground, they can 
also attack with their clawed feet.
Spiked devils are typically used for herding assagim or lemures, 
or serving as a messenger for more powerful devils.
These devils rarely have any sort of treasure or possessions 
beyond their weapon. Only when serving as a messenger are 
===== PAGE 140 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 139
they likely to possess something of value. In these cases, the 
GM should decide what, if anything, the spiked devil is carrying.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (25%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 25% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: affect normal fires  (as the 1st level magic-user spell), 
change self (as the 1st level illusionist spell), command (as the 
1st level cleric spell), produce flame  (as the 2nd level druid 
spell), and scare (as the 2nd level magic-user spell). 
1/day: Attempt to summon a barbed devil (5% chance of suc -
cess).
Special Attacks
Flying Attack. When flying, if attacking a target on the ground, 
these devils can also attack with their clawed feet.
Ranged Attack: Spines. The spiked devil’s spines burst into flame 
when plucked from the devil’s back. If attacking from above, 
these devils can also shoot up to 12 of their spines (1d4 dam -
age, like a dart). In melee, should they choose to grapple, 1d4 
of the spines will damage the opponent in addition to any 
grappling damage.
Unarmed Combat. Spiked devils can be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, although they do not use these tactics. 
If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. 
Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF 
-2/+2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.
PIT FIEND (GREATER DEVIL)
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1 or 1d3
SIZE Large (12ft)
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 60ft; 150ft flying (AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS –3 [23]
HIT DICE 13
MELEE ATTACKS 1 scimitar (1d8+6) and 1 spiked club 
(1d6+7) and tail (2d4 constriction)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect invisible 65%
LAIR CHANCE 65%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 110
LOOT Individual 1 and Cache 4
EXPERIENCE 7,900 +18/hp
Pit fiends are native to the 9th Plane of Hell, and are the per -
sonal servants of Asmodeus, the Lord of Hell. They are both 
incredibly strong and diabolically evil. All pit fiends have secret 
personal names which can be used to summon them.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (65%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 65% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane and +1 Weapons.  This creature is immune 
to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and un-
armed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a 
magical bonus of +2 or more.
Special Features
Fear Aura.  Pit fiends exude fear (as the 4th level magic-user 
spell) in a 20ft radius.
Regeneration. Pit fiends regenerate 2 hp per combat round until 
they are killed, at which point the regeneration stops.
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: animate dead (as the 5th level magic-user spell), charm 
person (as the 1st level magic-user spell), fear (effect varies, 
as the 4th level magic-user spell), know alignment (as the 2nd 
level cleric spell), spectral force  (as the 3rd level illusionist 
spell), suggestion (as the 3rd level magic-user spell), teleport 
(no chance of error, otherwise as the 5th level magic-user 
spell), detect invisibility (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), de-
tect magic (as the 1st level magic-user spell), hold person (as 
the 3rd level magic-user spell), polymorph self (as the 4th level 
magic-user spell), produce flame (as the 2nd level druid spell), 
pyrotechnics (as the 2nd level magic-user spell), wall of fire (as 
the 4th level magic-user spell), or summon 1d3 barbed devils 
(60%) or another pit fiend (70%). 
1/day: Once per day, a pit fiend can use a symbol of pain (as the 
7th level cleric spell).
Special Attacks
Tail Constriction. In addition to their weapons, pit fiends can also 
strike with their serpentine tail. A hit causes 2d4 points of con-
striction damage per round (automatically after a hit) and can 
only be escaped if the victim makes a successful Open Doors 
roll as an action.
Unarmed Combat. Pit fiends can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and under some circumstances they may 
use these tactics. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +3/-3.
===== PAGE 141 =====
140 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
DINOSAURS
In OSRIC the “dinosaur” category also includes other creatures 
contemporaneous with or predating the dinosaurs. This means 
that for the purposes of the OSRIC core rules, prehistoric crea -
tures such as dimetrodon or plesiosaurus are included with 
“dinosaurs”, even though palaeontologists classify them sepa -
rately.
These rules do include an indication of the rough period in 
which the dinosaur lived, but dinosaur-infested areas often re -
semble Sir Arthur Conan Doyle’s The Lost World in that a mish -
mash of dinosaurs from different times co-exist. Dinosaur areas 
are only really suitable for relatively high-level play. Portals to 
such areas can sometimes be found in the nethermost depths 
of large dungeons.
Unarmed Combat. Dinosaurs cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics. 
TYRANNOSAURIDS AND ALLOSAURIDS
ALBERTOSAURUS ALLOSAURUS GORGOSAURUS SAUROPHAGANAX TARBOSAURUS TYRANNOSAURUS

Tyrannosaurids and allosaurids are not related families, but 
they occupy the same ecological niche: large bipedal carnivores. 
Allosaurus and saurophaganax are allosaurids, the others are 
tyrannosaurids. The “Number Encountered” listing allows for 
two interpretations—they could be solitary predators or pack 
hunters.
Tyrannosaurus: Cretaceous carnivore, long thought to be the 
largest land carnivore of the dinosaur age. Gorgosaurus: Cre-
taceous carnivore, smaller than tyrannosaurus. Tarbosaurus: 
Cretaceous carnivore, closely related to tyrannosaurus. Alberto-
saurus: Much smaller Cretaceous carnivore. Allosaurus: Jurassic 
carnivore. Saurophaganax: Jurassic carnivore, may be just a very 
large allosaurus.

Herbivores who foraged on all fours and ran on hind legs, there 
are two main families of the duck–billed dinosaurs known as 
hadrosaurids: hadrosaurines and lambeosaurines. In game 
terms there is no difference. Some could be interpreted as 
Semi-amphibious swamp or wetland dwellers, in which case 
they should have a swimming move, but many, perhaps all, are 
fully terrestrial. Trachodon and Anatosaurs are now–supersed -
ed names for kinds of hadrosaurid.
Edmontosaurus: Cretaceous herbivore, formerly “anatosaurus”. 
Charonosaurus: Cretaceous herbivore. Shantungosaurus: Creta-
ceous herbivore. Saurolophus: Cretaceous herbivore. Hypacro-
saurus: Cretaceous herbivore. Telmatosaurus: Smaller Creta -
ceous herbivore.
