GIANT GAR
Giant gar live in deep rivers and lakes. 
Special Attacks
Swallow Whole. Giant gar can swallow prey whole on an attack roll of 
20, including creatures up to Medium size. A swallowed person has 
a cumulative 5% chance per segment of drowning, but if they have a 
bladed weapon they can try to cut their way out of the fish. To do so, 
25% of the giant gar’s total hp must be inflicted. Any piercing attack 
against a giant gar has a 20% chance of causing equal damage to a 
swallowed creature.
GIANT PIKE
Giant pike are aggressive predators found in larger and deeper lakes. 
They often guard nixie lairs. 
Surprise and Initiative
Due to their great speed and acuity of vision, giant pike will surprise 
their prey on a 1–4 on 1d6.
LEVIATHANS
Leviathans are huge fish that may originate from the deepest oceans 
or the Elemental Plane of Water. 
Special Attacks
Capsize Ships.  Leviathans can overturn small vessels 
90% of the time, oceangoing ships 50% of the time, 
and huge vessels 10% of the time.
Swallow Whole. A successful “to hit” roll inflicts the dam-
age shown and any creature attacked must save vs 
death or be swallowed whole (see “giant gar” for the 
effects of this).
FLY, GIANT
GIANT  
BLOW FLY
GIANT 
HORSEFLY
FREQUENCY Rare Very rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1d12 1d6
SIZE Medium Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 90ft; 300ft flying 
(AA:III)
90ft; 300ft flying 
(AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] 5 [15]
HIT DICE 3 6
MELEE ATTACKS 1d8+1 2d6
SENSES Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0) Non- (0)
MORALE N/A N/A
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 40 +3/hp 165 +6/hp
Giant flies are more massive than the normal variety of 
flying insects and thus are Aerial Agility: Level III, though 
they can hover. They do retain a normal fly’s ability to 
react quickly and thus they can jump away from an at -
tack in only 1 segment. This jumping defensive move 
is a backward springing leap which carries the giant fly 
30ft away and does not count as a retreat for purpos -
es of a free attack. Giant flies cannot be overborne or 
grappled, and do not employ these tactics. 
GIANT BLOW FLY
Giant blow flies have a shiny metallic green or blue 
body covered with coarse black hair. Their large eyes 
are a dark orange colour. These flies will rarely attack 
living prey unless someone is covered with blood or 
has open wounds. They are also attracted to sweet 
food and drink; an abundance of these might garner 
the attention of this monster. Giant flies are more often 
encountered wherever there is an abundance of their 
preferred diet: carrion, rotting food, and malodorous 
refuse. 
Special Attacks
The bite of a giant blow fly has a 10% chance of infecting 
the victim with disease. 
===== PAGE 165 =====
164 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
GIANT HORSEFLY
This giant insect is even larger than the giant blow fly and far 
more aggressive. The giant horsefly dines on fresh blood and it 
will land on any warm-blooded creature is encounters, attempt-
ing to draw blood from the target with its bite. Giant horseflies 
are flat tan and brown in colour, with shiny brown eyes.
Special Attacks
Suck Blood.  After a successful bite, the horsefly continues to 
automatically inflict damage as it draws blood from the bite 
wound. This process can only be halted by killing the giant 
horsefly.
FROG, GIANT
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 5d8
SIZE Small to Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 30ft; 90ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1 to 3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d3, 1d6 or 2d4)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE
1 HD: 10 +5/hp 
2 HD: 30 +10/hp 
3 HD: 50 +15/hp
Giant frogs can be 2, 4, or 6ft in length. They are found anywhere 
their normal-size cousins are. A 2ft frog weighs 50 lbs and can 
jump 180ft, a 4ft frog weighs 150 lbs and can jump 140ft, and a 
6ft frog weighs 250 lbs and can jump 100ft.
Surprise and Initiative
Giant frogs have natural camouflage and surprise on a roll of 
1–4 on 1d6.
Special Attacks
Tongue Pull. The frog can shoot its tongue up to three times its 
body length, and has +4 to hit with this attack. The tongue does 
no damage: it only sticks to the victim. Any creature hit with the 
tongue can attempt to free itself by striking the tongue. If the 
victim manages to strike the tongue, the frog will retract it and 
attack a different target next round.
If the victim is not freed and weighs less than the frog, the frog 
will pull the victim into its mouth, inflicting maximum damage 
automatically each round thereafter. If the victim weighs more 
than the giant frog it will take 2 rounds to drag the victim into its 
mouth. If the victim is more than double the frog’s weight, the 
frog will not be able to move the victim (on the 3rd round the 
frog will release its tongue).
Swallow Whole. If the frog scores a natural 20 on an attack, it 
has swallowed its prey (unless the target is a larger size than 
the frog). A giant frog can swallow whole anything elf-sized or 
smaller. If swallowed, there is a chance for the victim to cut 
their way out. They must have in hand a sharp edged weapon 
and score an 18 or better. Prey get three attempts to escape. 
Any hit to the giant frog, that has swallowed its victim, has a 
33% chance of doing damage to the victim as well.
Unarmed Combat. Giant frogs cannot be overborne or grappled, 
and do not employ these tactics. 
FUNGI, VIOLET
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Small to Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 10ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 1d4 branches (special)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 110 +2/hp
Violet fungi look like shriekers and in fact the two are often 
found growing together. The plants are usually 4ft to 7ft tall, 
and each plant has 1d4 branches growing out of it. At 4ft tall, 
the fungi will have 1ft branches. For every foot taller the fungi 
grows, the branches grow a foot longer. 

===== PAGE 166 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 165
Immunities and Resistances
Plant Immunities. This creature doesn’t need to eat, drink, sleep, 
or breathe as an animal would. Magic can’t put it to sleep. Gas-
es can’t harm it, and it is immune to poison and disease. It 
does not rely upon sight, so it automatically detects invisible 
opponents.
Special Attacks
Spores. When the fungi detects movement nearby it will wave its 
branches around, releasing its spores. Anyone within 4ft must 
save vs poison or their exposed flesh will begin to rot within 
1 round. Anyone who fails their save will die in 1d4+4 rounds 
unless the rotting is stopped by a cure disease spell.
G
GARGOYLE
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d8
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft; 150ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 4+4
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d3), 1 horn (1d4) 
and 1 bite (1d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 70
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 155 +4/hp
Gargoyles are cruel creatures, 90% likely to attack any living 
creature they encounter. They resemble statues of winged hu -
manoids, and are almost indistinguishable from carved stone 
until they move. They are usually encountered in ruins and un -
derground caverns.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Unarmed Combat.  Gargoyles can attack and be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score 
an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-
6. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
GELATINOUS CUBE
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 8 [12]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (2d4)
SENSES Special
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE 150 +4/hp
Gelatinous cubes are dungeon scavengers. They move slowly 
through corridors and rooms, absorbing carrion, fungi, algae 
and other nutrients from the surfaces they touch.
Gelatinous cubes can only digest organic material, and they 
will sometimes sweep up inorganic substances (including met -
al items such as coins, glass or ceramic items such as potion 
bottles, etc.). These incidental items are sometimes kept in the 
body of the ‘cube for many days before being ejected. Such 
items form the creature’s treasure.
Surprise and Initiative
Being silent and virtually transparent, gelatinous cubes have a 
higher-than-usual chance to surprise (1–3 on 1d6).
Immunities and Resistances
Ooze. This creature cannot be knocked prone, and is not affect-
ed by lack of light, invisible opponents, gases, or other circum-
stances that would not affect an ooze.
Immunities. Gelatinous cubes are immune to electricity, fear, 
sleep, hold, paralysis, and polymorph. 
Reaction to Cold. Cold-based attacks slow a gelatinous cube (as 
the spell) but only inflict a maximum of 1d4 damage, unless 
they make a saving throw, in which case the cold-based attack 
will have no effect at all (including the slowing effect).
Special Attacks
Paralysing Touch. The touch of a gelatinous cube causes paraly-
sis for 3d6+2 rounds (a saving throw vs paralysation is permit-
ted to avoid this effect).
Unarmed Combat.  It should go without saying that gelatinous 
cubes cannot and should not be overborne or grappled. They 
do not employ these tactics. 
===== PAGE 167 =====
166 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
GHAST
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d6
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d4) and 1 bite (1d8)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 65
LOOT Hoard 2, Cache 3, 4, 5 and 6
EXPERIENCE 195 +4/hp
These terrible undead creatures are more powerful versions of 
ghouls, and are indistinguishable from them save their terrible 
stench, released when engaged in melee. Ghasts, when acting 
as a pack, can travel the dream-realms and can also enter the 
lower planes. However, this takes too long for it to be a factor in 
combat situations. Certain entities of the higher hells use ghasts 
as slaves.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Ghasts are turned as type 6 Undead.
Undeath. This creature is immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by sleep, gases, 
poison, or disease.
Immune to Protection from Evil.  Ghasts are not affected by pro-
tection from evil spells (or effects, such as a paladin’s ability).
Special Features
Stench. When ghasts are in melee combat, they release a stench 
of death so strong that anyone within 10ft must make a saving 
throw vs poison. Anyone affected by the stench begins retch -
ing, suffering -2 to all actions, including to-hit rolls and damage.
Special Attacks
Paralysation. If a ghast hits with its claws or bite, the victim must 
make a saving throw vs. paralysation or be paralysed for 3d4 
full turns.
Unarmed Combat. Ghasts can attack and be targeted with grap-
pling or overbearing attacks although they seldom use this 
tactic. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ 
on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
GHOST
FREQUENCY Very Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Any evil
MOVE 90ft floating
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20] when manifest/special
HIT DICE 10+4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (Wither)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect invisible 25%
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE High (13-14)
MORALE 100
LOOT Hoard 5, Cache 5
EXPERIENCE 4,200 +14/hp
Ghosts are the spiritual remains of extremely evil humans who 
have been denied the ordinarily inexorable movement of their 
souls to the outer planes of existence after discarding their 
mortal shell. This sundering of their metaphysical essence cre -
ates a foul thing, roaming dark and desolate places, existing in 
both the ethereal plane and the prime material, seeking to slake 
a thirst that can never be sated. This exigent need for living es -
sences is what drives these spirits into contact with mortals. The 
ghost has different attributes when non-manifest (ethereal) and 
after it has manifested.
A ghost’s first attack, before it manifests itself, is an attempt at a 
magic jar. Any creature within 180ft is threatened by the being’s 
magic jar ability, as per the spell. The ghost cannot be attacked 
while it is still unmanifested (unless the attacker is also ethere-
al). If the magic jar fails, then the ghost will manifest itself, taking 

===== PAGE 168 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 167
on an ectoplasmic quality. Specially forged “pure” metals, espe-
cially silver, can cause half-damage to a ghost while it is mani -
fested in this fashion, and magic weapons can affect it normally. 
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Ghosts are turned as type 11 Undead.
Undeath. This creature is immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by sleep, gases, 
poison, or disease.
Ethereal Form. Until the ghost manifests itself physically, it is im-
mune to all attacks, spell or no, unless the attacker has a meth-
od to become ethereal. Even if the spell caster is in this state, 
ghosts are still immune to many forms of spells, though no 
sage has recorded what those magics might be. Magical weap-
ons used in an ethereal state against a ghost typically have to 
strike against AC 7 [13] or 8 [12].
Immune to Non-Silver Mundane Weapons.  Once manifested, the 
ghost is still immune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage 
from weapons, unless the weapon causing that damage has a 
magical bonus of +1 or more, or is silver (which only inflicts half 
damage unless also magical).
Special Features
Deadly to Look Upon.  Merely beholding the awfulness of the 
ghost requires a saving throw vs magic (bonus described be -
low). Failure causes the viewer to flee in panic (for 2d8 turns) 
and initiates a weaker form of the ghost’s wither attack—which 
ages the victim 3d6 years as the ghost feasts on the life of the 
victim. Clerics level 7 or greater can ignore this effect, as they 
have a better understanding of how to ward off such meta -
physical forces, while other creatures with 9 or more hit dice 
or levels gain a +3 to their saves.
Special Attacks
Magic Jar. A ghost’s first attack, before it manifests itself, is an 
attempt at a magic jar. Any creature within 180ft is threatened 
by the being’s magic jar ability, as per the spell. 
Wither. The manifest state allows the ghost to strike victims 
physically, with the full power of their withering attack—caus -
ing the victim to age 7d6 years per successful attack, no save 
possible. Victims who exceed their allotted lifespan are forever 
dead, with only a wish capable of reversing this end.
Optional Rule: As an abstract way of handling aging, for every 
10 years of aging, the victim is treated as having a -1 to all sav-
ing throws (until the aging is reversed). If the victim has been 
aged 60 years (120 years in the case of demi-humans), this 
indicates death from old age. Demi-humans generally have a 
longer lifespan than 120 years, but it’s easy to consider that 
ghostly aging is also quite traumatic.
Unarmed Combat. Ghosts cannot be overborne or grappled, and 
do not employ these tactics. 
GHOUL
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 4d6
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 1d3/1d3/1d6
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 55
LOOT Horde 2, Cache 6
EXPERIENCE 70 +2/hp
Ghouls are undead creatures, possibly once human. Loping 
through the darkness with their vague canine cast and long, 
black, marrow-licking tongues, they haunt graves and ruins, 
seeking the flesh of the dead and the living. Though their minds 
are warped by the transformation, and their general intellect 
stunted, they still retain a terrible cunning, and they attack in 
fearless packs to good effect, bringing down the living to feast 
on their remains. Any person killed by ghouls will eventually rise 
as a ghoul unless the body is blessed. Such casualties can be 
raised from the dead normally, as well, as long as it is done be -
fore the ghoul transformation sets in.
Protection from evil will keep these things at bay.
Ghouls are also said to be able travel the lands of dreams, using 
them as conduit to enter the waking world of men at various 
graveyards, and escaping without a trace. Marine ghouls are 
called “lacedons” and are sometimes found on ghost ships or 
dwelling in wrecks on the sea bed.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Ghouls are turned as type 3 Undead.
Undeath. This creature is immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by sleep, gases, 
poison, or disease.
Special Attacks
Paralysation. Any human or demi-human (except elves) hit by a 
ghoul must make a saving throw vs. paralysis or be paralysed 
for 3d4 turns.
Unarmed Combat. Ghouls can attack and be targeted with grap-
pling or overbearing attacks although they seldom use this tac-
tic. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 
1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-6. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/
DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
Language
Ghouls, unlike most undead, have their own language which 
they share with ghasts. Their voices are weird and eldritch, 
sometimes described as “meeping” and “glibbering”.
===== PAGE 169 =====
168 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
GIANTS
Far from being mere brutes, giants’ cunning makes up for their 
sometimes low intelligence. Giants can sometimes be persuad-
ed, so long it is within their interests, to cooperate with other 
beings of like alignment.
Despite the diversity of giant types, these monsters share some 
common characteristics. Giants are able to lift and throw large 
rocks like a catapult, even at close range, with their immense 
strength of 19–25. Immature giants function at a percentage of 
that of adults.
Giants’ great love of gold nearly matches their strength, and 
they commonly carry 1,000–6,000 coins, usually gold, with them 
when away from their cave dens. As if in defence of this trea -
sure, they also commonly carry one or two rocks to use as con-
venient weapons.
Giants speak one language unique to their type, as well as their 
alignment language.
GIANT, CLOUD
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d6
SIZE Large (18ft tall)
ALIGNMENT Neutral good (50%) or Neutral evil 
(50%)
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 12 (+1d6 hp)
MELEE ATTACKS 1 weapon (6d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect invisible 15%
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10) up to very (11-12)
MORALE 110
LOOT Hoard 5, Cache 3 (x5)
EXPERIENCE 3,520 +16/hp
Cloud giants usually live in giant castles at high elevations. When 
not high up in the mountains, their castles are on clouds given 
substance by magic. The majority (90%) of these giants have av-
erage intelligence; the remainder are very intelligent and most 
often live in castles in the sky. Cloud giants often dress in flow -
ing clothing and fancy jewellery. They have bronze to white hair, 
with skin in tones of blue ranging from nearly white to cold, light 
blue.
When multiple giants are found in a castle, there is a 75% chance 
that it is a family consisting of two adults, with any remaining gi-
ants being children (treat as ogres). The other 25% of the time, 
all of the giants are adults. Evil cloud giants have a 50% chance 
to be keeping 1d3 human slaves, and also have a 50% chance to 
own 1d4 prehistoric lions.
Surprise and Initiative
Sense of Smell. Cloud giants are rarely (1 on 1d6) surprised due 
to their impressive olfactory abilities.
Special Features
Levitation. The “very intelligent” class of cloud giants have the 
ability to levitate the equivalent of 20,000 gp, plus their body 
weight, twice a day.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack (Thrown Rock).  All cloud giants can throw rocks 
to inflict 2d12 blunt damage from 10 to 240ft distance from a 
target, and can catch thrown rocks 60% of the time.
Unarmed Combat.  Cloud giants cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and do not use this tactic. 

===== PAGE 170 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 169
GIANT, FIRE
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d8
SIZE Large (12ft)
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 11 (+1d4 hp)
MELEE ATTACKS 1 weapon (5d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect invisible 5%
LAIR CHANCE 35%
INTELLIGENCE Low up to average (5-10)
MORALE 105
LOOT Hoard 5
EXPERIENCE 2,720 +16/hp
Fire giants’ hair is the red and orange colour of flames, and their 
skin is soot black. Their teeth are flame orange, and their eyes 
red. The commonly wear armour on their broad, 6ft wide shoul-
ders. This armour is sometimes made from the skin of dragons.
Like cloud giants, fire giants often live in castles (50%), but are 
just as likely to frequent caves. It is not uncommon for many 
adult fire giants to live together. When more than six fire giants 
are encountered, the additional individuals will be immature 
and will only have a percentage of adult capabilities (as hill gi -
ants or ogres). 
Fire giants sometimes share their lairs with 1d4 hell hounds 
(25% chance).
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Fire.  As their name suggests, all fire giants are un -
harmed by fire. This extends to the flame breath produced by 
red dragons.
Special Attacks
Like most giants, fire giants delight in throwing rocks at en -
emies. They can hit a target from a distance of 10ft to 200ft 
(2d10 hit points of blunt damage), and can similarly catch (50% 
chance) thrown rocks.
Unarmed Combat. Fire giants cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks. They will occasionally make an over -
bearing attack. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 16+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/na. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +4/na. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
na/na.
GIANT, FROST
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d8
SIZE Large (15ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 10 (+1d4 hp)
MELEE ATTACKS 1 weapon (4d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Sixth sense 5%
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Low up to average (5-10)
MORALE 100
LOOT Hoard 5
EXPERIENCE 1,820 +14/hp
Frost giants have the overall appearance of giant, muscled bar-
barians. They have light yellow or blue eyes, with white hair that 
can likewise be tinged with yellow or blue. These giants get their 
name from their love of the cold. They often live within frosty 
caverns, but also have a propensity to take up residence in cas-
tles, as do some of their other giant kin. 
Winter wolves often share residence with frost giants (50% 
chance, 1d12 in number). When encountering more than 4 frost 
giants, the first six giants are adults, and additional individuals 
will be immature (treat as hill giants or ogres). 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Cold. Frost giants are completely immune to ill effects 
from cold, including the bone–chilling breath of white dragons.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack (Thrown Rocks).  Frost giants can cause 2d10 hit 
points in damage by throwing large rocks from between 10ft 
to 200ft. Likewise, they can catch rocks tossed at them with a 
40% probability.
Unarmed Combat.  Frost giants cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and do not use such tactics.
===== PAGE 171 =====
170 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
GIANT, HILL
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d10
SIZE Large (10.5ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 8+1d2
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (2d8)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 85
LOOT Hoard 4
EXPERIENCE 1,200 +12/hp
In many ways hill giants resemble large ogres, and they will often 
wield some type of bludgeoning weapon. They dress in animal 
skins and live in mostly inhospitable locations. Half of the time 
hill giants have other animals or beings guarding their homes. 
When guards are present, there is a 30% probability they are 1 
to 3 giant lizards, 20% probability they are 2d4 ogres, and 50% 
probability they are 2d4 dire wolves. When encountering more 
than 4 hill giants, the first seven are adults and additional indi -
viduals are young (treat as ogres). 
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack (Thrown Rocks). Hill giants can cause 2d8 hit points 
in damage by throwing large rocks from between 10ft to 200ft. 
Likewise, they can catch rocks tossed at them with a 30% prob-
ability.
Unarmed Combat. Hill giants cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks. They will occasionally make an over -
bearing attack. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/na. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +4/na. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF na/na.
GIANT, STONE
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d8
SIZE Large (12ft)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 9+1d4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (3d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 90
LOOT Hoard 4
EXPERIENCE 1,500 +14/hp
Stone giants are partially named for their stone-like complex -
ions and iron coloured eyes. Their hair is also dark-stone co -
loured, sometimes with hints of blue. Whereas hill giants often 
use wooden clubs, stone giants prefer stone and wear skins the 
colour of stone.
These giants live in mountainous locations exclusively, in cav -
erns or otherwise in the earth. They are most active at night. 
Three-quarters of the time stone giants have 1d4 cave bears 
guarding their homes. When encountering more than 4 stone 
giants, the first six adults. Additional giants are young (treat as 
hill giants or ogres). 
Stone giants sometimes share their lairs with cave bears (50% 
chance of 1d4).
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack (Thrown Rocks).  Stone giants can cause 3d10 hit 
points in damage by throwing large rocks from between 10 to 
300ft. Likewise, they can catch rocks tossed at them with a 90% 
probability.
Unarmed Combat.  Stone giants cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and do not use such tactics.
GIANT, STORM
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Huge (21ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic good
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 1 [19]
HIT DICE 15 (+1d6  hit points)
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (7d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect invisible 80%
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 125
LOOT Hoard 5, Cache 3 (x10), Cache 5
EXPERIENCE 6,000 +20/hp
There are two typical appearances of storm giants, related to 
their preference for environment. Those who choose to live un-
derwater are often of green hair, eyes and skin, while those who 
reside on land have purple skin, eyes either purple or cloudy 
grey, and blue–tinted black hair.
Storm giants are the most formidable of giants in many cam -
paigns. They live in remote locations; about 60% of the time 
they live on clouds like their cloud giant cousins, and 30% of 
the time in the mountains like their stone giant relatives. Some-
what more rarely (10% of the time) storm giants choose to live 
underwater. In all cases, they live in immense, luxurious castles. 
Like other giants, storm giants have a fondness for pets and if 
living above water will have 1d4 griffons 30% of the time and 1 
or 2 rocs 70% of the time. However, storm giants who have their 
castle abodes under water will instead have 2d4 sea lions.
===== PAGE 172 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 171
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Electricity. Storm giants are immune to all damage by 
electricity, including the fearsome electrical bolt breathed by 
the dreaded blue dragon.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
1/day: lightning bolt  (8d8 damage), call lightning  (10 to 15d6 
damage, 3 bolts), control winds, predict weather, and weather 
summoning.
2/day: levitate their own weight plus an additional weight equal 
to 30,000 gold pieces two times per day.
Underwater Abilities.  Storm giants can breathe and otherwise 
function underwater unimpeded.
Unarmed Combat. Storm giants cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and do not use such tactics.
GNOLL-KIN
FLIND
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d12
SIZE Medium (6.5ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 2+3
MELEE ATTACKS 2d4 or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 55
LOOT Hoard 1
EXPERIENCE 40 +4/hp
Flinds are close relatives of, and to a degree resemble, gnolls. 
Flinds stand 6.5ft tall, with powerful limbs and a leonine head. 
Both flinds and gnolls regard flinds as a majestic, superior form 
of gnoll. Groups of gnolls are often led by one or more flinds, 
and the gnolls will obey any order given by a flind without ques-
tion. A gnoll defending its flind leader will not check for morale 
unless the flind is killed. 
If 20 or more flinds are encountered, there will be a leader type 
with 3+3 hit dice, a strength of 18 (+1 “to hit” and +2 damage). 
Leader type flinds always wield a disarming weapon in combat.
Regarding Player Character Use of Flindish Disarming Bars:  
The wielder must be medium-sized or larger, and possess min -
imum ability scores of 13 in both strength and dexterity. The 
disarm function can only be used by PCs who select the flindbar 
as a weapon of proficiency.
Special Attacks
Strength. Flinds attack with a +1 to-hit bonus due to strength.
Disarm. There is a 25% chance that any particular flind wields a 
disarming weapon, a pair of iron bars linked by a short piece 
of chain. A flind wielding one of these weapons can make two 
attacks per round for 1d4 hp bludgeoning damage. For every 
hit, the victim must save vs aimed magic items or be disarmed.
Unarmed Combat. Flinds can attack and be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks although they seldom use this tac-
tic. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 12+ on 
1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-4. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/
DEF +2/-2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2.
Language
Flinds share a common language with gnolls and can also speak 
bugbear, hobgoblin, ogrish, and orcish. They are generally on 
friendly terms with these humanoid ancestries and will work 
with them as need arises. For unknown reasons, flinds do not 
like, and will not cooperate with, trolls.
GNOLL
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 20d10
SIZE Large (7ft)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 2d4 or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Low to average (5-10)
MORALE 55
LOOT Individual 3, 4 each; in lair Hoard 4, 
Cache 3 (x5), and Cache 5
EXPERIENCE 30 +2/hp
Gnolls resemble tall humans, but they have the head of a hye -
na and are covered in yellowish-grey hair. They live and travel 
in small, roving bands. There are rumours of a gnoll overlord, 
though if one exists its power and reach are limited. In general, 
these creatures are scavengers, raiders, and thieves, their be -
haviour analogous to hyena packs.
Raiding parties of gnolls are led by a leader with 16 hp, fighting 
as a 3 HD monster.
Gnoll bands (when encountered in a lair) are led by a chieftain 
(AC 3 [17], 22 hp, attacking as a 4 HD monster, and inflicting 
2d4+2 damage), and 2d6 guards (AC 4 [16], 20 hp, attacking as a 
3 HD monster, inflicting 2d4+1 damage).
Gnolls have been known to ally with orcs, hobgoblins, bugbears, 
and even trolls.
===== PAGE 173 =====
172 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Unarmed Combat. Gnolls can attack and be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks although they seldom use this tac-
tic. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 12+ on 
1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-2. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/
DEF +2/-2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2.
Language
Gnolls speak troll, their own barked tongue which they share 
with flinds, the chaotic evil alignment tongue, and occasionally 
orcish and hobgoblin.
GNOME
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 40d10
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral good or Lawful good
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS 1d6 or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 50%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 50
LOOT Individual 4 (x3) each; in lair Hoard 3, 
Cache 3 (x20)
EXPERIENCE 10 +1/hp
Gnomes live underground in hills that they have burrowed into, 
seeking gems and precious metals. They are divided into clans 
which are rather competitive with each other, but are never out-
right hostile. They are a distant relative to dwarfs.
For every 40 gnomes encountered there usually is a fighter of 
2nd level or higher levels acting as leader. When the groups are 
of 200 or more there are clerics as well, usually 4th level or high-
er. 
Most of the time the gnomes will have badgers, or other bur -
rowing animals, that they have trained to act as sentries.
Typically when gnomes are encountered they will be wearing 
studded leather armour or ring armour and a shield. Gnomes 
are usually armed with a club and spear, sometimes with a 
short sword and spear.
Immunities and Resistances
Saving Throw. Due to their natural resilience to both magic and 
poison, gnomes save against these effects at four levels higher 
than they actually are. 
Nimbleness. When gnomes battle gnolls, bugbears, ogres, trolls, 
or giants these creatures receive a -4 to their attack rolls due 
to the gnomes’ smaller size and nimbleness.
Special Features
Gnomes are excellent miners, possessing the abilities to detect 
whether a passage slopes upward or downward or if a corridor, 
walls, floors or ceiling is safe or not. They can even estimate 
depth and direction underground.
Special Attacks
Kobolds and Goblins.  When gnomes fight kobolds and goblins 
they gain an additional +1 to hit. 
Unarmed Combat. Gnomes can attack and be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks. For a gnome wearing chain 
mail - if not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 12+ 
on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/+0. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF -2/+2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.
Language
In addition to their own language, gnomes can also speak ko -
bold, goblin, halfling, dwarfish, and can also speak a language 
commonly known to all burrowing animals.
GOBLIN
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 40d10
SIZE Small (4ft)
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 1d8-1  hit points
MELEE ATTACKS 1d6 or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Average (low) (8-10)
MORALE 50
LOOT Individual 2 each; in lair Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 10 +1/hp

===== PAGE 174 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 173
Goblins are small evil creatures with a yellow-to-red or a green-
ish-coloured skin tone. They typically wear leather armour, and 
have an average lifespan of 50 years. They are subterranean, 
and are only found on the surface when raiding or in the very 
darkest of forests. Goblins have a deep hatred of gnomes and 
dwarfs and will attack them on sight.
Goblins are organised into war-bands, with the strongest rul -
ing the war-band. The bands all owe fealty to the goblin king. 
A goblin raiding party will be led by a captain and 4 sergeants, 
each with 7 hp and who will fight as orcs (1 HD monsters).
A goblin war party will be led by a sub-chief and personal guards 
(2d4). They will fight as hobgoblins (each with 8 hp, AC 5 [15], 
and doing 1d8 damage). There is a 1 in 4 chance that any war 
party will be mounted on worgs.
A goblin lair will be led by a chief and personal guards (2d4). 
They will fight as gnolls (each with 12 hp, AC 4 [15], doing 2d4 
damage). Worgs are often used as guards of goblin lairs, and 
sometimes a small band of bugbears will be found there as well.
Goblins are good cavers and have a chance (25%) to note new 
or unusual construction.
Immunities and Resistances
Vulnerability to Light.  Goblins are subterranean creatures that 
detest direct sunlight, and will fight at -1 if exposed to it.
Unarmed Combat. Goblins can attack and be targeted with grap-
pling or overbearing attacks although they seldom use this tac-
tic. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 10+ on 
1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-2. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/
DEF -2/+2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.
Language
Goblins speak kobold, orcish, hobgoblin, lawful evil, and their 
own language.
GOLEM
A golem is a magically created monster. Of the four basic types, 
three are created with earthen materials, while the flesh go -
lem is created from human body parts. The creation process 
requires powerful spells, rare components, and eldritch forces.
The golems listed below are typically humanoid in shape.
A golem has hit dice equal to its hit points divided by 4.5 round-
ed up; thus, for example, a stone golem has 14 hit dice (60 
hp/4.5).
Magical creatures may strike a golem with effect if the crea -
ture’s hit dice equal or exceed that of the golem.
Unarmed Combat. Golems cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and do not use such tactics.

CLAY GOLEM
A clay golem is a construct created through the following proce-
dure: a lawful good cleric of at least 17th level (or a lower level 
cleric of same alignment using a magical tome written for such 
a process) creates a man–shaped figure out of clay and then un-
dertakes an uninterrupted ritual involving material components 
worth no less than 20,000 gp, vestments and ritual items worth 
another 30,000 gp and the spells resurrection, animate object , 
commune, prayer and bless.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Non-Blunt and Mundane Weapons.  This creature is 
immune to piercing and slashing damage from all weapons 
(magical or not), and from blunt damage from non-magical 
weapons.
Unaffected by Most Spells. Clay golems are not affected by spells 
other than move earth (6d6 damage), disintegrate (1d12 dam -
age and slows the golem permanently as per the spell) and 
earthquake (5d10 damage).
===== PAGE 175 =====
174 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Special Features
Loss of Control. A clay golem is under the direct command of its 
creator. It stands a 1% cumulative chance of possession by a 
spirit of chaotic evil alignment for each round the golem is in 
combat. The creator can never regain control if the golem is 
possessed. Once possessed, the golem will kill any nearby liv -
ing thing starting with the closest creature and will haste itself 
if it has not already done so.
Healing-Resistant Damage. Damage inflicted by a clay golem can 
only be healed by a cleric of 17th level or greater, although it 
will heal naturally at the normal rate.
Special Attacks
The clay golem attacks as an 11 hit dice monster.
Haste. Once per day, provided it has been in combat for one 
or more rounds, a clay golem can act as if hasted (duration: 3 
combat rounds). This allows the golem to move and attack at 
twice normal speed. A golem that loses control will immediate-
ly haste itself if possible.
FLESH GOLEM
Flesh golems are constructs built from human body parts … 
basically, Frankenstein’s monster. A flesh golem follows simple 
commands by its creator, and can be ordered (if serving as a 
guard) to wait until a specific event takes place before undertak-
ing a series of commands. 
A flesh golem is extremely strong and can break down doors 
and other wooden structures. 
A flesh golem is created with a magical tome written for such 
a process or by a magic-user (of at least 14th level) using the 
following spells: wish, polymorph object, geas, strength, and pro-
tection from normal missiles. One thousand gold pieces per hit 
point of the golem must be spent on material components, and 
the entire creation process takes one month.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Immune to Most Spells. Cold- and fire-based spells do not dam -
age the golem, but slow it to 50% speed for 2d6 combat rounds. 
Electrical attacks heal damage the golem has sustained, at a 
rate of 1 hit point restored per die of damage that would have 
been inflicted (example: an 8 hit dice lightning bolt will restore 8 
hit points of damage). Other spells have no effect at all.
Special Features
Loss of Control. For each melee round spent in combat, the flesh 
golem stands a 1% cumulative chance of going berserk and 
attacking all targets in sight. The golem’s creator can attempt 
to reassert control at a chance of 10% per round (non-cumu -
lative).
IRON GOLEM
An iron golem is created through use of a magical tome or by an 
18th level or higher magic-user using the following spells: cloud 
kill, wish, geas, and polymorph object . Creation time is three 
months and material cost is 1,000 gp per hit point of the golem. 
The magic-user can give the construct simple commands, and 
it can be ordered (if serving as a guard) to wait until a specific 
event takes place before undertaking a series of commands.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Mundane, +1, and +2 Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +3 or more.
Immune to Most Spells. Only electrical magical attacks can affect 
it; such spells will slow the golem to one-half speed for 3 com-
bat rounds. Fire attacks repair damage to the golem at a rate 
of one hit point per die of spell damage (example: a 6 hit die 
fireball repairs 6 hp of damage). Other spells have no effect at 
all.
Special Attacks
Breath Weapon. In addition to its normal attacks, once every 7 
combat rounds an iron golem may breathe poison gas directly 
before it in a cloud of 10 cubicft. Anyone in the cloud must 
make a saving throw vs. poison or die.
STONE GOLEM
A stone golem is created through use of a magical text or by a 
16th or higher level magic-user using the following spells: geas, 
slow, wish and polymorph object. Creation time is two months 
and cost is 1,000 gp per hit point of the golem. The magic-user 
can give the construct simple commands, and it can be ordered 
(if serving as a guard) to wait until a specific event takes place 
before undertaking a series of commands. 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Mundane and +1 Weapons.  This creature is immune 
to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and un-
armed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a 
magical bonus of +2 or more.
Immune to Most Spells. A stone golem is invulnerable to most 
magic. The only exceptions are rock to mud  (halves the go -
lem’s attack and movement speed for 2d6 rounds), stone to 
flesh (makes the golem susceptible to normal weapons for one 
round), and mud to rock (acts as a heal spell on the monster).
Special Features
Slow. A stone golem can cast a slow spell every other melee 
round on all opponents within 10ft of its front side.
===== PAGE 176 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 175
GORGON
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 8
MELEE ATTACKS 1 gore (2d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 85
LOOT Hoard 5
EXPERIENCE 1,500 +10/hp
Gorgons are magical creatures resembling giant bulls, but with 
thick metal scales instead of hide.
Special Attacks
Petrifying Breath (3/day). Gorgons can breathe a cloud of petrify-
ing gas in a cone 60ft long and 10ft in radius at the base up to 
3 times per day. Anyone in the cloud must make a saving throw 
vs. petrifaction or be turned to stone. 
Unarmed Combat. Gorgons cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and do not use such tactics.
GREY OOZE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3
SIZE Medium to Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 10ft
ARMOUR CLASS 8 [12]
HIT DICE 3+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 pseudopod (2d8)
SENSES See below
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 75 +3/hp
Grey ooze is indistinguishable from wet stone until it attacks. It 
will form tentacles that lash out at any who pass. Grey ooze also 
takes full damage from normal weapons, but if the weapon is 
made of metal it too will begin to dissolve.
As it matures grey ooze gets larger. Specimens over 20 HP can 
be as large as 36 square feet. While they cover a huge area, they 
are never more than a few inches deep. At larger sizes they be-
gin to form a kind of consciousness.
Immunities and Resistances
Ooze Senses. This creature cannot be knocked prone, and is not 
affected by lack of light, invisible opponents, gases, or other 
circumstances that would not affect an ooze.
Immunities. Grey ooze is not affected by fire or cold damage.
Special Features
Corrosive Properties.  Any metal weapon that hits a grey ooze 
must make a saving throw (target number: 7) or be destroyed 
by the ooze’s acidic properties. Magical weapons have a bonus 
to the saving throw of one higher than the weapon’s to-hit bo-
nus (see PG Section 6.4, Item Saving Throws).
Special Attacks
Corrosive Pseudopods. Grey ooze is corrosive. If the ooze hits an 
opponent wearing metal armour, the armour will come apart 
and become useless unless it makes a saving throw vs. acid 
(target number: 7). The acid will eat through chain mail in 1 
round, and requires 2 rounds to eat through plate. See Play -
er Guide Section 1.6.4, Item Saving Throws. The acid does not 
damage wood, leather, or stone.
Unarmed Combat. Grey oozes cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, and do not use such tactics.

===== PAGE 177 =====
176 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
GRIFFON
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d6
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft; 300ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 7
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d4) and 1 bite (2d8)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 80
LOOT Hoard 3, Cache 5
EXPERIENCE 225 +8/hp
Griffons build nests on cliff faces and rocky outcrops. Where 
possible they even have been known to build nests inside shal -
low caves. Horses are their favourite meal and being the fear -
some hunters they are, griffons will usually attack their prey on 
sight.
Hatchlings are particularly valuable as they can be trained as 
mounts. This takes time, effort, and money. The training takes 
years and must begin early in a griffon’s life. Once the griffon 
reaches maturity there is no chance of training it.
When ridden by a creature weighing more than 100 lbs, a grif -
fon’s aerial agility decreases to III.
Unarmed Combat. Griffons cannot be attacked or targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks although they may occasion -
ally overbear as the end of an aerial attack. If not fended off, 
they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/
DEF +10/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +3/na. Grappling 
Result Mods: ATK/DEF na/na.
GRIMLOCK
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 20d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 1d6 or by weapon
SENSES Special
LAIR CHANCE 75%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 55
LOOT Individual 2, 3, 4 each; in lair 
Horde 2
EXPERIENCE 28 +2/hp
Grimlocks are a fierce species of humanoid that dwells in the 
dark and hidden places of the earth, periodically issuing forth to 
the surface in search of prey, preferably human. Whilst some -
what resembling men in shape, they have a thick, almost scaly, 
hide that is a dusky grey in colour, a head of wild and matted 
black hair, a mouthful of vicious pointed teeth and two white 
featureless orbs for eyes. (The original inspiration for this mon-
ster was probably the morlocks).
Though it is unknown whether grimlocks speak a language, it 
is certain that they are entirely blind. They instead rely on their 
other senses, which are highly developed and allow them to 
perceive the environment around them within a 20ft radius 
with astounding accuracy. 
Grimlocks show very little grasp of tactics or strategy, prefer -
ring to attack directly and all at once. However, they do seem 
to prefer to fight under cover of darkness, which often yields to 
them a distinct advantage. Although they are capable of inflict -
ing 1d6 damage with their filthy claws, most prefer to use axes 
or swords. 
For every 30 grimlocks encountered there will be one with 3 HD 
and AC 4 [16]; for every 40 grimlocks encountered there will be 
one with 4 HD and AC 3 [17]. These act as leaders, being generally 
more ferocious than their fellows and even capable of developing 
and employing elementary stratagems. For every one of these 
leaders present, their morale is increased by 5%. It is rare for 
them to be found in conjunction with any other monsters.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Grimlocks surprise on a die roll of 1-3 on a d6 if the 
encounter is underground. 
Immunities and Resistances
Native Blindness. Grimlocks are immune to the effects of bright 
light or other visual stimuli that might otherwise adversely af -
fect them, including magical effects, such as invisibility, dark -
ness and any visual illusions. However, grimlocks are also 
known to be particularly vulnerable to effects that obscure 
their other senses, such as overpowering scents or overloud 
noises that have a significant duration. When exposed to such 
hindrances, their perception is reduced to a 10ft radius and 
they suffer a -2 penalty to hit.
Improved Saving Throw. Regardless of hit dice, grimlocks always 
make saving throws as 6th level fighters.
Unarmed Combat. Grimlocks can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
===== PAGE 178 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 177
H.
HALFLING
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 30d10
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Lawful good
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1d6 hp
MELEE ATTACKS 1d6 or by weapon
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 70%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 50
LOOT Individual 2 each; in lair Hoard 
2
EXPERIENCE 5 +1/hp
Halflings as an ancestry value hard work and simple living. Their 
settlements are as likely to contain small cottages as they are to 
have underground dwellings. They tend to keep to themselves.
When encountered in groups there will likely be a leader, usu -
ally a 2nd level fighter, for every 30 individuals. In groups con -
taining over 90 individuals there will likely be a 3rd level fighter 
as well. In groups containing more than 150 halflings there will 
usually be a 4th level fighter in addition to the 3rd and 2nd level 
fighters. There is a 10% chance per level that higher level hal-
flings may have magic armour and/or weapons.
If the halflings are encountered in their homes (lair) they will 
have 1d4 dogs acting as guards.
Their lifespan averages about 150 years.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Halflings are very quiet 
and unobtrusive, surprising 
enemies on a roll of 1–4 on 
a d6.
Immunities and 
Resistances
Magic and Poison Save.  
Halfling NPCs make saving 
throws with a +4 against magic 
and poison.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack Bonus. Halflings are +3 “to hit” with bow or sling, 
and almost all halflings are equipped with some sort of missile 
weapon.
Unarmed Combat. Halflings can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with an 
8+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-4. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF -2/+2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.
Language
Halflings speak their own language and will also be able to 
speak common, gnome, goblin, and orc.
HARPY
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d6
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 60ft; 150ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d3) and 1 club (1d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 60
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 50 +3/hp
These creatures are part vulture (wings and lower body) and 
part woman (head and torso). Harpies use their charm ability to 
bind prey, after which the harpies will torture, kill, and consume 
the victims. In combat a harpy attacks with her clawed feet and, 
usually, some kind of club as a melee weapon. 
Special Features
Singing. Anyone hearing the song of a harpy must make a saving 
throw vs spells or move toward the source of the song.
Special Attacks
Charm. If the harpy hits with either of its claw attacks, the victim 
must make a saving throw vs. spells or be charmed (as per the 
magic-user spell).
Unarmed Combat. Harpies cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and do not use such tactics.
Language
Most harpies speak only their own language and the alignment 
tongue of chaotic evil.

===== PAGE 179 =====
178 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
HELL HOUND
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 4 to 7
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d10)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 70
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE
4 HD: 75 +4/hp 
5 HD: 110 +5/hp 
6 HD: 160 +6/hp 
7 HD: 225 +8/hp
Hell hounds are not native to the material plane; they are 
brought here by others to serve as guard dogs. These fiendish 
dogs are dark reddish in colour with glowing red eyes and black 
mouths.
Surprise and Initiative
Stealthy Surprise. Because hell hounds can move so quietly, they 
surprise on a roll of 1–4 on 1d6.
Keen Senses. Hell hounds can only be surprised on a roll of 1 due 
to their own sharp senses. 
Immunities and Resistances
Eyes of the Underworld. The vision of Hell Hounds is unnaturally 
sharp: 50% of the time they can spot hidden or invisible crea -
tures.
Special Attacks
Breathe Fire.  Hell hounds breath fire up to a 10ft range. Any -
one caught in the area (roughly a line 10ft long and 5-6ft wide) 
must make a saving throw vs. breath weapons or take fire 
damage equal to the hound’s hit dice. If the saving throw suc -
ceeds, the victim takes only half damage. Example: a 6 hit dice 
hell hound breathes fire for 6hp damage, unless the opponent 
saves vs breath weapons and thus takes only 3 hit points. The 
hell hound can attack and breathe fire in the same round.
Unarmed Combat.  Hell hounds cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, but they may overbear. If not 
fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit 
Mods: ATK/DEF +8/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +3/na. 
Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF na/na.
HIPPOGRIFF
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d8
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 180ft; 360ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 3+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1d6/1d6/1d10
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 65
LOOT Cache 3 (x5)
EXPERIENCE 150 +3/hp
Hippogriffs have the body of a horse, but the head, wings, and 
foreclaws of an eagle. They are only found in remote locations, 
far from civilisation, nesting on rocky outcrops. Hippogriffs do 
not mix well other winged beasts, and will try to eat pegasi. If 
encountered near its nest, a hippogriff will fight ferociously to 
defend itself and its young. Eggs and hatchlings bring a high 
price from those wishing to train them as mounts.
When ridden by a creature weighing more than 100 lbs, a hip -
pogriff’s aerial agility rating decreases to III.
Unarmed Combat. Hippogriffs cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, but they may overbear. If not fended 
off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: 
ATK/DEF +10/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +4/na. 
Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF na/na.
HOBGOBLIN
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 20d10
SIZE Medium (6.5ft)
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 1+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1d8 or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 50
LOOT Individual 1 and 4 each; in lair, 
Hoard 4 and Cache 3 (x5)
EXPERIENCE 20 +2/hp
Hobgoblins are bigger cousins of goblins, and form their own 
society. In general, they have red or orange skin, and are hair -
ier than normal goblins. Their organisation is usually into war-
bands, which may fight each other over territory if they are not 
===== PAGE 180 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 179
restrained by strong leadership. Although hobgoblins are not 
affected by sunlight as goblins are, they are still essentially a 
subterranean species. They typically lair underground, and will 
sometimes (50%) keep carnivorous apes as guards.
Hobgoblin raiding parties are led by a sergeant and 2 assis -
tants, each with 9 hp.
Hobgoblin war parties are led by a captain (AC 3 [17], 16 hp, 
fights as a 3 HD monster, doing 1d8+2 damage).
In their lair, hobgoblins are led by a chief (AC 2 [18], 22 hp, fights 
as a 4 HD monster, doing 1d10+1 damage) and 5d4 personal 
guards (AC 3 [17], 16 hp, fights as a 3 HD monster, doing 1d8+2 
damage).
Hobgoblins hate elves, and will attack them on sight.
Special Features
Hobgoblins are very skilled miners and cavers, and can detect 
new construction, sloping passages and shifting walls with a 
40% chance.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack: One in five hobgoblins will carry a bow or other 
ranged weapon as well as a sword or spear.
Unarmed Combat. Hobgoblins can make grappling or overbear-
ing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
12+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-2. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
Language
Hobgoblins speak goblin, orcish, their own brutish tongue and 
the very limited language of carnivorous apes. Some can speak 
common as well.
HOMUNCULUS
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Tiny (1.5ft)
ALIGNMENT Any
MOVE 60ft; 180ft flying (AA:V)
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d3 + sleep)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE By creator
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 81 +2/hp
A homunculus is a small construct that is vaguely humanoid in 
form, stands about 18 inches tall, and has bat-like wings with a 
24-in wingspan. homunculi have greenish reptilian skin, a bat-
like head and ears, and a mouth full of needle-sharp teeth. 
Its alignment is the same as its creator. The homunculus is com-
pletely under control of its creator, who need not concentrate 
on control to maintain it. This means the homunculus can be 
given a specific goal, which it will then plan to achieve, carrying 
out its orders without further attention from its creator.
Homunculi are created in a process involving both the spell 
caster and the services of an alchemist. The alchemist will re -
quire 1d4×500 (500–2,000) gold pieces, a pint of the magic-us -
er’s blood, and 1d4 weeks to prepare the base mixture of fluids 
which will form the creature. Within 24 hours of the alchemist 
completing the mixture process the magic-user must cast the 
following spells upon the fluid, in order: mending, mirror image, 
and wizard eye. The spells must be cast by the person who gave 
the blood for the basic mixture (though scrolls may be used). 
Failure to cast the spells within 24 hours or casting the required 
spells out of order ruins that mixture and the whole process 
and all costs must be repeated. Upon completion of the wizard 
eye spell, the fluid coalesces into a ready-to-use homunculus.
Homunculi make excellent messengers, scouts, or spies. Since 
they are constructs and not natural creatures, they are never 
encountered in the wild.
Immunities and Resistances
Per Creator. A homunculus is a reflection of its magic-user cre -
ator, and therefore rolls saves at whatever level its owner 
makes their saving throws. Magical defences in effect upon 
the owner, resist fire or bless for example, also protect the ho -
munculus.
Special Features
Climber. This creature can move on walls and ceilings as if they 
were floors.
Connection to Creator. Killing a homunculus deals an immediate 
2d10 points of damage to its master. If the owner of the ho -
munculus dies, the homunculus immediately disappears in a 
puff of smoke.
Special Attacks
Soporific Bite. The bite of a homunculus is venomous and causes 
a comatose state for 1d6×5 (5–30) minutes unless the victim 
saves vs magic to negate.
Unarmed Combat. Homunculi can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, although they do not use these tactics. If 
not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. 
Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF 
-4/+4. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF -3/+3.
Language
A homunculus cannot speak, but knows what its creator knows, 
and can communicate what it sees and hears to its creator via 
a limited form of telepathy up to 500ft away. A homunculus will 
never willingly exceed the maximum communication range.

One of the most common domesticated animals, horses are 
almost ubiquitous in most human societies. They are insuffi -
ciently sure-footed to take into most dungeon environments 
(this requires a mule). “Heavy”, “Medium” and “Light” refer to 
warhorses. Only about 10% of horses can be trained as war -
horses, even by an expert, and warhorse training requires very 
specialised knowledge on the part of the trainer.
Unarmed Combat. Horses cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and do not use such tactics. A warhorse 
might be trained to overbear.
HIPPOCAMPUS
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d4
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Chaotic good
MOVE 240ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d4 piercing)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 65
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 60 +4/hp
The hippocampus (plural “hippocampi”) is a mer-horse with the 
forequarters of a horse and a fish-like tail instead of rear legs. 
They are used as steeds by some underwater species, most no-
tably the tritons. Hippocampi are entirely aquatic, and cannot 
venture out of the water.
HIPPOPOTAMUS
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d6
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 90ft; 120ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 8
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (3d6 or 2d6 piercing)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 85
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 600 +12/hp
Hippopotami, or “hippos”, are large semi-aquatic mammals 
that spend most of their time in the water. They are herbivores 
but they are quite aggressive in defending their territory and 
their great size makes them dangerous opponents. This is com-
pounded by the fact that, in spite of the bulky appearance, they 
can run faster than a human on dry land. Hippos can’t float, 
though they can propel themselves to the surface of the water 
for a breath of air, and they can stay submerged for 15 minutes 
at a time.
A herd will have one bull for every four hippos present. In com-
bat, a bull bites for 3d6 damage and cows bite for 2d6. A boat 
passing over a hippo has a 50% of having the animal surface 
under the craft, tipping it over.
Unarmed Combat. Hippopotami cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and do not use such tactics.
===== PAGE 182 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 181
HUMANS
Normal humans (non-adventuring types) will usually have 1d6 
hit points. In any encounter with groups of humans, there will 
always be higher level characters as leaders (the number will be 
given under each entry).
It’s worth noting that these entries are also groups that aren’t 
unique to humankind. A caravan of dwarf merchants or elven 
mounted nomads are absolutely possible, and any caravan 
might be accompanied by a dwarf or elf. You, as the GM, can de-
cide to modify any randomly occurring results on the encounter 
tables to reflect the campaign.
All higher level clerics and fighters will be mounted on medi -
um warhorses. For each level a leader type has, there is a 5% 
chance of possessing a magic item in each of the categories be-
low. For each “Y” in the following table, roll for an item of that 
type. If the result is undesirable, one re-roll is allowed.
 FIGHTER MAGIC-USER CLERIC THIEF

Shield Y N Y N

Misc. Weapon Y N Y* Y

Scroll N Y Y N

Wand/Staff/
Rod N Y N* N
Misc. Magic N Y Y Y
*If no weapon (or an edged weapon is rolled), roll again on Wand/
Staff/Rod (if not usable by a cleric then there is none present).
BANDIT (BRIGAND)
Base number encountered: 20d10

Bandits are found in almost any locale, for wherever there is a 
civilisation there will be those who prey upon the weak. There 
will always be higher level fighters found with large groups of 
bandits. Consult the chart below to determine the exact num -
ber and level. Bandits are generally of neutral alignment, and 
brigands are generally chaotic evil.
The base number of bandits in a lair is 20d10. Half of these will 
be footmen, 20% will be archers, 20% will be light cavalry, and 
10% will be medium cavalry, as shown in the stats.
Leaders are in addition to the base number:
• For every 20 bandits: 1 additional 3rd level fighter 
• For every 30 bandits: 1 additional 4th level fighter 
• For every 40 bandits: 1 additional 5th level fighter 
• For every 50 bandits: 1 additional 6th level fighter
===== PAGE 183 =====
182 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Commander. Bandits will always be led by a high level fighter (8th 
to 10th level), 6 guards (2nd level), and a lieutenant (7th level). 
These members are in addition to those described above. The 
level of the commander is determined as follows:
• Under 100 bandits: 8th level fighter
• 100 to 150 bandits: 9th level fighter
• Over 150 bandits: 10th level fighter
Other Leaders
• There is a 25% chance per 50 bandits of a magic-user being 
present with the group. If there is a magic-user present they 
will be 7th to 10th level (determined by 1d4 roll).
• There is a 15% chance per 50 bandits of a cleric being present 
with the group, if a cleric is present they will be 5th or 6th 
level and they will be accompanied by an assistant of 3rd or 
4th level.
Brigand: Brigands are a chaotic evil lot; otherwise they conform 
to the rules for bandits above. However they fight more fiercely 
in battle, and have higher morale (the number in parentheses 
is for brigands).
Unarmed Combat. Bandits can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with an 
8+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-6. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
BERSERKER
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 12ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1d6+1 hp
MELEE ATTACKS By weapon
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10) to very
MORALE N/A
LOOT Individual 2 each; in lair Hoard 
2
EXPERIENCE 10 +1/hp
Berserkers are roaming bands of fighters. They are always un -
armoured, as they see it as a sign of weakness (but have an 
improved armour class). 
There will always be higher level fighters found with large 
groups of berserkers. Consult the chart below to determine the 
exact number and level.
• For every 10 berserkers: 1 additional 1st level fighter
• For every 20 berserkers: 1 additional 2nd level fighter
• For every 30 berserkers: 1 additional 3rd level fighter
• For every 40 berserkers: 1 additional 4th level fighter
• For every 50 berserkers: 1 additional 5th level fighter
Berserkers will always be led by a war chief of 9th or 10th level 
and a sub-chief of 6th or 7th level, as follows.
• If less than 60: 9th level chief & 6th level sub-chief
• If more than 60: 10th level chief & 7th level sub-chief
Other Leaders: There is a 50% chance per every 10 berserkers, 
that a cleric will be present. The cleric will be of 7th level and will 
have 1d4 assistant clerics of 3rd or 4th level.
Special Attacks
Frenzy. Berserkers fight with a fierce battle-lust, and as a result 
they may either strike twice in a round or make one attack at +2.
Unarmed Combat. Berserkers can make grappling or overbear -
ing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with an 
2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8 (with additional +2 on at -
tack). Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
BUCCANEER (PIRATE)
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 50d6
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral (Chaotic evil)
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS By  armour type
HIT DICE 1d6 hp

INTELLIGENCE Mean (average to very) (8-12)
MORALE 50
LOOT Individual 2 each; in lair, 
Cache 9
EXPERIENCE Varies
One out of every 3 buccaneers (or pirates) is armed with a light 
crossbow as well as an axe or sword.
Buccaneers are found on all large bodies of water, their lair is 
most always the ship they use for raiding. They are always led 
by high level fighters as follows:
• For every 50 buccaneers: 1 additional 3rd level fighter
• For every 100 buccaneers: 1 additional 5th level fighter
===== PAGE 184 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 183
The overall captain will be a fighter of 8th to 10th level as fol -
lows:
• If there are less than 200 buccaneers: 8th level fighter with a 
lieutenant of 6th level
• If there are 200 buccaneers or more: 10th level fighter with a 
lieutenant of 7th level.
The captain will also have 4 mates (4th level fighters) in addition 
to a lieutenant.
There is a 15% chance of a cleric being present per every 50 buc-
caneers. The cleric will be between 12th and 15th level (1d4+11).
There is a 10% chance of a magic-user being present for every 
50 buccaneers; the magic-user will be between 6th and 8th level 
(1d3+5).
Pirate: Pirates are chaotic evil, but otherwise conform to buc -
caneers above.
CAVEMAN
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 8 [12]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 1 club or spear (1d6)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 40
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE 20 +2/hp
Cavemen are humans living beyond the reach of normal civilisa-
tion, with stone-age technology. Hunting parties of cavemen (10 
or more) are led by a 3rd level fighter. In their lair, in addition 
to the number of cavemen determined there will be a chief (5th 
level fighter) and 1d4 guards (4th level fighters). There is always 
a 10% chance (per every 10 cavemen) that a 3rd level cleric will 
be present.
Cavemen typically dwell in caves and cavern systems. Their trea-
sure will consist of ivory tusks, gold nuggets, or uncut gems (5% 
chance per 10 cavemen present). If one is present, it will be the 
only treasure there. The value of each is 1,000 gp per ivory tusk 
(2d6 tusks), 5 gp gold nuggets (2d4×10), and 10 gp gems (1d100).
Special Attacks
Strength Bonus. Cavemen add +1 to damage rolls due to their 
great strength.
Unarmed Combat. Cavemen can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with an 
6+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-6. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
TRIBAL WARRIORS
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d12
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 sword (1d8) or spear (1d6)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 50
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE  10 +1/hp
Tribal warriors are usually at a bronze-age level of technology, 
although they may have steel weapons obtained from else -
where. This entry allows for anything from Roman-era German-
ic tribes to isolated island settlements, to post-catastrophic ar -
eas that have lost even a feudal level of civilisation.
In their lair, there will be a chief (5th level fighter) and 1d4 guards 
(4th level fighters). There is always a 10% chance (per every 10 
tribal warriors) that a 3rd level cleric will be present.
Additional Leaders.
• One 4th level fighter per 10
• One 6th level fighter per 30
• One 8th level cleric/druid.
They are usually armed with spears, shields, short bows, and 
short swords (longswords, if there is better access to steel, or 
spears if metal is scarce in the area).
Their treasure will consist of furs, gold nuggets, or uncut gems 
(5% chance per 10 tribal warriors present). If one is present, it 
will be the only treasure there. The value of each is 1,000 gp per 
bale of furs (2d6), 5 gp gold nuggets (2d4×10), and 10 gp gems 
(1d100).
Unarmed Combat. Tribal warriors can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with an 8+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-10. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+0/+0.

MYSTIC NOMAD
FREQUENCY Rare (Uncommon)
NO. ENCOUNTERED 30d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Any lawful
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS Usually 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1d6 hp
MELEE ATTACKS By weapon
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Individual 1 each; in lair Cache 12
EXPERIENCE Varies
Previous editions used the term “dervish”, which isn’t accurate 
to the broader concept.
Mystic nomads are highly-religious warrior-bands, of any lawful 
alignment. They will always be led by higher level fighters and 
clerics as follows. 
Fighters
• For every 30 mystic nomads: 1 additional 3rd level fighter
• For every 40 mystic nomads: 1 additional 4th level fighter
• For every 50 mystic nomads: 1 additional 5th level fighter
Clerics
• If under 125 mystic nomads: (1) 10th level cleric and 2 assis -
tants (4th & 5th level  clerics)
• If under 250 mystic nomads: (1) 11th level cleric and 2 assis -
tants (6th & 7th level  clerics)
• If more than 250: (1) 12th level cleric and 2 assistants (both 
8th level  clerics)
There is a 15% chance per 50 mystic nomads that a magic-us -
er will be present. The magic-user will be of 7th or 8th level (if 
more than 200 mystic nomads are present) with two assistants 
of 3rd or 4th level.
Mystic nomads are always mounted on light or medium war -
horses.
Special Attacks
Mystic nomads are disciplined and fearless, attacking with +1 
on to-hit and damage rolls.
Unarmed Combat. Mystic nomads can make grappling or over -
bearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with an 8+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-6. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
MOUNTED NOMADS
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 30d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1d6 hp
MELEE ATTACKS By weapon
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 15%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 50
LOOT Individual 3 each; in lair Cache 12
EXPERIENCE Varies
Nomads are mounted clans that live on steppes, plains or in 
desert climes. They live by hunting and gathering and are close-
ly tied to their native climate. Nomads will always be led by high-
er level fighters as follows:
• If under 150 nomads: (1) 8th level fighter, (1) 6th level fighter, 
and 12 guards (2nd level fighters)
• Between 150–250 nomads: (1) 9th level fighter, (1) 7th level 
fighter, and guards (2nd level fighters)
• Over 250 nomads: (1) 10th level fighter, (1) 8th level fighter, 
and guards (2nd level fighters)
There is a 15% chance per 50 nomads that a cleric will be pres -
ent. The cleric will be of 4th to 7th level (determined by 1d4). 
There is also a 15% chance per 50 nomads that a magic-user (of 
5th to 8th level, also determined by 1d4) will be present. 
In addition to the above, there will always be (2) 3rd level clerics 
and (1) 4th level magic-user present.
Nomads will always be mounted on either light or medium war-
horses.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Mounted nomads surprise on 1–4 on a d6.
Special Attacks
Unarmed Combat.  Mounted nomads can make grappling or 
overbearing attacks but only if unmounted. If not fended off, 
they score an unarmed hit with an 8+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/
DEF +8/-6. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling 
Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
===== PAGE 186 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 185
PILGRIM
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT See below
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 8 [12]
HIT DICE 1d6 hp
MELEE ATTACKS Staff (1d6)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 100%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12) to High (13-14)
MORALE 50
LOOT Individual 1 each
EXPERIENCE 5 +1/hp
Pilgrims are groups of religious followers making their way to 
some holy site. They can be encountered almost anywhere. 
There will always be higher level character types with groups 
of pilgrims: these higher-level types are the actual threat and 
carry the lion’s share of the treasure. The “pilgrim” described in 
the stats above is almost a noncombatant, but the higher-level 
defenders can make attacking a pilgrimage quite dangerous!
Clerics: There will always be clerics with pilgrims, but if the 
group is neutral these clerics will be druids.
• 1d6 2nd level  clerics
• 1d4 4th level  clerics
• 1d2 6th level  clerics
• 1 8th level cleric with 2 assistants (3rd and 5th level)
Fighters: There is a 10% chance per every 10 pilgrims that there 
will be 1d10 fighters present (roll 1d8 to determine the levels of 
the fighters). If the group is lawful good, some of these fighters 
may be replaced by paladins. If the group is chaotic good, some 
of these fighters may be replaced by rangers.
Thieves: There is a 10% chance per every 10 pilgrim that there 
will be 1d6 thieves present (roll 1d6+1 to determine the levels 
of the thieves). If the group is evil, some of these thieves may be 
replaced by assassins.
Magic-Users: There is a 5% chance per every 10 pilgrims that 
there will be a magic-user present (level 1d4+5).
Alignment is determined for the entire group (with little vari -
ation) by rolling d100 with the likelihood of each alignment as 
follows:
D100 ALIGNMENT
01–34 Lawful good
35–55 Chaotic good
56–66 Neutral
65–80 Chaotic evil
81–00 Lawful evil
Most of the time (75%) pilgrims will be travel without mounts. 
However if they are mounted, all members of the group will be 
mounted.
There is a chance (5%) that a holy item will be with the pilgrims. 
If so, it will be hidden and carefully guarded.
Unarmed Combat. Pilgrims can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with an 
6+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-6. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.

Base number encountered: 1d6x50
