186 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Merchant encounters are actually caravans of merchants, drov-
ers, and guards. The caravan will also contain pack animals, 
carts, and horses. 
The guards will always be led by a higher-level fighter of 6th to 
11th level (1d6+5), a lieutenant (1 level lower than the captain), 
and 12 guards (2nd level fighters).
• There is a 10% chance per 50 persons that a magic-user of 6th 
to 8th level is present (1d3+5). 
• There is a 5% chance per 50 persons that a cleric of 5th to 7th 
level (1d3+4) is present. 
• There is also a 15% chance per 50 persons that a thief of 
8th to 10th level will be present (along with 1d4 lower level 
thieves of 3rd to 7th level). 
All of the above leader or special character types are in addition 
to the number of merchants generated. 
Treasure: The treasure for a caravan will be as follows:
• Individual Merchants: 3d12 cp, 3d6 sp, 2d6 ep, 2d4 gp, 1d6 pp, 
2d4 gp (40%), 1d6x10 pp (50%), 4d8  gems (55%), 1d12 pieces 
of jewellery (45%).
• Individual  Mercenaries: 3d6 sp. Individual Leaders: 2d4 gp.
• Mercenary Pay Box (Hidden in Caravan):  2,000–4,000 gold 
(1d3+1), 100–400 pp (1d4), and 4d4  gems).
• Caravan Goods: The caravan will be carrying goods and mer -
chandise worth 10,000 to 60,000 gp. This requires either 10 
pack animals or 1 cart per 5,000 gp worth of goods.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack (Archers). Archers are armed with longbows (1d6 
piercing).
Unarmed Combat. Footmen can make grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
12+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-6. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
HYDRA
If not indicated by an encounter table, roll 1d8+4 to determine the number of heads.
5-6 HEADED 7-10 HEADED 11-12 HEADED
FREQUENCY Uncommon Uncommon Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1 1 1
SIZE Large Large Huge
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 90ft 90ft 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15] 5 [15] 5 [15]
HIT DICE 5 or 6 7-10 11-12
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite per head (1d6) 1 bite per head (1d8) 1 bite per head (1d10)
SENSES Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20% 20% 20%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4) Semi- (2-4) Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 70 or 75 80, 85, 90, or 95 100 or 105
LOOT Hoard 2 Hoard 2 Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE See below See below See below
A hydra is a multi-headed reptilian monster that lives in damp, 
dark places like swamps, bogs, or marshes. Sometimes they are 
found underground if the lair is sufficiently dank and wet.
Hydras have reptilian bodies, four legs, a tail and 1d8+4 heads. 
Each head counts as 1 hit die and has 8 full hit points, so a 10 
headed hydra will have 10 hit dice and 80 hp. Each head can 
attack independently or up to four heads can attack a single tar-
get. The damage a hydra does is based on the number of heads. 
The colouration of a hydra ranges from light brown to an almost 
blackish brown with a yellow or tan underbelly. Their eyes range 
from yellow to orange.
Experience point value:
5 heads: 165 +5/hp
6 heads: 250 +6/hp
7 heads: 400 +8/hp
8 heads: 650 +10/hp
9 heads: 1000 +12/hp
10 heads: 1500 +14/hp
11 heads: 2150 +16/hp
12 heads: 2850 +16/hp
Unarmed Combat. Hydrae cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.

 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 187
HYENA
NORMAL BIG GIANT
FREQUENCY Common Rare Very rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 2d6 2d6 1d6
SIZE Medium Medium Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 120ft 120ft 180ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13] 6 [14] 5 [15]
HIT DICE 2 3 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (2d4) 1 bite (2d4+1) 1 bite (2d6)
SENSES Keen Keen Keen
LAIR CHANCE Nil Nil Nil
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1) Animal (1)

EXPERIENCE 50 +2/hp 75 +3/hp 160 +6/hp
Normal hyenas are medium-sized mammals, 4–5ft long and weighing up 
to around 100 lbs. They are muzzle-snouted, fur-covered, four-legged and 
long-tailed predators and scavengers that hunt in packs and live in dens in 
sub-tropical grasslands, woodlands and waste-lands. Many find their haunt -
ing, laugh-like bark unnerving and their tendency to raid shallow graves has 
sometimes earned them an evil reputation.
The jaws of a hyena are very powerful for their size.
Unarmed Combat (Normal and Big):  Normal and Big Hyenas cannot be tar -
geted with grappling or overbearing attacks, but they may overbear. If not 
fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+8/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +2/na. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/
DEF na/na.
Unarmed Combat (Giant): Giant Hyenas cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, but they may overbear. If not fended off, they score an 
unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/na. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +4/na. 
I.
INVISIBLE STALKER
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large (8ft)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 8
MELEE ATTACKS 1 strike (2d8)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE High (13-14)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 1,100 +10/hp
A denizen of the Elemental Plane of Air, an invis -
ible stalker encountered on the Prime Material 
Plane has been conjured and bound to service by 
a magic-user. The summoning magic-user retains 
command of the creature until it either fulfils the 
mission which it was summoned to perform or it 
is killed.
Invisible stalkers are useful, if dangerous, ser -
vants. Once given a mission they will relentlessly 
pursue their goal, motivated more by a dislike of 
forced service and a desire to return to their home 
plane than by any sense of devotion to their sum-
moner. Invisible stalkers are faultless trackers 
across any surface or distance and, if ordered to 
kill their prey, will attack without checking morale 
until either they or their target is dead. As implied 
above, invisible stalkers are not willing servants 
but will not normally begrudge performing a sim-
ple task of short duration for the summoner.
More complicated tasks or tasks of duration lon -
ger than a week will anger the invisible stalker 
and may lead it to attempt to pervert the wording 
of its commands without directly violating them. 
If ordered to guard the summoner’s treasure 
in perpetuity, for instance, the invisible stalker 
might remove every last copper piece of the sum-
moner’s treasure to a secret vault located within 
the invisible stalker’s lair on the Elemental Plane 
of Air, where the ‘stalker could easily keep watch 
over it.
If the GM feels the invisible stalker’s service too 
onerous or complicated, assign a cumulative 1% 
per day chance of the ‘stalker wearying of its task 
and attempting to twist the meaning of its orders. 

===== PAGE 189 =====
188 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
A clever summoner might be able to word their orders in such a way as to 
make its meaning airtight and clear. In this case the ‘stalker must continue 
to serve until its duties are discharged.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Invisible stalkers surprise on a 1–5 on 1d6.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (30%). Any magic spell or effect has a 30% chance (at cast-
er level 11) to have no effect on this creature; but if it does, the magic 
takes full effect.
Special Features
Invisibility. While on the Prime Material Plane invisible stalkers can only be 
viewed by magical means (e.g. gem of seeing, true seeing, detect invisibil-
ity); otherwise anyone attacking an invisible stalker suffers a -2 penalty 
“to hit”. An invisible stalker cannot be killed on this plane. Reducing it to 
zero hit points merely banishes it back to its home plane for a period of 
1 year plus 1 day.
Unarmed Combat. Invisible stalkers cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
Language
Invisible stalkers can understand the common tongue of the Prime Ma -
terial Plane, the language of the Elemental Plane of Air, and their own 
language. They will speak no tongue but their own.
J.
JACKAL
NORMAL HUGE GIANT
FREQUENCY Common Rare Very rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1d6 1d6 1d4
SIZE Small Small Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 120ft 150ft 180ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13] 6 [14] 5 [15]
HIT DICE 1 2 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d4 1 bite (1d4+1) 1 bite (1d8)
SENSES Keen Keen Keen
LAIR CHANCE 25% 25% 25%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE 50 55 65
LOOT Nil Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 10 +1/hp 30 +1/hp 75 +3/hp
Jackals are small mammals, 2ft to 3ft long and weighing about 25 lbs. They 
are muzzle-snouted, fur-covered, four-legged and long-tailed nocturnal 
scavengers and occasional predators. They generally hunt and forage 
singly or in mated pairs, but occasionally gather in 
small packs to bring down difficult prey. Jackals can 
be found in subtropical grasslands, woodlands and 
wastelands, and occasionally in more mountainous 
terrain. They are most active at dawn and dusk, which 
has occasionally led to them being revered by death 
cults.
The small size of the jackal makes it cautious and an 
unlikely combatant, but they can bite if necessary, 
such as when defending their cubs. Huge jackals are 
larger, more aggressive and have a stronger bite, but 
are otherwise similar. Giant jackals are almost cer -
tainly unnatural and are known to often be associ -
ated with religious groups. Though they are thought 
to usually behave as other Jackals, there are rumours 
that some possess a greater degree of intelligence 
and an evil will.
Unarmed Combat (Normal and Big):  Normal and Huge 
jackals cannot be targeted with grappling or over -
bearing attacks, but they may overbear. If not fend -
ed off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. 
Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/na. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/na.
Unarmed Combat (Giant):  Giant jackals cannot be tar -
geted with grappling or overbearing attacks, but they 
may overbear. If not fended off, they score an un -
armed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/
na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +2/na.
JACKALWERE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Medium (small)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 2d8 or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 65
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 75 +4/hp
A jackalwere is a malevolent beast that can assume 
human form (and hence is not a lycanthrope, since 
lycanthropes are humans who can assume animal 
form). They will mingle with people, choosing easy tar-
gets to victimise. Living for the thrill of murder, many 
times they will consume the remains of their prey. Of-
ten they steal their victims’ possessions, assume their 
identities, and go on to the next town. In human form 
===== PAGE 190 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 189
a jackalwere will most often attack with a weapon. Jackalweres 
sometimes prefer the company of normal jackals and can be 
found living among them from time to time.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Non-Iron Mundane Weapons.  The jackalwere is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons, 
unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical bonus 
of +1 or more, or is made of unsmelted cold iron (most metal 
weapons are steel rather than unsmelted iron).
Special Features
Shapeshifter. A jackalwere can shift at will into the form of a hu-
man or back into its jackal form. Shifting does not affect the 
creature’s hit points or armour class, or cure the creature of 
any damage.
Special Attacks
Sleep Gaze. Any creature meeting the gaze of a jackalwere must 
successfully save vs spells or fall into a magical sleep (save 
each time the gaze is met). This applies to anyone who is gen -
erally in front of the creature and not using an indirect method 
of sight such as a mirror. Attacking the creature by looking at a 
reflection incurs a to-hit penalty of -2. Attacking blindly incurs 
a to-hit penalty of -4.
Unarmed Combat. In human form a jackalwere can make grap -
pling or overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an 
unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-10. 
Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
K.
KOBOLD
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 40d10
SIZE Small (3ft)
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1d4 hp
MELEE ATTACKS 1d4 or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Average or Low (8-10)
MORALE 45
LOOT Individual 1 each; in lair Cache 
1 and 3
EXPERIENCE 5 +1/hp
Kobolds stand 3ft tall and are vaguely reptilian in appearance, 
with scaly skin, small horns, short, snaky tails and red eyes. The 
weakest of humanoids, physically, kobolds live in a hierarchical 
society of tribes and clans. They tend to make their lairs in dark 
places such as caves or deep forests. 
Kobold raiding parties are led by a sergeant and 2 assistants, 
who fight as goblins.
Lairs are ruled by a chief and 5d4 bodyguards (all fight as gob -
lins). 
Kobolds often (75%) use wild boars or giant weasels (the small-
sized variety) to guard their lairs.
Immunities and Resistances
Vulnerability to Sunlight. Kobolds detest direct sunlight and fight 
at -1 if they are exposed to it.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack (Slings).  Kobolds often employ slings as missile 
weapons (1d4 blunt damage), 35ft range increment (-2 at 36ft, 
-4 at 71ft).
Unarmed Combat.  Kobolds can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with an 8+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-2. Overbear Re-
sults Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
-2/+2.
Language
These creatures speak lawful evil and their own tongue. Some 
also speak goblin.
KRAKEN
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Huge
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 210ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]/0 [20]
HIT DICE 20
MELEE ATTACKS
2 barbed tentacles (2d6), 6 
tentacles (2d4) and 1 bite 
(5d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect 
invisible 95%
LAIR CHANCE 75%
INTELLIGENCE Genius (17-18)
MORALE 145
LOOT Hoard 7, Cache 4 and 5
EXPERIENCE 17,500 +30/hp
It is rumoured that the krakens once lived in shallower coast -
al waters and had armies of slaves who worshipped them. For 
some reason they were forced to retreat to the deepest depths 
===== PAGE 191 =====
190 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
of the oceans. In the darkness they grew larger and more pow -
erful. They excavated huge labyrinths in the sea floor. Now they 
prey on ships for food and slaves which they drag down to their 
watery dungeons. 
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
1/day: create a sphere of airy water 240ft across, faerie fire (8 
hours), control temperature (40ft), control winds, and weather 
summoning. 
3/day: animal (fish) summoning III which gives them the ability 
to summon the fish but not control them.
Segmented Armour Class. A kraken’s body is protected by a tough 
shell with AC 0 [20], but its tentacles and head are more vul -
nerable, with AC 5 [15].
Special Attacks
Attacking Ships. A kraken will attack a ship in the same way a 
giant squid does, by anchoring itself with 2 arms and attacking 
with the other 6. If the kraken wraps 6 tentacles around a ship 
and squeezes for 3 consecutive rounds, it will cause enough 
damage to sink the ship. If the kraken loses more than 3 arms it 
will release the ship, squirt ink, and flee. 
Tentacle. With each successful hit the kraken grabs its victim and 
holds it (see below). Each round after the hit, the tentacle does 
automatic damage without a to-hit roll, inflicting normal dam-
age plus an additional 3d4 blunt damage from crushing. The 
only way to get free is to sever the tentacle by causing 16 hp 
damage to it (which does not damage the monster itself).
Those within the tentacle’s grasp are 25% likely to have both 
arms held, and as such they cannot attack. Most of the time 
(50%) they will have one limb held and may attack with a -3 
penalty. The other 25% of the time the victim will have both 
arms free and can attack the kraken with only a -1 penalty.
Ink. When fleeing, a kraken releases ink in the water, leaving a 
cloud 80ft deep by 80ft wide by 120ft long. A kraken’s ink is poi-
sonous and will cause 1d4 points of damage per round until the 
ink dissipates. The ink lasts for about 5 rounds.
L.
LAMIA
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 240ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 9
MELEE ATTACKS 1 dagger (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 60%
INTELLIGENCE High (13-14)
MORALE 90
LOOT Hoard 4
EXPERIENCE 1,500 +12/hp
Lamiai like to live in deserted towns, ruins, and other desolate 
places. These creatures have the upper body of a woman and 
the lower half of an animal (usually a lion or tiger, but some -
times a serpent). Typically a lamia is armed with a dagger, but 
these are really just tools. They use spells to lure their victims 
close, and then drain their wisdom by touch. 
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
1/day: charm person, mirror image, suggestion, and illusion (like 
the wand).
Special Attacks
Wisdom-Draining Touch.  The touch of a lamia permanently 
drains a single point of wisdom. The lamia does not need to 
roll to hit against a charmed victim, but must attack normally 
to touch a resisting opponent. Once a victim’s wisdom falls be-
low 3, they become permanent slaves of the lamia (although 
they are likely to just be eaten).
Unarmed Combat. Lamiai cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
Language
Lamiai speak the common tongue as well as the alignment lan-
guage of Chaotic evil.

===== PAGE 192 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 191
LAMMASU
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d8
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Lawful good
MOVE 120ft; 240ft flying (AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 7+7
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d6+1)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect 
invisible 5%
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Genius (17-18)
MORALE 85
LOOT Cache 4, 5, 6
EXPERIENCE 875 +10/hp
Champions of all that is good and just, lammasu have qua -
drupedal bodies the size and shape of the largest of lions, ful -
ly-functional feathered wings, and a human head bearing the 
kindly visage of a bearded wise man.
Lammasu are the guardians and protectors of all lawful good 
persons and have a friendly disposition to all good-aligned crea-
tures they encounter. Lammasu tend to lair in warm climates 
but travel widely and can thus be encountered anywhere.
Lammasu prefer to advise and support the forces of good if at 
all possible, but are formidable opponents in battle. In fact, it 
is almost impossible to press a lammasu into combat against 
its will due to its ability to dimension door and become invisible 
at will. 
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (30%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 30% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Protection from Evil. Lammasu are constantly surrounded by an 
extra strength protection from evil, 10ft radius (-2 to attack, +2 
on saving throws from attacking evil creatures).
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: dimension door and become invisible. 20% of these 
creatures can also speak a holy word at will.
Spells. Lammasu can cast cleric spells of up to 4th level as an 8th 
level cleric does. Spell list: 4—1st level, 3—2nd level, 2—3rd 
level, 1—4th level. They cast cure light wounds and cure serious 
wounds at double normal efficacy (2d8 hp and 4d8+2 hp re -
spectively). 
Unarmed Combat. Lammasu cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
Language
Lammasu can communicate in their own tongue, the alignment 
language of lawful good creatures, and also through telepathy.
LARVAL FIEND
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Any evil
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d4+1)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 100%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 50
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 10 +1/hp
Larval fiends, sometimes called soul worms, are the extraplanar 
form of evil souls. Their appearance is similar to a putrescent 
maggot with a leering humanoid face. They live in great squirm-
ing masses and are tended by night hags, who trade them to 
demons and devils for the creation of quasits and imps. Liches 
also make use of soul worms to help preserve their essence 
throughout the centuries. They are not usually found on the 
prime material plane unless they have somehow been inter -
cepted by a night hag or lich.
Unarmed Combat. Larval fiends cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
LEECH, GIANT
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 4d4
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 30ft
ARMOUR CLASS 9 [11]
HIT DICE 1–4 HD
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite. Damage: 1d4 (1 HD), 
1d6 (2–3 HD) or 1d8 (4 HD)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 50 +3/hp
Giant leeches inhabit warmer, fresh water. 
===== PAGE 193 =====
192 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Special Attacks
Blood Drain. If a giant leech successfully hits a character, it drains 
hp equal to its hit dice each round automatically thereafter (so 
a 4 HD leech drains 4hp per round).
Disease. A character bitten by a giant leech must save vs poison 
or suffer a disease that will be fatal in 1d6 weeks.
Unarmed Combat. Giant leeches cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
LEPRECHAUN
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d20
SIZE Tiny (2ft)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 8 [12]
HIT DICE 1d4+1hp
MELEE ATTACKS Nil
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 50
LOOT Hoard 6
EXPERIENCE 50 +3/hp
Leprechauns are a type of fey that love to torment those they 
encounter with practical jokes, general mischief, and swindles. 
They normally live in green fields or rolling hills on the outskirts 
of civilisation. Because of their sharp senses they are never sur-
prised.
One of the leprechaun’s favourite tricks is to grab some valu -
able object, turn invisible and flee with it. If they are chased too 
closely, they will drop the item rather than be tracked back to 
their lair. If they are caught or tracked back to their lair, the lep-
rechaun will do or say just about anything to win their freedom 
back. Be warned: leprechauns are magical, and they take great 
pride in their confidence games. Nothing they say should be 
considered the truth. A leprechaun will always be playing some 
kind of scam.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Leprechauns cannot be surprised.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (80%).  Any magic spell or effect has an 80% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: A leprechaun can turn invisible, polymorph inanimate 
objects, make illusions, and perform ventriloquism at will.
Unarmed Combat.  Leprechauns can be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an 
unarmed hit with an 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-
10. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF -4/+4. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF -4/+4.
LICH
FREQUENCY Very Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Any evil
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 12 or more
MELEE ATTACKS 1 strike (2d6+paralysation)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect 
invisible 95%
LAIR CHANCE 95%
INTELLIGENCE Genius (17-18) or higher
MORALE 95
LOOT Hoard 1
EXPERIENCE at least 10,000 +16/hp
Liches are the remains of powerful wizard-priests who, through 
fell magics and sinister grimoires, have cheated death and 
live on beyond the grave in a decaying shell that still revels in 
awesome magical energies. Unholy magics and an unwavering 
devotion are not the only things keeping them on the prime 
material plane. Their souls are already traded to dark gods, but 
a spark of their essence remains that must be encased in a tal -
isman of sorts. This trinket is a requirement of their Unlife, but 
no scholar knows how or why this is.
The lair of a lich will often be a complex underworld maze or a 
wickedly diseased stretch of thick wilderness, at whose black 
heart resides a tower riddled with magic traps and deadly 
guardians. Liches do not suffer the fools who would tread upon 
their mysteries.
Often dressed in rich decaying rags that were exquisite in life, 
liches are now horrors to behold. They are cadaverous in ap -
pearance, with unholy pin-points of green light emanating from 
the otherwise empty pits that are the eye-sockets of its fleshless 
skull. Diseased and leprous tatters of meat cling feebly to an -
cient bones, reeking with the rot of the grave.
Liches are believed to feast on larval fiends as part of the regi -
men that maintains their unlife.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Liches are turned as type 12 Undead.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
===== PAGE 194 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 193
Undeath. This creature is immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by sleep, gases, 
poison, or disease.
Other Immunities. Liches are immune to cold, electrical, paral -
ysation, polymorph, and death magic . Spells that drain attri -
butes or statistics also have no effect.
Special Features
Spell Use. All liches are powerful spellcasters. 40% of liches cast 
spells as a magic-user, 40% as a cleric, and 20% as both, at 18th 
level of ability. Those with magic-user abilities will have multi -
ple copies of powerful spell tomes.
Aura of Fear. Anything below 6th level beholding a lich (even in 
a reflection or a projected image) must save vs magic or flee, 
never to return to the area again.
Special Attacks
Paralysing Touch. The mere touch of a lich will inflict 2d6 cold 
damage upon the victim, and they must save vs paralysation 
or be held frozen to the spot for 3d8 turns. 
Unarmed Combat. Liches can be targeted with grappling or over-
bearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with an 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
LION
LION COUGAR PREHISTORIC
FREQUENCY Common Common Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d6 1d2 2d4
SIZE Large Medium Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 120ft 150ft 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15] 6 [14] 5 [15]
HIT DICE 5+3 3+2 6+3
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d6) and 1 bite (1d10) 2 claws (1d4) and 1 bite (1d6) 2 claws (2d4) and 1 bite (2d6)
SENSES Standard Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE 25% 15% 25%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4) Semi- (2-4) Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 75 65 80
LOOT Nil Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 250 +6/hp 100 +3/hp 300 +6/hp
LION
These prefer warm climates and thrive in any such locale be 
it jungle or savannah. Lions organise themselves into family 
units called prides. Males are aggressive fighters, defending the 
territory and the other members of the pride. The female, re -
ferred to as a lioness, does all the hunting. A pride will have 1d2 
males and 2d4+1 females. If encountered in their lair, there will 
be 1d10 cubs in various stages of growth but too immature to 
fight. All adult pride members will fight to the death to protect 
the young. Lions cannot climb trees and will only swim under 
duress.
COUGAR
Popularly known as mountain lions, these great cats are not true 
lions but are similar in most respects. Cougars are more solitary 
than lions and tend to range more widely while hunting. They 
are also more prone to attack if approached than are true lions. 
Though they share habitat preferences with lions they also inhab-
it mountains as well, giving rise to their popular name. Mountain 
lions can leap 20ft to attack or spring upwards 15ft.
PREHISTORIC LION
Also known as “cave lions” or “spotted lions,” with the excep -
tion of their larger size these cats are very similar to regular 
lions. Cave lions tend to be found in “lost world” type settings 
but have been encountered in the same areas as ordinary lions.
Surprise and Initiative
Alert. Lions (all types) are only surprised on a 1 in 1d6.
Special Attacks
Rake. All lions gain an extra two rear leg raking claw attacks (for 
the same damage as their forepaws) if they successfully hit 
with both clawing attacks in melee.
Unarmed Combat (Lions and Prehistoric Lions):  Lions cannot be 
targeted with grappling or overbearing attacks, but they may 
overbear. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/na. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +5/na. 
Unarmed Combat (Cougars):  Cougars cannot be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, but they may overbear. If not 
fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit 
Mods: ATK/DEF +10/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +3/na. 
===== PAGE 195 =====
194 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
LYCANTHROPE
A lycanthrope is a human with the ability to change their shape 
to some animal form, or a hybrid human-animal form, during 
the night hours. A full moon is 90% likely to trigger the transi -
tion to animal form automatically. Any humanoid bitten by a 
lycanthrope and damaged for 50% or more of its total hp who 
survives will contract the lycanthropic disease of its attacker. A 
cure disease from a cleric of 12th level or higher will remove the 
disease if performed within three days of the attack. If the vic -
tim ingests belladonna within one hour after the attack there is 
a 25% chance the disease will be cured. However, swallowing 
belladonna in such a manner will incapacitate the victim for 1d4 
days; there is also a 1% chance of the herb killing the victim.
A lycanthrope’s “size” stat shows its shape-changed size. In hu-
man form they are obviously always medium-sized.
Each type of lycanthrope has its own language. The five most 
common types are listed below.
WEREBEAR WEREBOAR WERERAT WERETIGER WEREWOLF
FREQUENCY Rare Rare Uncommon Very rare Common
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1d4 2d4 4d6 1d6 3d6
SIZE Large Large Small to medium Large Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic Good Neutral Lawful evil Neutral Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft 120ft 120ft 120ft 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18] 4 [16] 6 [14] 3 [17] 5 [15]

(x5) Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 850 +10/hp 250 +6/hp 150 +4/hp 500 +8/hp 200 +5/hp
WEREBEAR
A werebear appears in human form as a large hairy human with 
a solitary disposition. In ursine form, they are 50% likely to be 
accompanied by 1d6 brown bears. 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Non-Silver Mundane Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +1 or more, or is made of silver.
Robust Health. Werebears are immune to disease and heal at 3x 
the normal rate.
Special Features
Summon Allies. A werebear may summon 1d6 brown bears in 
2d6 turns if they are within one mile.
Physic. Wearbears can cure disease in another creature in 1d10 
x3 days. This might not be fast enough to save a person’s life, 
but if there is no cleric available with cure disease, it might be 
the only option.
===== PAGE 196 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 195
Lycanthropy. See general description for all lycanthropes (shape-
shifting and transmission).
Special Attacks
Bear Hug. If the werebear hits with both claw attacks, it crushes 
the opponent for an additional 2d8 points of blunt damage, 
and can continue inflicting this 2d8 damage automatically each 
round thereafter without making a to-hit roll.
WEREBOAR
A wereboar has a foul tempered disposition in either form. It is 
found in heavily wooded areas, occasionally with normal boars 
(15% chance of 1d4 boars that will attack on direction by the 
lycanthrope).
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Non-Silver Mundane Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +1 or more, or is made of silver.
Special Features
Lycanthropy. See general description for all lycanthropes (shape-
shifting and transmission).
WERERAT
The horrid ratmen infest the underground sewers and cata -
combs under large cities. They can assume any of three forms: 
human, giant rat, or man-rat. In human or man-rat form, they 
often arm themselves with swords, but prefer to lure unsus -
pecting individuals into traps where the victim may be held for 
ransom or later meals. Rumours persist of a city of black wer -
erats on a remote shore; the more fanciful tales suggest the 
black wererats may have powerful spell casters among their 
number who can magically affect the phases of the moon.
Surprise and Initiative
Stealth. Wererats surprise on a 1-4 on 1d6.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Non-Silver Mundane Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +1 or more, or is made of silver.
Special Features
Summon Rats. A wererat may summon 2d6 giant rats and con -
trol them. They appear in 1d4 rounds.
Lycanthropy. See general description for all lycanthropes (shape-
shifting and transmission).
WERETIGER
Weretigers are usually found in the same climates as tigers, and 
occasionally live with normal tigers (5%). All normal cats are 75% 
likely to be friendly to a weretiger due to the lycanthrope’s abil-
ity to speak with them.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Non-Silver Mundane Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +1 or more, or is made of silver.
Special Features
Lycanthropy. See general description for all lycanthropes (shape-
shifting and transmission).
Special Attacks
Rake. The weretiger gains an extra two rear leg raking claw at -
tacks (1d4+1 each) if they successfully hit with both clawing 
attacks in melee.
WEREWOLF
When in wolf form, the lycanthrope tends to walk on its back 
legs. If a werewolf pack numbers 5 to 8, then it will be a family 
pack which consists of a male, a female and 3–6 younger were-
wolves ranging from 60% to 90% mature. Any pack may also 
include wolfweres (wolves who can assume human form).
Surprise and Initiative
Stealth. Werewolves surprise on a 1-3 on 1d6.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Non-Silver Mundane Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +1 or more, or is made of silver.
Special Features
Lycanthropy. See general description for all lycanthropes (shape-
shifting and transmission).

FIRE LIZARDS
Fire lizards are also known as “false dragons” and are thought 
to be an evolutionary dead-end of the dragon family tree. These 
giant lizards have a tough scaled hide very similar to a dragon 
but lacking the long life, wings, and horns of their fire-breath -
ing cousins. Colouration tends to be a rather neutral grey with 
patches of a dark brownish red on the dorsal surface, and a 
lighter red on the ventral surface. Fire lizards darken in colour 
as they age. Curiously, red dragons avoid confrontation with 
fire lizards and will not even lair in the same area fire lizards 
frequent.
False dragons are normally slow-moving creatures and spend 
50% their time sleeping in their subterranean lairs. When they 
emerge every two weeks or so to feed, however, they are ag -
gressive foes.
Fire lizards have an affinity for shiny objects and their lairs tend 
be littered with coins and gems. A lair also has a 15% chance of 
containing 1d6 eggs, but the fire lizard is largely indifferent as to 
their fate. The first hatchling will generally eat the others as they 
emerge. A fire lizard egg can fetch as much as 5,000gp from an 
interested party.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Fire. Fire lizards are immune to fire damage.
Special Attacks
Breath Weapon. Fire lizards can exhale a cone-shaped blast of 
flame 10ft wide at the terminus with a range of 150ft, causing 
2d6 points of fire damage. A saving throw vs breath weapons 
is allowed for half damage. Breathing fire takes up the lizard’s 
action for that round, but the breath weapon can be used as 
often as the lizard chooses.
Unarmed Combat. Fire Lizards cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks; they may, occasionally, overbear. If not 
fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit 
Mods: ATK/DEF +6/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +10/
na.
===== PAGE 198 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 197
GIANT LIZARDS
Giant Lizards dwell in marshy or swampy areas, but can also be 
found underground. Other than their size, they are unremark -
able beasts.
Special Attacks
Powerful Bite. On a to-hit roll of 20+ (including a modified 20), 
a giant lizard has grabbed its opponent firmly in its powerful 
jaws and will cause double damage that round.
MONITOR LIZARDS
Monitor Lizards are aggressive carnivores found in warm re -
gions. Their colouration tends toward dark grey and black and 
they have longer snouts and longer, sharper teeth than other 
types of giant lizards. 
Surprise and Initiative
Monitor lizards move slowly, but can lunge suddenly, surprising 
their prey on a 1-4 on 1d6.
Special Attacks
Bite. On a to-hit roll of 20+ (including a modified 20), in addition 
to ordinary damage, the monitor has seized its opponent in 
its toothy maw and the unfortunate victim is subjected to an 
automatic hit in the following round.
Unarmed Combat. Giant monitor lizards cannot be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
CAVE LIZARDS
Cave Lizards dwell in underground settings such as caverns and 
dungeons.
Special Features
Climber. This creature can move on walls and ceilings as if they 
were floors.
Special Attacks
Powerful Bite. On a natural to-hit roll of 20, the cave lizard’s jaws 
have clamped onto its prey, causing double damage on that 
attack.
Unarmed Combat.  Giant cave lizards cannot be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
LIZARD MAN
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d4
SIZE Medium (7ft tall)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft; 120ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15] or 4 [16] with shield
HIT DICE 2+1
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d2) and 1 bite (1d8) 
or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Low (average)
MORALE 55
LOOT Hoard 4
EXPERIENCE 20 +2/hp
Lizardmen are bipedal reptile-people. Their hides are tough and 
leathery, and are coloured in many different shades of green. 
They have cold black eyes, and hard nails on their fingers. Some 
have a bony ridge along their backs.
Lizard men often make their homes in underwater caves that 
contain air. They are semi-aquatic and require air to breathe. 
They are excellent swimmers, and will eat most anything, 
though they prefer human meat to all others.
Some communities of lizardmen are more civilized than others. 
These make their homes in crude villages and use shields for 
protection. These lizard men tribes also make use of barbed 
darts and javelins as missile weapons. For the most part, these 
lizardmen still hunt and eat humans.
Immunities and Resistances
Hold Breath.  This creature can go for up to an hour between 
breaths.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack. Advanced lizardmen can use missile weapons as 
humans do.
Unarmed Combat. Lizardmen can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+0/+0.
Language
Lizard men speak their own tongue, and a few (10%) can also 
speak common.
===== PAGE 199 =====
198 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
LOCATHAH
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 20d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (By weapon)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 55
LOOT Hoard 1
EXPERIENCE 30 +1/hp
Locathah are bipedal fish-people, covered in scales. They have 
large, black eyes and large fan-shaped finned ears. Locathah 
can leave the water for short periods (2d10 minutes) of time but 
are loath to do so.
For the most part, locathah make their home in the warm, 
shallow, salt waters of seas and oceans. They roam the area 
around their lair, hunting and gathering food. They are rather 
distrustful of strangers, even strangers of their own species. 
Lairs are built in undersea rock formations, very much like hu -
man fortifications, and they often alter the rock face to a distinc-
tive fortress-like appearance. The locathah will hollow the rock 
into rooms, chambers, and passages as needed and doors are 
typically quite sturdy and well-guarded either by moray eels or 
trapped air bubbles containing Portuguese man-o-wars.
Locathah troops are organised into companies of 40 warriors, 
each led by a war-chief with 22 hit points who fights as a 5th 
level fighter. The war-chief is assisted by 4 sub-chiefs with 15 hp 
who fight as 3rd level fighters. 
Any group of 3 or more companies (120 warriors) will be led by 
a full chief with 30 hp who fights as a 6th level fighter. The chief 
will be protected by an honour guard of 12 warriors who fight 
as sub-chiefs. 
There is a 5% chance a sub-chief or war-chief will be carrying a 
magic weapon of the appropriate type and a 10% a full chief will 
have one.
Locathah troops ride giant eels (q.v.) into battle and these 
mounts will also fight. 
• One-fourth of locathah troops are armed with lances (2d4+1 
piercing).
• One-fourth of locathah troops are armed with tridents (1d6+1 
piercing).
• One-fourth of locathah troops are armed with light crossbow 
(1d4+1 piercing) and dagger. The light crossbows have a 20ft 
range underwater.
• One-fourth of locathah troops are armed with net and dag -
ger. 
Special Attacks
Ranged or Melee Attack (Net).  Some locathah are armed with 
nets. These have a range of 20ft (and can be thrown while in 
melee combat). If the attack hits, the target is automatically 
(no saving throw) entangled for 1d4 rounds, unable to attack 
or move.
Unarmed Combat. Locathah can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks and may use these tactics. If not fended 
off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: 
ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grap -
pling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0.
LURKER ABOVE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 10ft; 90ft flying (AA:I)
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 10
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (Smother)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 95%
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Hoard 3, Cache 11
EXPERIENCE 1,500 +10/hp

===== PAGE 200 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 199
Lurkers are dangerous creatures that wait in high places for 
prey to pass beneath them. In shape they resemble manta rays, 
but have such colouration and form that they can become al -
most indistinguishable from the stone of the caverns in which 
they dwell.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Lurkers surprise on a 1-5 in 1d6, even if they wait to 
drop (this is a measure of how difficult they are to see).
Special Attacks
Drop and Smother.  Lurkers attack by dropping onto a creature 
and enfolding it. Lurkers that successfully engulf their victim 
(on a successful to-hit roll) automatically cause 1d6 points 
of damage from constriction every round thereafter and will 
cause suffocation within 1d4+1 rounds. Those engulfed may 
only use short weapons (short sword or smaller) against their 
attacker and are not in a position to change weapons. Lurk -
ers continue to attack until slain. Fortunately for adventurers, 
lurkers have such a tough body that attacking a lurker that has 
enfolded someone does not damage the enfolded victim.
Unarmed Combat. Lurkers above cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
LYNX, GIANT
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 2+2
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d2) and 1 bite (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 60
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 90 +3/hp
Rare and magical, giant lynxes live in the icy wastes and in deep 
forests, where they use their intelligence to advantage against 
competing predators. 
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Owing to their camouflaged pelts, giant lynxes surprise 
opponents on a 1–5 on 1d6.
Special Features
Thief Abilities. Giant lynxes have the thief skills Hide, Move Qui -
etly and Climb at 90% and Traps at 75%.
Special Attacks
Rake. If a giant lynx hits with both claw attacks, it gains an addi-
tional 2 attacks with the rear claws (1d3 slashing).
Unarmed Combat.  Giant lynxes cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, but may choose to overbear a 
lone opponent. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/na. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +2/na.
Language
Giant lynxes speak their own language and that of the fey.

MANTICORE
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 120ft; 180ft flying (AA:II)
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 6+3
MELEE ATTACKS 3 (1d3/1d3/1d8)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 80
LOOT Hoard 5
EXPERIENCE 525 +8/hp
A manticore has a lion’s body with a human head and bat-like 
wings. Its tail is thick and club-like at the end, and bristles with 
iron spikes. They make their lairs in dark, dank caves or des -
olate underground caverns, flying out by night to hunt sheep, 
cattle, and (if available) humans.
Special Attacks
Tail Spikes. The manticore can launch 6 tail spikes (1d6 piercing) 
at a range of up to 60ft (120ft at -2, 180ft at -4). A manticore 
has 24 spikes total, which regrow fully after as little as 2 days.
Unarmed Combat. Manticores cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, but may choose to overbear a lone op-
ponent. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/na. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +4/na.
Language
Manticores are monsters, and despite the humanlike head they 
only speak the alignment tongue of lawful evil.

200 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
MEDUSA
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 6+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 snakebite (1d6 + poison)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 50%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12) to High (13-14)
MORALE 75
LOOT Cache 2, 3 (x10), 10, 11
EXPERIENCE 750 +6/hp
Medusae resemble the monster from Greek myth: women with 
snakes for hair, whose gaze turns people to stone. One of the 
most effective weapons against a medusa is a mirror, for a me-
dusa that sees her own reflection may be turned to stone her -
self if she fails her save.
Special Attacks
Poison Snakebite.  The medusa attacks by drawing near to the 
victim, allowing her snakes to lash out and bite. In addition to 
the 1d6 piercing damage, anyone bitten must make a saving 
throw vs. poison or die.
Petrifying Gaze.  Any creature meeting the gaze of a medusa 
must successfully save vs petrifaction or instantly be turned to 
stone (save each time the gaze is met). The gaze effects extend 
into both the astral and ethereal planes. This applies to anyone 
who is generally in front of the medusa, and not using an indi-
rect method of sight such as a mirror. Attacking the creature 
by looking at a reflection incurs a to-hit penalty of -2. Attacking 
blindly incurs a to-hit penalty of -4.
Unarmed Combat. Medusae can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-6. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+0/+0.
Language
Medusae speak the common tongue.

Mephits are the varlets, lackeys, messengers, errand runners, 
and fetchers of the Lower Planes. They can be found in any of 
the evilly-aligned lower planes and will be serving the various 
demons, devils, evil elemental gods, or any extra-planar crea -
ture of dark purpose and evil intent. Their exact origins and 
home plane are not known; even the mephits themselves do 
not know for certain. This entry lists the known types of me -
phits, but dark rumours persist of other types more dangerous 
than these.
Mephits share various common characteristics. They are all 
about 5ft tall, bat-winged, with sharp fangs. Mephits speak their 
own language shared by all the different types of mephits, their 
alignment tongue, and usually speak the common tongue of 
whatever lower plane they happen to call home. Mephits are 
mischievous and have a malignant sense of humour, finding joy 
in the pain of others. 
Unarmed Combat. Mephits cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
===== PAGE 202 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 201
FIRE MEPHITS
Fire mephits are wreathed with tiny wisps of flame, and touch -
ing one bare-handed will cause 1 point of damage. In combat 
mephits attack with two claws and a breath weapon. 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Fire. Fire mephitis are immune to fire damage.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
1/Hour: attempt to gate in another mephit, type determined 
randomly, (33% chance of success).
1/day: magic missile (two missiles) and heat metal.
Special Attacks
Breath Weapon.  The fire mephit’s breath weapon has two 
modes: a jet of flame 15ft long and 1 inch wide, or a blanket 
of flame 5ft square. The jet is directed against a single target 
and always hits, causing 1d8+1 fire damage; a saving throw 
vs breath weapons is allowed for half damage. The blanket of 
flame causes 4 points of damage to each victim in the area of 
effect, no saving throw allowed.
LAVA MEPHIT
These mephits are red in colour and sweat droplets of molten 
lava. As a result, the lava mephit is so hot it can be sensed up 
to 30ft away. Touching one without protection results in 1d8 
points of damage.
The touch of a lava mephit dissolves metal rather quickly, de -
stroying plate mail armour in 3 melee rounds. Wood dissolves 
a little more slowly, about an inch per hour of contact. The lava 
mephit can shape-change into a pool of lava but doing so will 
not recharge the mephit’s breath weapon or allow it to regen -
erate. 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Fire. Lava mephitis are immune to fire damage.
Special Features
Limited Regeneration. When in contact with molten lava, the lava 
mephit can regenerate 2 hp per round, but if killed it cannot 
regenerate back to life as a troll does. 
Spell-Like Abilities.
1/Hour: gate in 1d2 mephits (25% chance of success). The type 
is random, but if two appear, they will be the same type.
Burning Touch. Although it has no effect in combat, a lava me -
phit can melt metal and burn wood by prolonged contact, de -
stroying doors or other impediments. 
Special Attacks
Spit Lava. In addition to its attack, every 3 rounds a lava mephit 
can spit a blob of molten lava, up to 8 times before recharging 
(see below). The projectile has a 10ft range, always hits the me-
phit’s target, and inflicts 1d6 fire damage with no saving throw. 
Contact with lava also allows the mephit to recharge its breath 
weapon.
SMOKE MEPHIT
This type of mephit is charcoal black, and constantly emits 
smoke. When encountered on the Prime Material Plane, smoke 
mephits rarely stray from dark or smoky areas except in great 
need. Smoke mephits attack with two claws, and may employ 
their breath weapon while in combat. 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Fire. Smoke mephits are immune to fire damage.
Special Features
Death Flash. If slain, the smoke mephit emits a burst of flash-
fire which inflicts 1 point of damage to everyone with 10ft, no 
saving throw allowed.
Spell-Like Abilities.
1/Hour: gate in 1d2 mephits (25% chance of success). The type 
is random, but if two appear, they are the same type.
1/day: invisibility and dancing lights.

===== PAGE 203 =====
202 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Special Attacks
Smokeball (every other round). The smoke mephit’s breath weap-
on is a ball of oily smoke that automatically strikes its target, 
with a range of 20ft, dealing 1d4 hp, no save allowed. In ad -
dition to the hit point damage, the victim is blinded for 1d2 
rounds. There is no limit to the number of times a smoke me -
phit may use its breath weapon.
STEAM MEPHIT
These mephits constantly sweat hot water in profuse amounts, 
leaving puddles of hot water behind them as they travel. Touch-
ing a steam mephit causes 1 point of damage to unprotected 
skin and has a 50% chance to stun the victim for 1 melee round.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Fire. Steam mephits are immune to fire damage.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
1/Hour: gate in 1d2 mephits (30% chance of success). The type 
is random, but if two appear, they are the same type. Also, 
the steam mephit can contaminate water (as the reverse of 
the purify water spell).
1/day: rain boiling water in a 40×40ft square area of effect for 
2d6 hp to all in the affected area (no saving throw).
Special Attacks
Spit Scalding Water (every other round).  The steam mephit can 
spit a stream of scalding water that will automatically hit any 
single target within 20ft. The stream inflicts 1d3 points of dam-
age and has a 50% chance to stun the target (no saving throw 
allowed). 
MERMAN
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 20d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 10ft; 180ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (By weapon)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 50
LOOT Hoard 3, Cache 4
EXPERIENCE 30 +1/hp
Mermen (also called “merfolk”) appear as normal humans from 
the waist up, but as human-sized fish or dolphins from the waist 
down. They disdain clothing, though they will often adorn them-
selves with plundered jewellery and use any enchanted weap -
ons and protective magic rings or bracers they may gain in their 
raids.
Merfolk are the inhabitants of oceans and seas in the warmer 
areas of the world, being particularly fond of tropical seas. Mer-
men are much like rural humans, farming and harvesting vege-
tation on the ocean floor and hunting in the area around their 
settlements; they hunt for fish and other sea–going creatures. 
Merfolk have been known to herd certain fish, keeping them 
corralled in large, spherical netted seaweed pens in much the 
same way their human counterparts will herd cattle.
Merfolk tend to congregate in small communities, establish -
ing their dwellings in reefs or underwater cliffs and tunnelling 
many rooms and passages into these natural structures. Rarely, 
mermen will construct dwellings of natural materials such as 
seashells, coral, rocks, and materials scavenged from sunken 
sailing vessels. These communities, no matter the location, will 
correspond to a human agricultural type community in many 
respects: there will be workshops, residences, pens for the food 
fish, and storage areas for the harvested sea vegetation. Mer -
folk rarely venture from the ocean but can sometimes be spot -
ted sunning themselves on coastal rocks in isolated areas.
Merfolk communities are guarded by 3d6 giant gar (see Fish, 
Giant), bred and trained by the mermen for this purpose.
In battle, merfolk troops encountered will be armed as follows:
• 50% trident
• 25% crossbow (1d4+1 piercing) and dagger. The light cross -
bows have a 20ft range underwater.
• 25% net (see below), javelin, and dagger. 
Though mermen tend to avoid humans under most circum -
stances, they have been known to attack and sink surface ves -
sels to plunder them. Grappling hook-equipped mermen will 
surface and hurl their grapples up to 30ft as the other mer -
men attack exposed crewman on the ship with crossbows and 
thrown javelins. The merfolk are quite proficient with grapples 
and will successfully score a hit on a roll of 1–9 on a 1d10. If a 
grapple hits it will be grabbed and held by 10 mermen, causing 
the ship to lose 30ft per round of top speed for each successful 
grapple attack. If the crew of the ship manages to cut a grapple 
line it will take the 10 mermen holding the line a round to re -
group and return to the fight. Any merman grappling the ship 
or laying down cover fire is exposed to ranged attack from the 
ship. Further, fire-based attacks will cause double damage but 
only for a single round. If the grappled ship is successfully ren -
dered motionless it will be pulled underwater in 4d4 rounds. 
Any crewmen abandoning ship at this time will be ignored as 
the ship and mermen sink beneath the waves. Mermen do not 
take surface dwellers prisoner, but will not normally harm any 
crewmen in the water—unless the crew of the ship foolishly try 
to continue the battle.
===== PAGE 204 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 203
Special Attacks
Ranged or Melee Attack (Net). Some mermen are armed with 
nets. These have a range of 20ft (and can be thrown while in 
melee combat). If the attack hits, the target is automatically 
(no saving throw) entangled for 1d4 rounds, unable to attack 
or move.
Language
Merfolk speak their own language, 50% speak locathah, and if 
the merfolk dwell near any other intelligent aquatic creatures 
at least 10% of the community will be able to speak that tongue 
as well.
MINOTAUR
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d8
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 6+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 gore (2d4) or 1 bite (1d4), or 
by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 80
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 225 +6/hp
Minotaurs usually like to inhabit vast mazes whether they be 
underground or outdoors. They are excellent trackers (50% 
chance to track) and will always give chase if they can see their 
prey. These beasts are man–eaters and will attack savagely any-
thing they think they can kill and eat. They are stupid and can 
be tricked.
Minotaurs can attack with their horns only if their opponent is 
medium-sized or larger. They have to bite smaller foes or attack 
with a weapon.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Minotaurs are surprised only on a 1 in 1d6.
Unarmed Combat. Minotaurs can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +3/-3. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+2/-2.
Language
Minotaurs can speak their own language and sometimes (25%) 
can speak common.
MOULD
BROWN YELLOW
FREQUENCY Very rare Uncommon
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1 patch 1 patch
SIZE Small to Large Small to Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 0 0
ARMOUR CLASS 10 [10] 10 [10]
HIT DICE N/A N/A
MELEE ATTACKS Nil Nil
SENSES N/A N/A
LAIR CHANCE Nil Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0) Non- (0)
MORALE N/A N/A
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE None None
BROWN MOULD
Brown mould can be found in places where it escapes the ef -
fects of ultraviolet light. It feeds on most other sources of en -
ergy, and absorbs the body heat of any creature that comes 
within 5ft. 
Immunities and Resistances
Almost Unkillable. Brown Mould cannot be killed without the use 
of a wish spell. The only thing that really affects it is magical 
cold. An ice storm or wall of ice will make the brown mould go 
into a dormant state for 5d6 turns.
Special Features
Growth by Fire. Brown mould grows in the presence of heat, so 
that the presence of high (fiery) temperatures can cause the 
mould to increase its size by a factor of 2d4 times in the course 
of a melee round.
Special Attacks
Heat Absorption.  Heat absorption from a brown mould deals 
damage to all creatures within 5ft, at a rate of 4d8 cold damage 
per combat round (this is approximately 1d8 per 10 degrees 
Fahrenheit of body heat over 50 degrees). Creatures that are 
colder (undead, in particular) will take less damage or poten -
tially none at all. 
YELLOW MOULD
Yellow mould is a growth found only in dark places, more of a 
hazard than a monster.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to All but Fire. Yellow mould is extremely susceptible to 
fire and fire attacks, but cannot be killed by any other means. A 
===== PAGE 205 =====
204 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
continual light spell will force the mould to go dormant for 2d6 
turns until it covers the light source. If it is set alight, the mould 
will die in 1d3+3 rounds.
Special Attacks
Spores. When touched, yellow mould releases spores in a 10ft 
(cube) cloud. Any creature within this area will die unless it suc-
cessfully saves vs poison. A dead character will require a cure 
disease in addition to raise dead or resurrection, or they will re-
main infected and die again. If any surface covered in yellow 
mould is handled roughly, there is a 50% chance spores will be 
released in such a cloud.
MULE
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d2) or 2 kicks (1d6)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 60
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 20 +2/hp
Mules are a sterile cross-breed of horses and donkeys, and are 
sure-footed enough to be taken into dungeons. Their stubborn-
ness is legendary. The stats given for mules can also be used for 
asses and donkeys, although these should have their hit points 
rolled on d6s rather than d8s to reflect their smaller size.
MUMMY
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 6+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d12)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 80%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Hoard 4
EXPERIENCE 985 +8/hp
The mummy is an undead creature. Normally found in tombs 
or other burial places, they hate life and attempt to destroy any 
living creature they encounter. A raise dead spell will transform 
the monster into a 7th-level fighter unless the mummy makes a 
successful save vs spells.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Mummies are turned as type 8 Undead.
Immune to Mundane Weapons and Resistant to All Weapons.  This 
creature is immune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage 
from weapons and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon caus-
ing that damage has a magical bonus of +1 or more. Even a 
magic weapon inflicts only half damage on a mummy (drop 
fractions).
Undeath. This creature is immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by sleep, gases, 
poison, or disease.
Vulnerable to Fire. A mummy is susceptible to fire. A hit from a 
torch will deal 1d3 hp damage, a flask of burning oil causes 1d8 
hp on the first round and 2d8 hp on the second round, and 
magical fires deal +1 for each die of damage. 
Special Features
Fear. All creatures within 60ft of a mummy, upon sight, will suf -
fer the effects of fear and revulsion unless a successful saving 
throw vs spells is made. If the save fails, the victim will be par -
alysed for 1d4 rounds. Groups of creatures gain a bonus of +1 
for their save at a ratio of six creatures for each mummy (for 
example: 12 creatures in sight of 1 mummy gain a +2 to save; 
12 creatures in sight of 2 mummies gain a +1, and 12 creatures 
within sight of 3 or more mummies gain no bonus). If humans 
confront a mummy, then each human will gain a +2 to their 
saves.
Special Attacks
Mummy Rot. A successful hit by a mummy causes a wasting and 
rotting disease which will cause death within 1d6 months. For 
each month of affliction, the victim permanently loses 2 points 
of charisma, and while diseased no cure wound spells will have 
any effect and any wounds will naturally heal at a 10% rate. The 
mummy rot can only be cured by a cure disease spell. A charac-
ter killed by a mummy cannot be raised (due to the amount of 
damage caused by the rotting waste) unless a cure disease and 
raise dead are both cast on the victim within one hour of death.
Unarmed Combat. Mummies cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
===== PAGE 206 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 205
N.
NAGA
GUARDIAN SPIRIT WATER

MOVE 150ft 120ft 90ft swimming

HIT DICE 11 to 12 9 to 10 7 to 8
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d6) and tail lash (2d8) 1 bite (1d3) 1 bite (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect 
invisible 30%
Infravision 60ft, Detect

LAIR CHANCE 75% 60% 45%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16) High (13-14) Very (11-12)
