Unarmed Combat.  Nagas cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
GUARDIAN NAGA
A guardian naga appears as a long, snake-like creature with a 
human head, golden eyes, and green-gold scales. The scales 
along its spine are triangular and silvery.
Guardian naga are mostly found in holy places. They serve as 
sentinels over some ancient evil or treasure belonging to the 
cause of good. 
Special Features
Spells. The guardian naga casts clerical spells as a 6th level caster:
• 1st level: 3
• 2nd Level: 3
• 3rd level: 2
Special Attacks
Poison Bite.  In addition to piercing damage, the naga’s bite is 
poisonous. Anyone hit by the naga’s bite must make a saving 
throw vs. poison or die.
Spit Poison. The naga can spit poison up to 30ft at any single 
target (to-hit roll required), and the target must save vs poison 
or die.
SPIRIT NAGA
The evil and corrupt spirit naga dwells in deep ruins and other 
subterranean places. Its scales are black with blood red bands, 
and its human-like head is large and misshapen.
Special Features
Spells. A spirit naga can use divine and arcane spells to 4th and 
5th levels respectively:
DIVINE ARCANE
1st level: 3 1st level: 4
2nd level: 2 2nd level: 2
3rd level: 1
Special Attacks
Poison Bite.  In addition to piercing damage, the naga’s bite is 
poisonous. Anyone hit by the naga’s bite must make a saving 
throw vs. poison or die.
Gaze Attack (Charm). Any creature meeting the naga’s gaze must 
successfully save vs paralysation or be charmed (as per a 
charm person spell). A saving throw is required each time the 
gaze is met. This applies to anyone who is generally in front of 
the naga, and not using an indirect method of sight such as a 
mirror. Attacking the creature by looking at a reflection incurs 
a to-hit penalty of -2. Attacking blindly incurs a to-hit penalty 
of -4.
===== PAGE 207 =====
206 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
WATER NAGA
The water naga’s scales range from deep green to turquoise 
and its eye colour varies from light green to brilliant amber. It 
dwells deep below the surface of fresh water lakes ponds and 
rivers. The water naga is typically quite curious and harmless 
unless provoked. 
Special Features
Spells. The water naga casts arcane spells as a 5th level mag -
ic-user:
• 1st level: 4
• 2nd level: 2
• 3rd level: 1
Special Attacks
Poison Bite.  In addition to piercing damage, the naga’s bite is 
poisonous. Anyone hit by the naga’s bite must make a saving 
throw vs. poison or die.
NECROPHIDIUS
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large (15ft long)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d8 + paralysis)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 125 +2/hp
Referred to in forbidden books of necromancy as the “death 
worm”, the necrophidius is a skeletal giant snake with the 
fanged skull of an adult human for a head. The necrophidius 
is a necromantic construct but is not an undead creature and 
cannot be turned by a cleric. 
There are several ways to go about creating a necrophidius. 
First, the basic materials must be assembled: the skull of a 
cold-blooded murderer who has been killed within 72 hours of 
the ritual, and the complete skeleton of a giant snake. The pro -
cess will also require 10 days plus the services and laboratory 
of an alchemist, who will charge the full monthly fee plus 500 
gp per hp of the completed necrophidius. At the end of 10 days, 
the potential owner has 24 hours to complete the ritual by one 
of the methods detailed hereafter.
First, a magical book can be used. These work the same way 
as a manual of the golems  (q.v.) can be used to create a golem. 
Second, a high-level magic-user can cast the following spells in 
this order upon the pre-necrophidius alchemical mixture: limit-
ed wish, geas, and charm person. Upon completion of the charm 
person spell the death worm magically assembles from the var-
ious parts, absorbing the fluids of the mixture. Lastly, a cleric 
can cast the following divine spells upon the pre-necrophidius 
alchemical mixture in this precise order: quest, neutralise poison, 
prayer, silence 15ft radius , and snake charm . As with the mag -
ic-user, when the last spell is completed the necrophidius mag-
ically assembles from the various parts, absorbing the fluids of 
the mixture.
Because of the artificial nature of the necrophidius, they are 
never encountered in a lair or as a wandering monster. Instead, 
they are created for a specific purpose, usually as an assassin or 
guard. They are perfect for this role because they never sleep, 
they never check morale, and they never give up.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Death worms move completely silently, and surprise 
prey on a 1-3 on 1d6.
Immunities and Resistances
Necromantic Construct. This creature is necromantic but not “un-
dead,” so cannot be turned by a cleric.
Creature of Necromancy. This creature is immune to all illusions, 
mind control (including charm) and hold spells. It cannot be af-
fected by sleep, gases, poison, or disease.
Special Attacks
Dance of Death . This attack is a swaying, hypnotic dance that 
dominates the attention of anyone who fails a saving throw vs 
petrifaction. An individual so dominated will stand completely 
motionless and unresisting as the necrophidius advances and 
attacks. The victim does not defend (no shield or dex bonus) 
but is not considered fully paralysed for purposes of immedi -
ate killing.
Paralytic Bite. A successful bite attack inflicts a magical paralysis 
upon the victim for 1d4 turns, unless the target makes a saving 
throw vs. paralysis.
Unarmed Combat. Necrophidius cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
===== PAGE 208 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 207
NEREID
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 10 [10]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS None
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 100%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 65
LOOT Cache 10
EXPERIENCE 150 +4/hp
Nereids are transparent and invisible in water. On land these 
fey creatures appear as young, slim humans. They are playful 
and capricious, but a few (10%) are actually evil. An equally 
small number are good.
A nereid will always have a diaphanous shawl containing the 
creature’s spirit essence. Anyone holding the shawl can control 
the nereid by threatening the shawl, but the nereid will use any 
means possible to recover it.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Control Water. As fey creatures, neireids can control the water 
of their homes, speeding or slowing swimming movement 
through it (up to double normal speed, or down to 25% of it), 
causing crashing waves that deafen, prevent conversation and 
interrupt spell-casting, or forming miniature waterspouts that 
attack as 4 HD monsters (inflicting 1d4hp damage—no more 
than one such waterspout per nereid per round).
Special Attacks
Kiss. The kiss of a nereid can cause the recipient to drown in 
air and also in water. This requires a to-hit roll unless the kiss 
is voluntarily received. If it hits, a saving throw vs spells is re -
quired, and if the saving throw fails, the victim will drown in 
1d4 rounds unless the magic is dispelled.
Spit. In extremis, a nereid can attack with their spittle (range 
20ft) that blinds its target for 1d12 rounds. This requires a to-
hit roll but no saving throw is allowed.
Unarmed Combat.  Nereids can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks if out of the water (this is a terrible idea 
since the kiss can be used while grappling). If not fended off, 
they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/
DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling 
Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
NIGHT HAG
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 10 [10]
HIT DICE 8
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (2d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect 
invisible 5%
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 85
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE 1,850 +10/hp
Night hags are not creatures of the material plane of existence 
(and are thus affected by protection from evil), being native to 
the neutral evil plane of Hades. They resemble hideous wom -
en with clawed hands and feet. Night hags hunt in the material 
planes. This “hunt” is to slay evil people, whose souls are carried 
back to Hades by the hag, where the soul will form into a lar -
val fiend—a valuable commodity in the demonic and diabolical 
planes.
If success seems likely, they will also attack any good-aligned 
creature. 
A night hag carries a special periapt forged in Hades, allowing 
her to astrally project her body at will. Upon losing the periapt, 
she can still depart the plane she is in at the time. This periapt 
gives the possessor +2 on all saving throws, and cures diseases 
the possessor contracts. With each use by a good creature, the 
periapt decays; after ten uses, it vanishes.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (65%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 65% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immunities. Night hags are immune to charm, fear, sleep, and 
cold- or fire-based spells. 
Immune to Non-Silver Mundane, +1, and +2 Weapons.  This crea -
ture is immune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from 
weapons and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing 
that damage has a magical bonus of +3 or more, or is made 
of silver.
Special Features
Know Alignment. A night hag knows the alignment of any crea -
ture it sees.
Gate. A night hag can try to gate in an ally (50% chance of suc -
cess), summoning a barbed devil or class A demon (equal 
chance of one or the other). The expected reward is one of the 
hag’s larval fiends, so hags tend not to gate in allies unless they 
are in dire danger.
===== PAGE 209 =====
208 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: polymorph self, enter/exit ethereal plane (see Section 
1.6.10.6: Ethereal in the Player Guide).
3/day: magic missile (2d8 damage), ray of enfeeblement
Special Attacks
Sleep and Dream-Ride. Finding a victim, a night hag casts a sleep 
spell that affects humans (up to 12th level) who fail a saving 
throw vs magic, then strangles the sleeper. If the spell fails, she 
returns nightly while ethereal and enters the victim’s dreams, 
making them ethereal. Each ride until dawn by the hag (who 
cannot be unseated from the unfortunate’s back) permanently 
drains a point of constitution. When all points are drained, the 
victim dies and the night hag carries the soul to Hades.
Unarmed Combat. Night hags can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-6. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+0/-0.
NIGHTMARE
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 150ft; 360ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS -4 [24]
HIT DICE 6+6
MELEE ATTACKS 2 hooves (1d6+4) and 1 bite 
(2d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 50%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 80
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 525 +8/hp
Nightmares serve as mounts for powerful evil beings. Whilst in 
shape they resemble large warhorses, they are unmistakably 
otherworldly, having blood red eyes, fiery nostrils, powerful 
fangs, a midnight black coat, a mane of shadow and hooves that 
appear to leap aflame when they run. 
Although most nightmares do not possess wings, they can fly 
through the air considerably faster than they can run along the 
ground. Their capacity for such swift flight is greatly valued by 
those they serve, as is their ferocity in combat. Nightmares hate 
living creatures and, being given to viciousness, will generally 
attack any non-evil entity they encounter. 
Nightmares have a strong sense of purpose and this can make 
them difficult to control; it takes an iron will or powerful magic 
for a rider to perpetually retain mastery. It is thought that their 
services can also be bargained for, but exactly what evil such an 
agreement might entail is unknown.
If ridden by a creature weighing more than 100 lbs, the night -
mare’s aerial agility level is reduced to III.
Special Features
Smoke Aura. Nightmares give off wisps of noxious smoke, caus-
ing anyone within ten feet to make a saving throw vs paralysis 
or suffer a -2 penalty on “to hit” and damage rolls while in the 
radius of effect.
Special Attacks
Flaming Hooves. A hit by a nightmare’s hooves may set flamma -
ble objects on fire. 
Unarmed Combat.  Nightmares cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
Language
Though it is known that nightmares can understand what their 
riders ask of them, they are not thought to be able to speak 
themselves; but they seem to be able to communicate with one 
another well enough.
NIXIE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d10
SIZE Small (4ft tall)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft; 120ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1d4 hp
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (by weapon)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 95%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 50
LOOT Hoard 3, Cache 3
EXPERIENCE 35 +1/hp

===== PAGE 210 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 209
Nixies are fey creatures, related to sprites, that make their 
homes in freshwater lakes. They appear as strangely beautiful 
humanoids with lightly scaled greenish skin and webbed hands 
and feet. Their eyes are silver and their hair is dark green, and 
they garb themselves in clothes of seaweed. Being fey, nixies 
take great delight in enslaving humans. 
Nixies are weak in combat but make up for this weakness by 
attacking en masse and overwhelming their enemies. They fa -
vour fighting with daggers (1d4 damage) and short spears (1d6 
damage) which can be thrown.
Nixies fear bright light and fire; their aquatic nature makes 
these things foreign to them and a strong presentation of either 
will drive them away. A strong light source presented underwa-
ter will be obscured by schools of summoned fish surrounding 
the spell effect. 
Nixies lair at the bottom of lakes, where they weave living sea -
weed into dwellings. These dwellings blend in with the under -
water growth so well they are 90% unlikely to be noted until 
within 20ft of them. These underwater villages are guarded by 
either 1d4 giant gar (25%) or 2d4 giant pike (75%) which will 
obey commands from the nixies (see: Fish, Giant). Nixies can 
venture onto dry land but do so only with great reluctance.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (25%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 25% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Confer Water-Breathing (1/day). Nixies can confer the ability to 
breathe water on another being, with duration of 24 hours.
Summon Fish (3/day).  Nixies can summon 20d4 small fish to 
obstruct both an invader’s vision and movement by massing 
upon them. Vision is reduced to 10ft, and underwater move -
ment is reduced by half.
Special Attacks
Group Charm.  If a human or demi-human approaches within 
30ft of a group of nixies they will attempt to charm him or her 
with a special group charm spell. This charm requires a min -
imum of 10 nixies to join hands and chant, and any person 
hearing this fey chanting must save vs spells at -2 or enter the 
water to serve the nixies for a period of a year and a day. It 
should be noted: a side effect of this charm grants the ability to 
breathe water for the duration of the spell. There is a brief op-
portunity to break the charm; if a dispel magic is cast upon the 
enthralled person before they enter the water there is a 75% 
the spell will be broken, but once the victim has begun breath-
ing water the chances of breaking the charm drop to only 10%.
Unarmed Combat.  Out of water, nixies can be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score 
an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-
4. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF -2/+2.
Language
Nixies speak their own language and the common tongue. They 
also appear to be able to communicate, at least on a rudimen -
tary level, with freshwater fish of all types.
NYMPH
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 9 [11]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d2 or by weapon)
SENSES Keen
LAIR CHANCE 95%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 60
LOOT Cache 3 (x10), Cache 10
EXPERIENCE 350 +3/hp
Nymphs are nature spirits of almost indescribable beauty, 
and are always female; it is said that even the mere glimpse of 
one is enough to rob a mortal of their sight, or perhaps even 
cause death. They typically inhabit natural places of particular 
loveliness, such as high mountain lakes or deep forest glades. 
The key thing about being damaged by seeing a nymph isn’t 
about gender (as described in the original rules), it is about the 
Ancient Greek concept of disrespect to the gods. Thus, an elf 
(being fey) might be immune to the effect of laying eyes on a 
nymph. Any human—not just males—are affected by the sight, 
because nymphs are creatures not meant by the gods to be 
looked upon.
Nymphs abhor evil and will occasionally aid someone in dis -
tress. 
A lock of nymph’s hair woven into a cloak or other item of cloth-
ing will magically increase the charisma of those who wear it by 
one point. Alternatively, the lock of hair may be used to create 
a powerful potion of sleep. Nymph tears are sometimes used in 
the creation of a philtre of love and any person who bathes in a 
nymph’s pool will have their charisma increased by two points 
until sundown.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Dimension Door (1/day). Nymphs can cast dimension door once 
per day.
Spells. A nymph casts spells as a 7th level druid.
===== PAGE 211 =====
210 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Not to be Looked Upon. Any character that chances to look upon 
a clothed nymph must make a saving throw vs spells or be per-
manently blinded. Should a nymph be seen unclothed, then 
the consequence of a failed saving throw is death. The nymph 
can “turn off” this effect if she wishes to communicate or be 
seen safely. As with medusas, a mirror can provide protection 
from the sight of a nymph.
Unarmed Combat.  Nymphs can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Re -
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +0/+0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+0/+0.
Language
Nymphs typically speak common in addition to the language of 
the fey (dryads, etc.).
O.
OCTOPUS, GIANT
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 180ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 8
MELEE ATTACKS 8 tentacles (1d4) and 1 bite 
(2d6)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Cache 4
EXPERIENCE 500 +10/hp
Occasionally a giant octopus will surface to attack a passing 
ship. They will attach themselves with two tentacles to the ship, 
leaving the other 6 tentacles free to go after the crew. When the 
squid has picked the ship clean it will crush the hull and drag the 
wreckage to its lair.
If the octopus loses more than 3 arms it will release the ship and 
flee. As it does, the monster will release ink in the water leaving 
a cloud 60ft deep by 80ft wide.
Special Attacks
Grab and Constrict.  With a successful hit, the octopus grabs its 
victim and holds it. The tentacle does an automatic 2d4 points 
of blunt damage each round after a successful grab (only 1d4 
on the initial hit). It takes one round to eat a dead victim, then 
the tentacle is free to attack a new target. The only way to es -
cape is to sever the tentacle (inflict 10 hp damage to it, which 
doesn’t affect the creature’s total hp). When the tentacle hits, 
roll 1d100 to determine how well it is holding the victim.
• 01-25: Both arms are held and the victim cannot attack.
• 26-75: One limb is held and the victim can attack with a -3 
penalty. 
• 76-00: Both arms are free and the victim can attack with only 
a -1 penalty.
OGRE
COMMON OGRE MAGE (ONI)
FREQUENCY Common Rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 2d10 1d6
SIZE Large (9ft+) Large (9ft+)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft 90ft; 150ft flying 
(AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15] 4 [16]
HIT DICE 4+1 5+2
MELEE ATTACKS 1d10 or weapon 1d12 or by weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20% 25%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7) Average (8-10) to 
high (13-14)
MORALE 70 75
LOOT
Individual 4 each; in 
lair Hoard 2, Cache 
3 (x10), Cache 5
Cache 4, 5, and 35% 
chance of 4 magic 
items (any) plus a 
scroll
EXPERIENCE 95 +5/hp 750 +6/hp
Ogres are hulking creatures, nine feet tall, although they are 
not true giants. Their faces are more feral and less human-like 
than a giant’s. Ogres live in all environments, whether above or 
below ground. They have affinity with hill giants and trolls, and 
often can be found with them. Ogres love wealth, and will hire 
themselves out to other creatures toward this end—although 
ogre mercenaries tend to get badly out of hand unless tightly 
controlled. Left to themselves, ogres are quite primitive, but if 
an ogre mage or another dominant non-ogre leader (such as 
a necromancer or giant) is in charge, they can be quite well-
equipped.
In a group of 11 or more ogres, one is a leader and is slightly 
more powerful (attack capability equal to a 7 HD monster, 33 hit 
points, AC 3 [17], and damage of 2d6). Groups of 16 or greater 
ogres will have two of these leaders, as well as an ogre chief. 
The chief also attacks as a 7 HD monster, but inflicts 1d10+4 hit 
points of damage and has an AC of 4 [16]. 
===== PAGE 212 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 211
When encountering ogres in their lair, the lair will contain slaves 
or prisoners 30% of the time. Anyone captured by ogres has a 
75% chance to be eaten within a few days of captivity.
Special Attacks
Weapon Damage Bonus.  When attacking with a weapon, ogres 
inflict +2 to damage, leader-types add +3 and a chief adds +4.
Unarmed Combat. Ogres can be targeted with grappling or over-
bearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit 
with a 12+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-2. Overbear Results 
Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2.
Language
Despite their low intelligence, ogres are social creatures and 
speak orc, troll, and the language of hill giants in addition to 
their own language and alignment tongue.
OGRE MAGE
Ogre Magi are fearsome evil creatures, well versed in magic and 
of unnatural size and strength. They are clearly related to ordi -
nary ogres, standing around nine or ten feet tall, with the feral 
face and thick muscles of a normal ogre. Unlike normal ogres, 
their skin colour is usually bright yellow, blue, or red. Most have 
two or more yellow horns, and short tusks. 
Though ogre magi are physically powerful, it is their command 
of magic that makes them truly dangerous adversaries. 
Ogre magi are not numerous and tend towards small groups 
living in castles or well-guarded and luxurious caves. Groups of 
5 or 6 are led by particularly powerful individuals with 30–42 
hp who fight and save as though they had 9 hit dice. These are 
leader-type monsters who almost always have underlings: their 
lairs are generally defended and often guarded by lesser beings 
(including normal ogres).
Immunities and Resistances
Regeneration. Ogre magi regenerate 1 hp per combat round un-
til they are killed, at which point the regeneration stops.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: fly (12 turn duration), invisibility, darkness, 10ft radius  
and polymorph self (limited to humanoid forms 4ft to 12ft in 
size). 
1/day: charm person, sleep, gaseous form, and cone of cold as a 
12th level spell caster.
Unarmed Combat. Ogre magi can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed 
hit with a 14+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-2. Overbear Re-
sults Mods: ATK/DEF +4/-4. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF 
+2/-2.
Language
Ogre magi speak common and ogrish, amongst other languag -
es.
ORC
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 30d10
SIZE Medium (6ft tall)
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 weapon
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 35%
INTELLIGENCE Average or Low (5-10)
MORALE 50
LOOT Individual 3 each; in lair Hoard 
3, Cache 1, 3 (x10), and 5
EXPERIENCE 10 +1 per hp
Orcs are subterranean humanoid creatures, with a snout and 
small tusks like a wild boar. Virtually all orcs are of lawful evil 
alignment (see Group Alignment for Monsters). This is a rein -
carnating-spirit species whose form attracts lawful evil spirits 
for reincarnation just as the elvish form attracts chaotic good 
spirits. Despite the predilection for evil-aligned spirits to inhabit 
orcs, there are a few orcs who are not of evil alignment, because 
the life-activating spirit changed alignment before or after re -
birth into an orcish body. These are never found with other 
orcs, however, because if detected they are usually immediate-
ly killed in the lawful and evil society of orcs—the inability to 
communicate in the group’s alignment tongue makes detection 
almost inevitable.

===== PAGE 213 =====
212 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
The ”lawful” aspect of orcish alignment is fairly specific. First, 
orcs are fanatically loyal to a particular horde, “horde” being a 
concept which is abstract enough to gain the “lawful” descrip -
tor. Second, they consider a strong leadership hierarchy to be 
absolutely necessary. Third, they consider orcs (as a species and 
more specifically their own horde) to be the supreme natural 
rulers of everything they survey. If it were not for these three 
points, orcs could easily be seen as chaotic. They do not form 
wider nations even among orcs, they have no respect for learn-
ing or non-warrior technology, and they destroy civilisations 
they conquer rather than assimilating them. They are “lawful” 
only within the framework of orcish species-superiority. Pow -
erful non-orcish creatures (such as an ogre mage or dragon) 
can easily take control of orcs as long as they keep an orcish 
horde-chieftain in place as a puppet. Orcs love to follow tyran -
nical leaders as long as they are promised plunder, and as long 
as they seem to have authority themselves (every orc needs 
someone to bully). The moment they lose fear of a non-orcish 
supreme leader, though, they will riot or depart seeking plun -
der, often replacing whatever horde leader allowed them to be 
“deceived” by the deal they were offered.
Orcs are fiercely loyal to a single horde, and these hordes are in 
fierce, often-violent competition. They will only unite with other 
hordes if there is an external force strong enough to be able to 
control the horde chieftains.
Orcs are skilled miners and cavers. They have the ability to note 
new or unusual constructions underground 35% of the time, 
and to spot sloping passages 25% of the time.
Most orcish lairs will be found underground, and those found 
above ground will consist of simple huts, typically with some 
kind of defence (ditch, moat, crude walls, etc.). Above-ground 
orcs prefer to sleep during the day and roam by night.
Leaders
Wandering orcish war–parties are led by a captain and the cap-
tain’s personal guards (3d6). These orcs fight as 2 HD creatures. 
They have AC 4 [16], 11 hp, and inflict +1 damage.
Orc chiefs will always be encountered in a lair. The chief and the 
chief’s personal guards (of which there are 5d6) fight as 3 HD 
monsters. They have AC 4 [16], 15 hp, and inflict 2d4 damage.
Immunities and Resistances
Vulnerability to Sunlight. Orcs fight at -1 to hit when they are in 
direct sunlight.
Special Attacks
Ranged Weapons. Orcs can use ranged weapons. Usually 25% of 
an orcish group will have short bows or crossbows.
Unarmed Combat. Orcs can engage in grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 
10+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-4. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
Language
Orcs speak goblin and hobgoblin in addition to their own foul 
tongue.
OWLBEAR
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4+1
SIZE Large (8ft tall)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 5+2
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d6) and 1 bite (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 225 +8/hp
These nightmarish creatures have the body of a large bear with 
the head of an owl. They are voracious eaters and possessed of 
a foul temper, fighting to the death. They are typically found in 
temperate forests.
Special Attacks
Hug. If an owlbear scores a natural 18 or better on a hit with 
either of its paws, it has grabbed the victim in a crushing hug. 
The victim takes an additional 2d8 points of blunt damage that 
round and the owlbear inflicts an automatic 2d8 points every 
following round until the owlbear is slain.
Unarmed Combat. Owlbears cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.

OTYUGH
LESSER GREATER
FREQUENCY Uncommon Rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1d2 1
SIZE Medium Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 60ft 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17] 0 [20]
HIT DICE 6 to 8 10 to 12
MELEE ATTACKS 2 tentacles (1d8) 
and 1 bite (1d4+1)
2 tentacles (1d12) 
and 1 bite (1d4+1)
SENSES Infravision 90ft Infravision 90ft
LAIR CHANCE 75% 75%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7) Average (8-10)
MORALE 75 to 85 90 to 105
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE
6 HD: 275 +6/hp 
7 HD: 400 +8/hp 
8 HD: 650 +10/hp
10 HD: 1,500 +14/hp 
11 HD: 2,000 +16/hp 
12 HD: 2,300 +17/hp
Otyughs are repulsive beasts that feed off the refuse and waste 
habitually discarded by other creatures. They are generally 
found in large rubbish pits and cisterns. Not always content with 
discards, they sometimes wait beneath the surface for anyone 
foolish enough to stray too close. Otyughs have no discernible 
head, the bulk of their form consisting of a great horn-plated 
torso supported by three stocky legs, with a powerful, sharp-
toothed maw. Three long tentacular appendages extend from 
their bodies, two of which are hard and thorny, being used to 
attack and grapple prey; the third is actually a sensory organ 
with three eyes at its end. 
Whilst Otyughs suffer no ill-effects from bright light, they do not 
like it, preferring to dwell in dark places that help them to strike 
with complete surprise, though their eyes, usually protruding 
above the surface, sometimes give them away. 
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Otyughs cannot be surprised, and surprise others on 
a roll of 1-3 on a d6.
Special Features
Limited Telepathy. Lesser Otyughs are capable of limited telep -
athy at a range of up to 30ft, and Greater Otyughs up to 60 ft. 
Such communication is limited to simple thoughts and ideas.
Special Attacks
Disease. Any character unfortunate enough to be bitten by an 
Otyugh may catch a disease (saving throw vs. poison negates). 
If contracted, the disease has a 20% chance to be fatal within 
1d8 days; if it is not fatal, the patient recovers fully.
Grab. Otyughs can use their tentacles for normal attacks, but 
may also use them to grab opponents. Grabbing an opponent 
only requires a successful to-hit roll, and normal damage is in-
flicted in that round. In later rounds, the otyugh inflicts dam -
age automatically, but less than it might inflict by making an 
attack roll. Grabbed opponents suffer 1d3+1 points of blunt 
damage per round, and bite attacks against them are made 
with +2 to hit. A character with 18 strength can break free af -
ter one round of struggling, but other characters must make 
a successful Minor Test of Strength die roll to do the same. 
Grabbed characters cannot attack until freed. Greater Otyughs 
may also use grabbed creatures as a shield; in doing so, they 
gain a +1 bonus to armour class.
Unarmed Combat. Otyughs cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
P.
PEGASUS
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d10
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Chaotic good
MOVE 240ft; 480ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 2 hooves (1d8 blunt) and 1 
bite (1d3 piercing)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 15%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 65
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 75 +3/hp
A pegasus is a winged horse who lives in secluded areas far 
from civilisation. They are intelligent, but very wild and un -
tamed. They do not trust humans and must be approached very 
carefully. If they can be caught and tamed, they become very 
devoted mounts. They will, however, only serve a good-aligned 
master. If mounted by a creature weighing more than 100 lbs, 
the pegasus’ aerial agility level decreases to III.
A pegasus is equal in size and strength to a medium warhorse. 
Unarmed Combat. Pegasi cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, but may choose to overbear a lone oppo -
nent. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 
1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/
DEF +5/na.

PHASE CAT (COEURL)
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 6+6
MELEE ATTACKS 2 tentacles (2d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 80
LOOT Hoard 4
EXPERIENCE 400 +6/hp
Inspired by the work of A. E. Van Vogt, phase cats (coeurls) are 
feline predators originating from a place alien in space or time, 
although probably from the prime material plane since they are 
not affected by protection from evil. They resemble jaguar-sized 
black cats with a pair of tentacles that can be used to rip at their 
foes. The oddest thing about phase cats is that they appear to 
be in a slightly different place than they actually are, making 
them difficult to target. 
Phase cats hate blink dogs, and will hunt them down and kill 
them if possible.
Immunities and Resistances
Improved Saving Throw. Phase cats make all saving throws with a 
+6 bonus to the die roll.
Special Features
Displacement. Phase cats are out of phase with reality, and their 
visual appearance is slightly off from their actual location. This 
is already taken into account  with the armour class and saving 
throw.
Unarmed Combat. Phase cats cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
Language
Phase cats can communicate telepathically, though they use 
this to coordinate with each other. They seldom bother to com-
municate with beings other than phase cats.
PIERCER
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 3d6
SIZE Small to Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 10ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 1 to 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 pierce (1d6, 2d6, 3d6 or 4d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE
1 HD: 10 +1/hp
2 HD: 30 +2/hp
3 HD: 50 +3/hp
4 HD: 80 +4/hp
A piercer looks exactly like a stalactite, but it is a living creature 
encased in a stone covering. Piercers are sensitive to noise and 
heat; when they detect a source of either passing beneath them 
they drop, seeking to kill and devour their target.
A piercer has a single attack, seeking to impale the unlucky vic -
tim with its sharp point. After killing and feasting the piercer will 
move via tiny cilia in its base back up to the ceiling. Any grouping 
of piercers will include a mix of the various HD, roll 1d4 with 1 = 
1 HD, 2 = 2 HD, 3 = 3 HD, and 4 = 4 HD. A piercer will do 1d6 of 
damage for each of its HD.
The 1 HD piercer is 3ft long and weighs about 200 lbs. With each 
additional HD the piercer adds another foot of length and an -
other 100 lbs of weight. Piercers devour even the bones of their 
victims and any intelligent cave or dungeon dwelling denizens 
living nearby will often (carefully) scavenge any armour, weap-
ons, money, magic, or anything else remaining of even the least 
value.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Piercers cannot be surprised, and have a 95% chance 
to attack with 1 segment of surprise whenever someone walks 
underneath them.
Immunities and Resistances
Cannot be Prone. Piercers are unaffected by being prone.
===== PAGE 216 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 215
PIXIE
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 5d4
SIZE Tiny (2ft)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft; 120ft flying (AA:V)
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 1d4hp
MELEE ATTACKS 1 dagger (1d4) or short bow 
(special)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 50
LOOT Cache 4, 5, 6, 10
EXPERIENCE 50 +1/hp
Pixies are a type of troublesome fey creatures who enjoy play -
ing tricks and generally annoying anyone who passes through 
their territory. They like to dwell in secluded forests. Unlike 
many fey, pixies to not capture humans, but they put intruders 
to sleep, remove their memory, and move them away. Repeat 
trespassers may be killed.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (25%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 25% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Invisibility. Pixies are normally invisible and can stay invisible 
even when they attack. Unless the pixie turns visible, or they 
are otherwise detected, opponents roll “to hit” at -4.
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: Pixies can make themselves visible or polymorph 
themselves at will. They are also able to create illusions with 
both sight and sound that do not have to be controlled. The 
illusion lasts until it is touched or dispelled, but they do not 
cause damage. 
1/day: Dispel magic (as 8th level caster), dancing lights, and ESP.
Special Attacks
Ranged Attack (Magic Arrows).  Their bows shoot three different 
kinds of arrows at +4 to hit. The first type of arrow is a standard 
type that causes 1d4+1 points of damage. The second type of 
arrow causes sleep for 1d6 hours unless the target saves vs 
magic. The third type of arrow causes total amnesia if the tar -
get fails their save vs magic. The only way to regain memory is 
to receive an exorcism.
A pixie’s touch causes confusion in anyone who fails their save 
vs magic. The confusion will last until they receive a remove 
curse.
Unarmed Combat. Pixies cannot meaningfully be attacked in this 
way, and they don’t use this sort of tactic.
Language
Pixies speak their own language, the language of the fey, and 
common.
POLTERGEIST
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d8
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 10 [10]
HIT DICE 1d4  hit points
MELEE ATTACKS Nil
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 97%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 35 +1/hp
Poltergeists are undead creatures: non-corporeal, invisible spir-
its of humans who died a tragic death or were murdered in cold 
blood. So far as is known, all poltergeists were formerly human 
or at least half-human. They are only rarely encountered as a 
wandering monster but in such a case can be treated as a type 1 
undead for purposes of turning or disrupting. More often, how-
ever, the poltergeist is in the area where it met its untimely end. 
The poltergeist’s bond to this area is strong and turning is much 
more difficult, treat the poltergeist as a turning a type 3 undead 
in this case.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Poltergeists are turned as type 1 or 3 Un -
dead.
Immune to Non-Silver Mundane Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +1 or more or is silver.
Unusual Undeath. A poltergeist will not cross over sprinkled holy 
water, but holy water does not cause actual harm to them. 
Undeath. This creature is immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by sleep, gases, 
poison, or disease.
Special Features
Invisibility. Poltergeists are naturally invisible and can stay invisi-
ble even when they attack. Unless affected by a detection spell, 
opponents make to-hit rolls at -4.
===== PAGE 217 =====
216 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Special Attacks
Telekinesis and Fear.  Poltergeists cannot physically attack. In -
stead, they hurl objects with a telekinetic power at their oppo-
nents (lamps, books, etc.), attacking as a 5 HD monster. Any -
one struck takes no damage, but must make a saving throw vs 
spells or flee at top movement speed for 2d12 rounds. A flee -
ing victim has a 50% chance to drop whatever they were hold-
ing in their hands. Once an opponent has made a successful 
saving throw, they are immune to further fear from that indi -
vidual poltergeist, and only for the duration of the encounter.
Unarmed Combat.  Poltergeists cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
PORTUGUESE MAN O’ WAR, GIANT
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d10
SIZE Variable
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 10ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 9 [11]
HIT DICE 1 to 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d10 poison damage + paralysis)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE
1 HD: 45 +1/hp
2 HD: 80 +2/hp
3 HD: 110 +3/hp
4 HD: 150 +4/hp
The giant Portuguese man o’war is a translucent jellyfish found 
in warm salt water, where it floats on the surface, trailing its 
poisonous tentacles below. The size is determined by hit dice: 
the body will be 2-1/2ft in diameter for every HD. The 1 and 2HD 
varieties are size small, the 3HD variety is size medium, and the 
4HD variety is size large.
Special Features
Transparent. Roll d100 for anyone fighting the jellyfish; if the re-
sult is 01-89, the jellyfish is treated as being invisible to that 
person.
Special Attacks
Paralysing Sting.  In addition to the base 1d10 poison damage 
from the sting, anyone hit by the jellyfish must make a sav -
ing throw vs. paralysis or be paralysed for 3d6 turns. Only a 
person immune to poison can avoid the base 1d10 points of 
poison damage. Paralysed creatures are drawn up to the body 
of the jellyfish and consumed in 3d4 turns.
PSEUDO-DRAGON
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Small (18-in)
ALIGNMENT Neutral good
MOVE 60ft; 240ft flying (AA:V)
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d3) and 1 sting 
(poison)
SENSES Infravision 60ft (and sees 
invisibility)
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 55
LOOT Cache 3 (x10)
EXPERIENCE 200 +2/hp
Pseudo-dragons are tiny dragons, dark red in colour, lacking 
the breath weapon of their large cousins but carrying a deadly 
sting in their tails. They are not generally counted among the 
true dragons. Pseudo-dragons can be be found in almost every 
climate save the very hottest and coldest, and prefer snug lairs 
such as small caves or hollow trees.
Pseudo-dragons are highly-prized familiars, and in some cases 
they will use their abilities on behalf of an ordinary companion 
of good alignment.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (35%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 35% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect. This magic resistance 
may also be extended to any ally in physical contact with the 
pseudo dragon, if the pseudo dragon desires.
See Invisible.  Pseudo dragons can see invisible creatures and 
objects.
Special Features
Chameleon. Pseudo-dragons can change their normal dark red 
colouration to match their surroundings. Any person not able 
to see invisible creatures must roll 1d100, and on a roll of 01-
80, the pseudo dragon is treated as being invisible to that per-
son.
Limited telepathy.  Pseudo-dragons are also able to communi -
cate everything they see and hear to a companion, via a limited 
form of telepathy at distances of up to 240ft.
Special Attacks
Poison Sting. The pseudo-dragon has a poison sting on its tail. 
Tail attacks have a +4 to-hit bonus but do not inflict damage. 
Any creature stung must save vs poison or fall into a death-like 
trance for 1d6 days. On the last day of the trance there is a 
===== PAGE 218 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 217
25% chance the victim will actually die, but if they survive, they 
awaken normally.
Unarmed Combat. Pseudo-dragons cannot effectively be target-
ed with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
PURPLE WORM
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Huge
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6
HIT DICE 15
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (2d12 piercing) and 1 tail sting 
(2d4 piercing+poison)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Hoard 2, Cache 3 (x5) and 10
EXPERIENCE 5,000 +20/hp
Purple worms are huge, almost 10ft around and 40ft to 50ft 
long. They live deep underground where they are constantly on 
the search for food. They are attracted to vibrations up to 60ft 
away. These creatures always return to their lair to rest. During 
this time, they will pass their waste consisting of metals and 
gems they could not digest.
Immunities and Resistances
Prone. This creature cannot be knocked prone.
Special Attacks
Swallow Whole. On a bite attack that rolls a natural 20, the pur -
ple worm swallows the victim. If the victim is not rescued, 
they will die at the end of 6 rounds spent in the creature’s 
stomach. It is possible for the victim to cut their way out (the 
inside of the worm is armour class 10 [10]). For each round 
the victim is inside, subtract 1 from their damage that they 
inflict. This penalty is cumulative for each round, so after four 
rounds the damage inflicted is at -4.
Poison Stinger.  Any creature hit by the worm’s tail takes 2d4 
damage and must save vs poison or die.
R.
RAKSHASA
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS -4 [24]
HIT DICE 7
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d3 slashing) and 1 
bite (1d4+1 piercing)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 80
LOOT Hoard 6
EXPERIENCE 1,000 +8/hp
Rakshasas are evil spirits embodied in flesh. Their main diet is 
humans, although they do not need to eat often. They hunt by 
deception: when first encountered, a rakshasa will use its ESP 
to gain an image of someone the victim trusts, then use illusion 
to assume that form. Once the victim lets their guard down the 
rakshasa will reveal its true self and attack. 
Immunities and Resistances
Level-Based Magic Resistance. Rakshasas are only affected by 8th 
level spells and higher. 
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from mundane weapons.
Resistant to +1 and +2 Weapons.  The rakshasa takes only half 
damage from magic weapons unless the weapon is +3 or bet -
ter.
Vulnerability. A crossbow bolt blessed by a cleric kills a rakshasa 
instantly if it hits.
Special Features
ESP. A rakshasa can use ESP (as per the spell) at will.
Spells. Rakshasas have the spellcasting abilities of a level 5 mag-
ic-user and a 1st level cleric.
Unarmed Combat. Rakshasas cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks (immune to mundane weap -
ons), and do not use these tactics.

Giant rats of both size categories are vicious, opportunistic om-
nivores often found in ruins and upper dungeon levels. 
Special Attacks
Disease. Each successful bite from a giant rat has a 5% chance of 
causing a disease (as the cleric spell cause disease) unless the 
victim passes a saving throw vs poison.
REMORHAZ
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Huge (21ft to 42ft length)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS Body 0 [20]; head 2 [18]; underside 4 
[16]
HIT DICE 7 to 14 (roll 1d8+6)
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (6d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 80+
LOOT Hoard 6
EXPERIENCE
7 HD: 625 +8/hp 
8 HD: 950 +10/ hp 
9 HD: 1,400 +12/hp 
10 HD: 1,700 +13/hp 
11 HD: 2,100 +14/hp 
12 HD: 3,000 +16/hp 
13 HD: 3,500 +17/hp 
14 HD: 4,200 +18/hp
These great dragon-like creatures are found only in arctic ar -
eas. The remorhaz attacks on sight, and if encountered in its lair 
there is a 1 in 4 chance it has a mate and 1d3 eggs; the eggs can 
be sold on some markets for 5,000 gp each.
The HD of the remorhaz determines its length: starting at 21ft, 
each hit die above 7 adds 3ft, so that a 14 HD specimen will be 
roughly 42ft long. The remorhaz is ice-blue in colour, with vesti-
gial wings and odd, spiny protrusions along its back.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (75%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 75% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Heat-Spines. When in combat, the internal heat of the remorhaz 
seeps up into the spiny protrusions along the creature’s back. 
Non-magical weapons that strike the back will melt (although 
they will cause damage first), and any physical touch deals 
10d10 hp damage.
Sectioned Armour Class. The head of a remorhaz is AC 2 [18] and 
its underside is AC 4 [16]. It exposes its belly in front while at -
tacking. Anyone not in front of the remorhaz in combat will 
have to attack the armoured body, which is AC 0 [20].

===== PAGE 220 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 219
Special Attacks
Swallow Whole. The remorhaz can swallow its prey whole on a 
natural to-hit roll of 20. All remorhaz generate an intense in -
ternal heat that instantly destroys any swallowed opponent 
unless the opponent is immune to fire damage.
Unarmed Combat. Remorhaz cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
RHINOCEROS
COMMON WOOLLY
FREQUENCY Common Common
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1d6 1d4
SIZE Large Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 120ft 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] 5 [15]
HIT DICE 8 or 9 10
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (2d4 or 2d6) 1 (2d6)
SENSES Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil Nil
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE N/A 95
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 550 +10/hp 900 +12/hp
Rhinoceroses are aggressive herbivores that will charge any 
creature they can detect. Rhinos have poor eyesight but have 
a very good sense of smell and excellent hearing. There are 2 
common types of rhinos, single horn and double horned. Dou -
ble horned rhinos are larger, having 9 HD and doing 2d6 dam -
age. The single horn variety has 8 HD and does 2d4 damage.
A woolly rhinoceros is a larger version of the rhino that roams 
Arctic areas of “lost world” settings, though they can also be en-
countered in the wild.
Special Attacks
Charge. In combat a rhino will charge, doing double damage on 
a successful attack. 
Trample. Any opponent on the ground (prone) may be tram -
pled. Roll to hit for 2 foreleg attacks (2d4 blunt damage).
Unarmed Combat. Rhinoceroses cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
ROC
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Huge (60ft+ wingspan)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 30ft; 300ft flying (AA:II)
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 18
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (4d6) or 2 talons (3d6)
SENSES Detect invisible 10%
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 135
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 3,010 +25/hp
Rocs are enormous birds of prey living in high elevations, usual-
ly in warmer environments. They will sometimes be found with 
giants, who keep them as pets. The immense size of a roc is ac-
companied by an equally immense appetite: rocs hunt for large 
mammals including horses, cattle, and even elephants. 
Any treasure found in the gigantic nest of a roc is there purely 
by coincidence, since rocs have no concept of wealth. The be -
longings of past victims will be found woven into the intricate 
nest.
Special Attacks
Grab. If a roc hits with either of its talons, it can pick up any prey 
smaller than huge size, and fly off toward its nest to feed its 
young or devour the victim at leisure.

Unarmed Combat.  Ropers cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
ROPERS
Ropers are cavern-dwelling monsters and are frequently mis -
taken for stalagmites. These monsters are grey-green in colour, 
standing around 8ft to 12ft tall, with a hide that mimics the 
smooth limestone formations of a natural cavern.
Ropers are about 3ft to 4ft at the base and approximately 1ft 
wide at the apex. These monsters are almost always encoun -
tered in their stalagmite shape but can alter their appearance to 
some degree. A roper can assume the shape of a pillar, a boul -
der, or flatten themselves and lie flat to appear as no more than 
an irregularity on the walking surface of the cavern floor. It can 
also cling to a cavern ceiling (or wall) and appear as a stalactite. 
Through means of tiny adhesive cilia on its underside, the roper 
can move slowly and these cilia are what allow it to cling upside 
down to the ceiling. Ropers are predators and attack by means of 
the 6 rope-like appendages that give these monsters their name. 
Ropers do not hoard treasure, but their acidic bile cannot dis -
solve platinum or gemstones. Cutting open the gizzard of a rop-
er has a 40% chance of yielding 3d6 (3–18) platinum pieces and 
30% chance of 4d6 (4–24) gems.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (80%).  Any magic spell or effect has an 80% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Damage Resistance.  Ropers take only half damage from elec -
tricity and cold, even if such an attack gets past the creature’s 
magic resistance.
Vulnerability to Fire. Ropers are vulnerable to fire, although mag-
ical fire must still overcome the creature’s magic resistance to 
inflict damage. The roper’s saving throw against fire is at -4.
Special Attacks
Adhesive Tentacles. The tentacles of a roper are 50ft long and se-
crete a powerful and poisonous adhesive. A successful “to hit” 
roll grabs and weakens the target (no saving throw), decreas -
ing its strength ability score by 50% (rounded down) within 1d3 
rounds and lasting 2d4 turns. Multiple hits have a cumulative 
strength drain effect. 
• The victim is not prevented from attacking (arms are not pin-
ioned), but is dragged closer to the roper by 10ft per round.
• An ensnared victim can break the strand by performing a Mi-
nor Test of Strength (using the reduced strength score). 
• A strand can be sliced with an edged weapon but the attack 
must do a minimum of 6 points of damage in a single attack 
to the AC 0 [20] tentacle to sever it. 
• Creatures within 10ft of the roper are close enough for the 
roper to bite. The bite attack automatically hits any victim 
held by the strands of the roper. 
QUARTZ ROPERS
To adventurers familiar with ropers, a quartz roper appears as 
a 5ft tall and 2ft wide (at the base) statue of a roper, hewn from 
some brownish or smoky grey crystalline mineral. The quartz 
roper is actually a monster with a rocky hide composed of liv -
ing quartz. Quartz ropers are very sensitive to motion and are 
able to sense movement up to 225ft away. They do not have the 
magic or damage resistances of normal ropers.
In combat, quartz ropers fight in the same way as normal rop -
ers, although the quartz roper can inject a venom and the ten -
tacles are not as strong.
Treasure: A quartz roper’s gizzard has the same percentage 
chance of containing platinum pieces and gemstones as the 
standard variety of roper.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Missile Weapons.  Due to its stone-like skin, the crea -
ture takes no damage from missile weapons (including magical 
ones). Magic missile and other spells affect the creature nor -
mally.
Special Attacks
Charm-Venom Injection (2/day). If the quartz roper hits with a ten-
tacle, it can inject a strange venom twice per day. This venom 
allows no saving throw and causes the victim to freeze in place, 
looking as if they have been turned to stone. One round after 
this apparent petrifaction, the victim recovers, but is under the 
===== PAGE 222 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 221
delusion the quartz roper is a close friend and valued ally. These 
influenced adventurers will fight to protect the monster to the 
utmost of their abilities for the duration of effect of the venom 
(10 turns). If the quartz roper is killed before the venom expires, 
the deluded defenders will cease attacking and wander about 
aimlessly until the venom expires.
Adhesive Tentacles. The quartz roper attacks with adhesive ten -
tacles as a roper does. The only difference is the ability to inject 
charm venom (see above) and that the tentacles are not quite 
as strong as a normal roper’s. An ensnared victim can break the 
strand by performing a Minor Test of Strength (with the reduced 
score) but the chance of success is doubled. Thus, if the target 
number for a Minor Test of Strength is 1-2, the attempt succeeds 
on a 1-4. If the target number is 1-3, the attempt succeeds on a 
1-6 (automatically). The tentacle’s hit points for severing are the 
same as for a normal roper. 
ROT GRUB
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 5d4
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 10ft
ARMOUR CLASS 9 [11]
HIT DICE 1 hit point
MELEE ATTACKS 0 (See below)
SENSES N/A
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 5 +1/hp
Although rot grubs can be found in animal waste and other foul 
refuse, they prefer to consume tissue that is still alive. 
Special Attacks
Burrow. Upon contact with a living being, rot grubs attempt a to-
hit roll, and if successful will begin to vigorously burrow deep 
into the body. Fire must be applied to the site of contact at 
once (within 3 rounds) in order to prevent the rot grub from 
burrowing further. This application of flame inflicts 1d6 hit 
points of damage per rot grub burrow. If not stopped imme -
diately, within 1 to 3 turns the rot grub will find the heart and 
kill the victim.
RUST MONSTER
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Medium (5ft long)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 180ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 5
MELEE ATTACKS 2 (Special)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Smell iron
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Cache 3 (x10)
EXPERIENCE 185 +4 per hp
Rust monsters are creatures vaguely resembling a 5ft long and 
3ft tall giant, bug-like, armadillo that weighs about 200 lbs. Rust 
monsters have rust-red colouration on their dorsal hides and 
two prehensile antennae on their heads.
Rust monsters are only found in underground type environ -
ments, which they prowl constantly in search of food.
Rust monsters consume metal of any kind, especially ferrous 
metals and ferrous metal alloys. The antennae of a rust mon -
ster can “smell” metal 90ft away, and the creature will dart to -
ward such a source of food with blinding speed, attacking the 
largest piece of metal it can sense. 
Rust monsters are motivated by blind hunger, and can be easily 
distracted from pursuit by dropping metal objects and fleeing; 
some iron spikes or a heavy mace will cause the attacking rust 
monster to stop for 1 round to devour the treat. Otherwise, rust 
monsters will relentlessly pursue the PCs until slain or all metal 
items have been consumed.
Special Attacks
Rust. A successful antennae attack causes up to 10 cubicft of 
metal instantly to crumble into easily-digestible rust. The crea-
ture will immediately cease attacking in such a case and begin 
devouring its newly-created meal. Metal with magical bonuses 
gains a 10% chance per plus of not being affected by the rust 
monster’s attack. For instance a +3 shield would have a 30% of 
resisting the rusting effect. A successful “to hit” roll against a 
rust monster with a metal weapon automatically subjects that 
weapon to a rust attack.
Unarmed Combat. Rust monsters cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
===== PAGE 223 =====
222 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
S.
SAHUAGIN
NORMAL HATCHLING GUARD WARCHIEF

PRIESTESS BARON PRINCE KING
FREQUENCY Very rare Very rare Very rare Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4+1 1 1 1
SIZE Medium Medium Medium Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil Lawful evil Lawful evil Lawful evil
MOVE 120ft; 240ft swimming 120ft; 240ft swimming 20ft; 240ft swimming 120ft; 240ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15] 3 [17] 2 [18] 1 [19]
HIT DICE 2 to 6 6+6 8+8 10+10
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (By weapon) 1 (By weapon) 2 (By weapon) 4 (By weapon)
SENSES Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 100% 100% 100% 100%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16) High (13-14) High (13-14) Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE By HD 80 95 120
LOOT Individual 5 each Individual 5 each Individual 5 each Individual 5 each
EXPERIENCE Variable 300 +8/hp 500 +12/hp 1,250 +14/hp
Sahuagin are humanoid fish-people of evil alignment, much 
more shark-like in their appearance than the locathah. They 
dwell in shallow, warmer salt waters and raid villages and com-
munities on land for loot and sport. Sahuagin have been known 
to tame sharks and keep them as pets.
Each sahuagin realm has a King and is divided into nine prov -
inces, each ruled by a Prince. Each Prince will have a number 
of Barons under his command, and each Baron controls a war-
band. The “Number Encountered” listing for this creature is for 
the lair of a typical war-band; the lair of a Prince would be much 
larger.
If encountered outside their lair, there will be only normal sa -
huagin and guards. The band will be led by a warchief.

===== PAGE 224 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 223
Sahuagin are typically armed as follows:
• Dagger and spear: 25% of the band
• Dagger, trident and net: 50% of the band
• Dagger and heavy crossbow: 25% of the band
All these weapons are fully usable both above and below the 
water, which makes them quite highly prized.
Priestesses
The sahuagin clergy will always be led by a priestess with 6 HD 
and the spell casting powers of an 8th level cleric. The remain -
ing priestesses will be trainees of 2nd to 7th level (1d6+1). Their 
HD are equal to their spell casting level minus 2 (minimum 2). 
Sahuagin priestesses will typically be attended by zombie or 
skeleton servants, as they are fond of the animate dead spell.
Special Attacks
Ranged or Melee Attack (Net). Some sahuagin are armed with 
nets. These have a range of 20ft (and can be thrown while in 
melee combat). If the attack hits, the target is automatically 
(no saving throw) entangled for 1d4 rounds, unable to attack 
or move.
Unarmed Combat.  Sahuagin can engage in grappling or over -
bearing attacks when out of water. For a normal sahuagin, if 
not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. 
Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF 
+0/-0. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
SATYR (FAUN)
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 180ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 5
MELEE ATTACKS 1 gore (2d4 piercing)
SENSES Keen
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 70
LOOT Hoard 9, Cache 5 and 10
EXPERIENCE 110 +4/hp
A satyr looks like a man with goat legs, and their heads have 
small goat-horns as well. These creatures are invariably male. 
As they are fey creatures, they are probably capable of breeding 
with humans and with other types of fey.
Satyrs live in remote forests or meadows far away from civili -
sation. They are a frisky sort of fey creature, occupying most of 
their time with playing, flirting, and drinking. They value their 
privacy and will try to drive away any intruders from their do -
main. Satyrs have been known to ignore intruders from time to 
time, if given enough premium food and or wine.
Satyrs will not attack physically unless they themselves are 
threatened. Sometimes (20%) a satyr will carry a magic weapon.
Surprise and Initiative
Hard to Surprise. Being nature spirits, fauns are very tuned into 
their surroundings and are surprised only on a roll of 1 on a 
d6. (See also “Invisibility in Foliage,” below).
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Magic Pipes. A faun’s primary defence is its magic pipes that only 
it can play. With it the faun can cast charm, sleep, or fear. All 
spells have a 60ft radius and those within range must save vs 
magic or suffer the effects.
Invisibility in Foliage.  Satyrs magically blend in with their sur -
roundings, and each one has a 90% chance to be treated as 
invisible when hiding in foliage. This can be used for an am -
bush, but once the satyr is moving, it becomes visible unless 
it hides again.
Unarmed Combat. Satyrs can engage in grappling or overbearing 
attacks. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ 
on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +1/-1. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
Language
Satyrs can speak their own language and that of the fey, as well 
as elven and common. 
===== PAGE 225 =====
224 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Giant scorpions are vicious, fearless predators found almost 
anywhere. Their usual tactic is to attack anything smaller than 
themselves. The scorpion can use its attacks independently on 
3 different targets. Anything that the scorpion kills is taken back 
to its lair and consumed. 
Special Attacks
Poison Sting. Anyone hit by the stinger of a giant scorpion must 
make a saving throw vs. poison or die. Giant scorpions are not 
immune to poison, so if one stings another it can be lethal.
Unarmed Combat. Giant scorpions cannot effectively be target -
ed with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
SEA HAG
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft; 150ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d3+3) or 1 weapon 
(+3 damage)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 50%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 60
LOOT Hoard 3, Cache 11
EXPERIENCE 410 +2/hp
Sea hags typically make their lairs beneath the ocean depths, 
but they are occasionally found in large lakes or other signifi -
cant bodies of water. Their true form is that of a decrepit old 
woman, ravaged by time and repellent beyond reason, but they 
generally use their magic to assume a much more pleasant vis-
age. They have sharp claws and teeth, as well as an insatiable 
appetite for flesh. Most sea hags are thought to be capable of 
speaking a number of languages.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.

Immune to Non-Silver Mundane Weapons.  This creature is im -
mune to piercing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons 
and unarmed attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage 
has a magical bonus of +1 or more, or is a silver weapon. Cold 
iron weapons also affect this creature.
Immunities. Sea hags are immune to charm, fear, sleep and fire- or 
cold-based spells.
Special Features
Change Self (At Will). A sea hag can change self at will, with un -
limited duration.
Evil Gaze. A sea hag can employ an evil gaze up to three times 
per day that subjects one creature within thirty feet to a saving 
throw vs poison; failure results in immediate collapse and paral-
ysis for three days, though for 1 in 4 victims the effect is stronger 
and causes instant death.
Special Attacks
Horrific Appearance. Should a sea hag’s true appearance ever be 
revealed, then the horror causes anyone within thirty feet to be 
subject to a saving throw versus spells to avoid losing half their 
strength score for 1d6 turns.
Unarmed Combat.  Sea hags can engage in grappling or over -
bearing attacks if not in the water. If not fended off, they score 
an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +6/-
6. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
SEA LION
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 3d4
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 180ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15] or 3 [17]
HIT DICE 6
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d6) and 1 bite (2d6)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Semi (2-4)
MORALE 75
LOOT Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 150 +6/hp
Sea lions are not to be confused with the seal-like sealion. 
These are fantastical aquatic beasts that could be described as 
a mer-lion. They have the head and front legs of a lion, but a 
fish-like tail instead of rear legs, and they are entirely aquatic.
Special Features
Sectioned Armour Class. The sea lion’s head is AC 5 [15], but its 
scales protect the rest of the body better, having an AC of 3[17].
Unarmed Combat. Sea lions cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
SEA SERPENT
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Huge (50ft long)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 10
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d6 + poison) and 1 
constrict (3d6)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 95
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 1,000 +12/hp
Descriptions of sea serpents vary with the region in which they 
are found. Sometimes they appear as giant sea snakes and oth-
er times as dragon-like serpentine creatures with legs or flip -
pers. What is known for certain is that a sea serpent can be en-
countered in any ocean, sea, or large body of fresh water. Sea 
serpents are very territorial but are not otherwise aggressive, 
only attacking when hungry. Sea serpents are capable of div -
ing to great depths and staying underwater for long periods of 
time. They only lair in caves in very deep water, and tend to be 
solitary or, at most, a mated pair.
Special Attacks
Poison Bite. The sea serpent has a poisonous bite, causing death 
in 1d4 rounds (save vs poison to negate).
Constrict. After a successful to-hit roll, the coils of a sea serpent 
do automatic damage to the captured person or vessel. Crea -
tures take an automatic 3d6 blunt damage per round. Ships 
are crushed in 1d6+4 rounds.

226 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
SEAHORSE, GIANT
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d20
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 210ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 2, 3, or 4

SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Semi (2-4)
MORALE 55, 60, or 65
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 20 +4/hp
Giant Seahorses are giant versions of the regular seahorse, not 
mer-horses (that’s the hippocampus). They are often trained as 
steeds by underwater species such as tritons.
SHADOW
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d10+1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 3+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d6 + special)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 65
LOOT Hoard 6
EXPERIENCE 250 +4/hp
Shadows flitter about old ruins and dusty dungeons, seeking to 
feed upon the living. 
Due to their partial immaterialness, they can only be hit by mag-
ic weapons. Infravision does not detect them as they do not ra-
diate heat, but magical light is sufficient to reveal these horrors 
for what they are.
Some shadows attack victims in their sleep, giving them night -
mares from which they never wake, as their physical forms are 
drained of life by the shadows’ horrid ties to worlds beyond 
mundane reckoning.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Shadows are turned as type 4 Undead.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Immunities. Shadows are immune to cold damage. As undead 
creatures they are also immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. They cannot be affected by sleep, gas -
es, poison, or disease.
Special Features
Hard to See.  Shadows are nearly undetectable in their normal 
conditions. In any given round, a shadow has a 90% chance 
to be treated as invisible unless the lighting is full daylight or is 
magical (such as a light spell).
Special Attacks
Drain Attributes. Any successful hit by a shadow causes the vic -
tim to lose a point of strength for a period of 6 turns (no saving 
throw). If a victim’s strength score or hit point total is reduced 
to zero, they become a shadow under control of the shadow 
that drained the last point. A character dying in this way can -
not be raised from the dead.
Unarmed Combat. Shadows cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
SHAMBLING MOUND
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 8 to 11
MELEE ATTACKS 2 strikes (2d8 blunt)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE By HD
LOOT Hoard 2, Cache 6 and 10
EXPERIENCE Variable according to hit dice
Shambling mounds are intelligent plants that appear to be 
walking piles of rotting vegetation. They dwell deep under -
ground and in swamps, wherever there is ample moisture and 
decay. Shambling mounds will eat anything organic. They feed 
by wrapping their roots around prey they have killed, absorbing 
the nutrients as the material rots.
Immunities and Resistances
Resistant to Weapons.  Weapons inflict only half damage to a 
shambling mound.
===== PAGE 228 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 227
Electricity Causes Growth. If a shambling mound is “damaged” by 
electricity, it gains a hit die.
Immune to Fire.  Shambling mounds are perpetually damp and 
immune to fire damage.
Resistant to Cold. Cold-based attacks do no damage if the crea -
ture makes a saving throw, half if it fails the save.
Vulnerability to Plant-Attacking Spells. Shambling mounds are 
considered plants for all spells that affect plants (such as plant 
control or charm plant). 
Special Attacks
Suffocation. If both arms strike the same target within the same 
round, that target has become tangled up inside the creature. 
The victim will be smothered in 2d4 rounds unless the monster 
can be killed. Anyone entangled can continue to attack, but at 
-4 to hit and damage.
Unarmed Combat. Shambling mounds cannot effectively be tar-
geted with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use 
these tactics.
SHARK
3 HD 5 HD 8 HD
FREQUENCY Common Common Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 3d4 3d4 3d4
SIZE Medium Medium Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 240ft swimming 240ft swimming 240ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] 6 [14] 6 [14]
