Sharks are scavengers of the seas who viciously attack anything 
that seems wounded or dying. They are attracted to splashing, 
kicking, or any other sound that can be mistaken for a fish in 
distress. The smell of blood in 
the water can attract sharks 
from miles away. When 
sharks have targeted their 
prey, they will enter a 
feeding frenzy and attack 
everything in sight, some -
times even each other.
SHEDU
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d4
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Lawful good
MOVE 120ft; 240ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 9+9
MELEE ATTACKS 2 hooves (1d6 blunt)
SENSES Detect invisible 10%
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 100
LOOT Hoard 7
EXPERIENCE 1,950 +14/hp

===== PAGE 229 =====
228 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Shedu are winged bulls with human heads. They wander end -
lessly, battling evil and chaos. They will aid anyone of good 
alignment who is in need. Shedu not only travel the material 
plane but the ethereal and astral planes as well. 
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (25%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 25% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Spells. All shedu cast spells as a 9th level magic-user.
Ethereality (at will). Shedu can become ethereal at will.
Unarmed Combat.  Shedus cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
Language
Shedu speak their own language, using their telepathy to com-
municate with others.
SHRIEKER
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d4
SIZE Small to large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 10ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [12]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS Nil
SENSES N/A
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 50 +2/hp
Shriekers are mobile fungi that wander around quietly under -
ground soaking up moisture. They are the favourite food of pur-
ple worms and shambling mounds. 
Special Features
Shriek. Shriekers give off an ear-piercing shriek whenever they 
detect light within 30ft, or movement within 10ft. Shriekers will 
continue to shriek for 1d3 rounds, with a 50% chance to attract 
a wandering monster each round.
SKELETON
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 3d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d6)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 15 +1/hp
Skeletons are the animated bones of humans and the like, es -
sentially mindless undead creatures. They are created by an evil 
(or neutral at best) magic-user or cleric, only obeying the simple 
commands of their animator, which is often limited by the spell 
itself to a score of words. Some sages warn, though, that mere 
proximity to great evil can animate the dead, resulting in armies 
of these horrors springing to unlife in forgotten catacombs and 
foul dungeons. Though given no commands, they seek out the 
living and attempt to force them into the ranks of the dead.
No matter how a skeleton attacks, whether with a rusty blade, 
a stone, or its own former femur, it always deals 1d6 damage. 
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Skeletons are turned as type 1 Undead.
Undeath. This creature is immune to cold, and to all mind con -
trol (including charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by 
sleep, gases, poison, or disease.
Resistant to Slashing and Piercing Damage.  Skeletons take only 
half damage from slashing and piercing damage. They take full 
damage from blunt weapons.
Unarmed Combat. Skeletons do not engage in grappling or over-
bearing attacks. They can be targeted this way, but do not 
suffer any temporary damage inflicted. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
===== PAGE 230 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 229
SKELETON WARRIOR
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 9+2 or higher
MELEE ATTACKS 1 By weapon (+3 to hit)
SENSES Infravision 60ft; Detect 
invisible 10%
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 90
LOOT Hoard 1
EXPERIENCE 2,000 +16/hp
Skeleton warriors are the unfortunate victims of powerful mag-
ic; in life they were fighters of great prowess and likely lords in 
their own right. In death they have become abominations, com-
pelled by evil sorcery to serve any who possess the golden cir -
clets that contain their souls. Skeleton warriors desire above all 
else to gain possession of their own circlet and to be revenged 
upon any who have possessed and used it in the past.
They are generally armed and armoured with the remains of 
what they wore in life, or their grave goods.
A character in possession of the circlet of a skeleton warrior and 
within 240ft may attempt to dominate it. The circlet must be 
worn on the attempting character’s head in order to do this; it 
cannot be used whilst wearing a helmet or similar headgear. On 
the first attempt at domination, the character has a chance of 
success equal to their wisdom score ×5, but they must be able 
to see their victim and have the freedom to concentrate for one 
round. If the attempt fails, it may be attempted again on the fol-
lowing round. If concentration is interrupted before domination 
is achieved, such as by an attack, the character must concen -
trate for a further three rounds. During this time, the Skeleton 
Warrior will attempt to kill its would-be master and take posses-
sion of the circlet if such is at all possible.
In the event of successful domination, the skeleton warrior is 
rendered inert for as long as the character remains in posses -
sion of the circlet. Additionally, whenever they are within 240ft 
of one another and the character wears the circlet without hel -
met as described above, the user may take control of the Skel -
eton Warrior, being able to see through its eyes and direct its 
actions as they desire; whilst controlling the actions of the skel-
eton warrior, the user may not take any action.
Should the user lose possession of the circlet for any reason, 
the skeleton warrior will seek them out and slay them, moving 
at double its usual movement rate. In the event that the skele -
ton warrior ever regains possession of its circlet, it will place it 
upon its head and as a result both will turn to dust.
The lair of a skeleton warrior is normally a richly adorned tomb 
filled with considerable treasure. However, because they are 
usually either seeking the current possessor of their circlet or 
else in the unwilling service of said possessor, they are rarely 
found in their lairs.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (90%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 90% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Unusual Form of Undeath. Contrary to appearances, the skeleton 
warrior is not undead in the conventional sense. It cannot be 
turned, is unaffected by a scroll of protection from undead, 
etc.
Special Features
Fear Aura.  The appearance of a skeleton warrior is terrifying, 
and creatures with less than five HD who look upon it will panic 
and flee from its presence. 
Unarmed Combat. Skeleton warriors do not engage in grappling 
or overbearing attacks. They can be targeted this way, but do 
not suffer any temporary damage inflicted. If not fended off, 
they score an unarmed hit with an 18+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/
DEF +4/+1. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2. Grappling 
Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
SLITHERING TRACKER
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 5
MELEE ATTACKS Special
SENSES Special
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 70
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 250 +5/hp
Slithering trackers live in dungeons, ruins and dark places. They 
are transparent and if not in natural sunlight are almost impos-
sible to see. They almost never attack their prey immediately, 
instead following and waiting until their target is asleep. They 
can follow through almost anything, being amorphous in shape 
and capable of seeping through tiny gaps, such as door jambs 
or cracks in stonework. 
===== PAGE 231 =====
230 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Immunities and Resistances
Ooze. This creature cannot be knocked prone, and is not affect-
ed by lack of light, invisible opponents, gases, or other circum-
stances that would not affect an ooze.
Compressible. This creature can fit through a gap one inch wide. 
It can’t be restrained and isn’t affected by being prone.
Special Features
Almost Invisible. If not in natural sunlight, a slithering tracker has 
a 19 in 20 chance to be effectively invisible. Since the tracker 
tries to follow its prey, this should be determined in one die 
roll by the GM that covers all the adventurers. Once combat 
begins, each adventurer may roll separately to determine if 
they can see the creature.
Special Attacks
Paralyse Sleeping Opponent. If a slithering tracker catches its vic-
tim asleep, it will touch it, forcing the victim to roll a saving 
throw vs paralysation or be totally paralysed for 1d6 hours. 
The tracker will then feed directly on its victim’s life energy, 
killing it in 1 hour.
Unarmed Combat. These creatures cannot effectively be target -
ed with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
SLIME, GREEN
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE None
ARMOUR CLASS 10 [10]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS Special
SENSES Special
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 20 +2/hp
Green slime is an occasional dungeon hazard. Sages debate 
whether it is vegetable or fungoid in nature. It grows over the 
ceiling of an area until a pendulous bulb of slime is almost ready 
to drop. The vibrations from passing creatures cause these 
bulbs to fall. This hazard makes it a good idea for adventurers 
to carry open flame and cure disease spells.
Immunities and Resistances
Only Damaged by Fire, Cold, Cure Disease.  Green slime is un -
harmed by weapons and most spells. It does take damage 
from cold and fire, and can be instantly killed by a cure disease 
spell.
Special Attacks
Converts Flesh to Slime.  If green slime touches exposed flesh, 
it will convert the flesh rapidly to green slime. It can also eat 
through wood (slowly) and metal (quickly—a metal item will be 
consumed in 1d6 rounds). Only stone can stop it.
Failing that, a creature with green slime on it must cut away the 
affected area, amputate the affected limb, or die in 1d4 rounds 
(after which it will be converted to green slime and cannot be 
raised or resurrected).
SLUG, GIANT
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft
ARMOUR CLASS 8 [12]
HIT DICE 12
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d12)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 2,000 +16/hp
Giant slugs live in dungeons and other underground complexes 
where they can avoid sunlight. Since these creatures are bone -
less, they can squeeze through narrow openings and navigate 
around most obstructions. 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Non-Magical Blunt Weapons.  Because of the slug’s 
flexible body, non-magical blunt weapons do not inflict dam -
age against them.
Special Features
Compressible. This creature can fit through a gap one foot wide. 
It isn’t affected by being prone.
Special Attacks
Spit Acid. Giant slugs have a nasty bite, but their most effective 
weapon is their ability to spit acid up to 100ft away. The first 
shot is almost always a miss (only 10% chance to hit), but this 
attack serves to approximate distance. After the first shot the 
chance hitting is base 100%, going down 10% for every 10ft 
distance from the giant slug; thus at 20ft away the chance to 
hit is 80%, at 70ft away 30%, etc. On a successful hit using this 
===== PAGE 232 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 231
percentage, the acid inflicts 4d8 points of corrosion damage 
on an individual target unless the target makes a saving throw, 
in which case the damage is halved. Items exposed to the acid 
must also make an item saving throw on Table 1.6.4a in the 
Player Guide.
Unarmed Combat.  Giant slugs cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
SNAKE, GIANT
BOA ADDER COBRA AMPHISBAENA
FREQUENCY Uncommon Uncommon Rare Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2 1d6 1d4 1d3
SIZE Large Large Large Medium (6ft long)
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Neutral Neutral
MOVE 90ft 150ft 120ft 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15] 5 [15] 5 [15] 3 [17]
HIT DICE 6+1 4+2 4+2 6
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d4) and 1 
constrict (2d4) 1 bite (1d4+poison) 1 bite (1d4+poison) 2 bites (1d4+ poison)
SENSES Standard Standard Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil Nil Nil Nil
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1) Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE 75 65 65 75
LOOT Nil Nil Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 345 +8/hp 155 +4/hp 190 +4/hp 475 +6/hp
All giant snakes are carnivorous and can be found in every type 
of climate except for the coldest. Note: For giant sea snakes see 
the listing for sea serpent.
Unarmed Combat. Giant snakes cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
GIANT BOA CONSTRICTORS
Special Attacks
Constrict. A boa constrictor will drop on its prey from above, coil-
ing its long body around the chosen target victim and attack -
ing by squeezing for 2d4 points of damage, inflicting automat-
ic damage from the constriction (a to-hit roll is still required 
for the bite). Attacks against a constricting snake have a 25% 
chance to also damage the victim in the coils).
GIANT ADDERS
This is the common name for giant poisonous snakes and they 
come in a variety of species. 
Special Attacks
Venomous Bite. The default poison for a giant adder is negated 
by a saving throw, but causes death if the save is failed. Some 
types of adders have a powerful poison which, even if saved 
against, causes 3d6 points of damage to the victim.
GIANT COBRAS
These are hooded giant snakes with the ability to spit poison at 
a single target up to 30ft distant. 
Special Attacks
Venomous Bite or Spit Venom. A successful to-hit roll on a bite or 
spit (range 30ft) requires a saving throw vs. death or the victim 
dies.
AMPHISBAENA
These are 6ft long snakes with a head at both ends of the body. 
Both heads are capable of delivering a poisonous bite. Its meth-
od of travel is as bizarre as the creature’s appearance: one head 
of this unusual snake will grab the neck of the other, and the 
creature then rolls like a hoop upon the ground.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Cold.  The amphisbaena is immune to cold damage, 
though the sages are at a loss as to why this is so.
Special Attacks
Venomous Bites. A successful to-hit roll on a bite attack requires 
a saving throw vs. death or the victim dies.
===== PAGE 233 =====
232 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
SPECTRE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d6
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 150ft hovering; 300ft flying 
(AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 7+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d8)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE High (13-14)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Cache 3 (x3), 10, 11
EXPERIENCE 1,815 +10/hp
Spectres are similar to ghosts, but unlike the ethereal-dwelling 
ghost, a spectre is an undead spirit that dwells mostly on the 
negative material plane with a “shadow” in the prime material 
plane. 
If a cleric (good or evil) attempts to restore a spectre back to life 
via a spell or ritual, the spectre must make a saving throw or it 
is destroyed.
Some sages speculate that spectres have no power in sunlight, 
but no one has ever proved this theory.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Spectres are turned as type 9 Undead.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Immunities. Spectres are immune to cold damage, paralysation, 
and elemental spells (any spell relying on fire, water, earth, or 
air).
Undeath. This creature is immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by sleep, gases, 
poison, or disease.
Special Attacks
Level Drain. With a successful hit, a spectre drains two levels of 
experience from the victim. Those reduced below zero energy 
levels become half-strength spectres under control of the one 
that stole their life-force.
Unarmed Combat. Spectres cannot effectively be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
SPHINXES
There are four kinds of sphinx. Androsphinxes are always male, 
while gynosphinxes are always female. It is not clear whether 
criosphinxes and hieracosphinxes are related to the andro-gy -
no type of sphinx, or even whether these types are related to 
each other.
Unarmed Combat. Sphinxes cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, but they may overbear. If not fended 
off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: 
ATK/DEF +10/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +6/na. Grap-
pling Result Mods: ATK/DEF na/na.
SPHINX, ANDRO-
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large (12ft long)
ALIGNMENT Neutral/Neutral good
MOVE 180ft; 240ft flying (AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS -1 [21]
HIT DICE 10
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (2d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft, Detect 
invisible 5%
LAIR CHANCE 50%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 95
LOOT Cache 7
EXPERIENCE 1,500 +4/hp
The androsphinx is a winged lion with a human head, some -
times with human head and torso (although the front arms are 
lion-like in this case). Like their female counterparts, the andro-
sphinx love intellectual pursuits and will often grant access to 
areas they are set to guard or hoards they are watching (pro -
viding doing so does not break a prior agreement or endanger 
the androsphinx in any way). Androsphinxes may be “hired” (if 
that is the right word) as guardians for a given treasure, place 
or thing for a while; they are circumspect in their acceptance 
of such duties and payment must be made in full or they will 
unleash their wrath on their would-be employers upon comple-
tion of the duties set.
Special Features
Spell Use. An androsphynx casts spells as a 6th level cleric.
Special Attacks
Roar. Androsphinx all have the ability to emit a deafening roar 
that functions as a horn of blasting.
===== PAGE 234 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 233
SPHINX, CRIO–
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Large (9ft long)
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 160ft; 240ft flying (AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS -2 [22]
HIT DICE 9
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (2d4) and 1 head-butt 
(3d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 90
LOOT Hoard 6
EXPERIENCE 900 +4/hp
Less “magical” than their more human-seeming kin, the crio -
sphinx is a winged lion with the head of a ram. These creatures 
simply collect treasure and hunt much as other intelligent be -
ings. 
Special Attacks
On a successful to-hit roll of 20, a head-butted opponent is 
stunned for a single round.
SPHINX, GYNO–
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large (10ft long)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic neutral or Chaotic 
good
MOVE 150ft; 240ft flying (AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 7
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (2d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 80
LOOT Cache 4, 10
EXPERIENCE 650 +8/hp
The storied gynosphinx is a winged half-woman, half lion crea -
ture that dwells in arid climes, amid abandoned tombs and the 
like. Like the androsphinx, the gynosphinx is a winged lion with a 
human head, sometimes with human head and torso (although 
the front arms are always lion-like). The chief pursuits of the gy-
nosphinx are intellectual; their kind prefers riddles and puzzles 
and complex games to (almost) any other type of activity.
Although basically good in nature, the gynosphinx is capricious 
and will not hesitate to “corner” a creature and demand some 
sort of payment for release (usually in the form of a song, story, 
a game of chess, etc.). Offers of payment in the form of material 
bribes are always welcome.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities. 
1/day: read magic, read languages, detect invisibility, locate ob-
ject, dispel magic, clairvoyance, clairaudience, remove curse or 
legend lore (this last spell she greatly prefers to use as gyno -
sphinxes can be storehouses of knowledge and pride them -
selves on the ability).
SPHINX, HIERACO–
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large (7ft long)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic neutral/Chaotic evil
MOVE 140ft; 220ft flying (AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 8
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (2d4) and 1 bite (1d10)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 85
LOOT Hoard 5
EXPERIENCE 600 +12/hp
The dreaded hieracosphinx is an eminently evil being. A 
hawk-headed, winged lion, it prowls desolate places and preys 
on unsuspecting lone travellers or weak parties of people. Its 
appetite for warm flesh is nearly insatiable and it is utterly mer-
ciless in its pursuit. The creature will often toy with opponents, 
chasing them for league upon league through the desert under 
the blazing sun, and when they think they have reached rela -
tive safety, the beast will swoop down and attack with its ra -
zor-sharp beak and talons.
===== PAGE 235 =====
234 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
SPIDER, GIANT
BIG BIGGER BIGGEST
FREQUENCY Common Common Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d12 2d6 1d8
SIZE Small Medium Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral Chaotic evil
MOVE 60ft; 150ft in web 180ft 30ft; 120ft in web
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] 6 [14] 4 [16]
HIT DICE 1+1 2+2 4+4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d4+poison) 1 (1d6+poison) 1 (1d8+poison)
SENSES Standard Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE 50% 50% 75%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1) Low (5-7)
MORALE 50 55 70
LOOT Individual 1-5 Individual 1-5, Cache 3 Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 75 +2/hp 150 +3/hp 325 +5/hp
Unarmed Combat. Giant spiders cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
BIG GIANT SPIDERS
Big giant spiders come in various shapes and colours. These are 
among the most frequently encountered denizens of dungeons 
and ruins. Some are jumping spiders and some spin webs, but 
unlike the larger types, neither characteristic is significant in 
game terms.
Special Attacks
Venomous Bite. On a successful hit, the target of a big giant spi -
der’s bite must make a saving throw vs. death or die, but the 
saving throw is made with a +2 bonus to the die roll because 
the venom is relatively weak.
BIGGER GIANT SPIDERS
The bigger variety of giant spiders resemble tarantulas. They do 
not spin webs because they are jumping predators. 
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. As hunting spiders, the bigger giant spider surprises 
prey on a roll of 1-5 on a d6.
Special Attacks
Leap. The bigger type of giant spider can leap 30ft to attack and 
attack in the same round. This is considered a charge.
Venomous Bite. On a successful hit, the target of a bigger giant 
spider’s bite must make a saving throw vs. death or die, but the 
saving throw is made with a +1 bonus to the die roll because 
the venom is slightly weaker than normal poison.
BIGGEST GIANT SPIDERS
These creatures build webs to ensnare prey. Usually, adventur-
ers will encounter the webs first.
Special Features
Webs. It takes 2 combat rounds to break free from their webs 
(+1 additional round for each point of strength below 17).
Special Attacks
Venomous Bite. On a successful hit, the target of a biggest giant 
spider’s bite must make a saving throw vs. death or die.
Language
In some fantasy worlds, giant spiders can speak Common; in 
others, they are voiceless.

===== PAGE 236 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 235
SPIDER, GIANT WATER
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 140ft underwater
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 3+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d4+1+poison)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 60
LOOT Individual 1-5, Cache 3
EXPERIENCE 200 +4/hp
These are like their giant land-based cousins in most respects. 
They differ in that they do not build webs; rather they build un-
derwater lairs and carefully transfer air trapped in web-sacs to 
the lairs (they do spin silk for this purpose and to cocoon their 
prey). They can hide within and wait for unwary swimmers, 
whereupon they will scuttle out and drag their prey down, poi -
soning them, cocooning them, and removing them to their lair 
to consume.
As their lairs are air-filled, if the creature is slain, these plac -
es may be used for one hour for creatures that require air to 
breathe.
Special Attacks
Venomous Bite. On a successful hit, the target of a giant water 
spider’s bite must make a saving throw vs. death or die.
Unarmed Combat. Giant spiders cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
SPIDER, PHASE
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft; 120ft in web
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13]
HIT DICE 5+5
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d4+1 + poison)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 75%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 75
LOOT Hoard 5
EXPERIENCE 700 +6/hp
The phase spider is so named because it is able to shift itself 
subtly in and out of reality during combat. If struck by a phase 
door spell, the phase spider must remain in phase for eight 
rounds. Becoming ethereal through magic or other means 
puts attackers in phase with the creature and they may strike 
at it with no disadvantage. The webs of this creature are highly 
sought after for the construction of bags of holding, etc.
Special Features
Shift Phase. A phase spider can shift in phase or out of phase, at 
will on its initiative segment, without using an action. Once the 
spider has changed phase, it cannot change phase again until 
its next initiative. While out of phase, the spider is considered 
to be ethereal.
Special Attacks
Venomous Bite. On a successful hit, the target of a phase spider’s 
bite must make a saving throw vs. death or die. The saving 
throw is made with a penalty of -2 to the roll (the venom is 
exceedingly deadly).
Unarmed Combat. Giant spiders cannot effectively be targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
SPRITE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d10
SIZE Tiny (1ft)
ALIGNMENT Neutral good
MOVE 90ft; 180ft flying (AA:III)
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (by weapon)
SENSES Keen
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 50
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 50 +1/hp
Sprites are fey creatures resembling tiny humans with insect 
wings. They use daggers in melee, but prefer small bows (treat 
as a short bow, but half range and 1d3 damage per arrow). 
They will typically only attack evil creatures or those that blun -
der through their homes. Evil things will be slain, others re -
moved in their sleep to somewhere far away.
Special Features
Fey Magic. Sprites can (at will) become invisible, detect evil as per 
the spell, and move in complete silence.
===== PAGE 237 =====
236 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Special Attacks
Arrows. Sprite arrows are coated with sleep poison. A successful 
hit requires a saving throw vs poison or the victim falls into 
magical sleep for 1d6 hours.
Unarmed Combat. Sprites are too small to be effectively targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
SQUID, GIANT
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Huge
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 180ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17] / 7 [13]
HIT DICE 12
MELEE ATTACKS 8 tentacles (1d6) and 1 bite 
(5d4)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Hoard 1
EXPERIENCE 2,000 +16/hp
These monsters live deep within the oceans at depths few can 
reach. Occasionally they will surface to attack a passing ship. 
When these squid attack, they will attach themselves with two 
of their ten tentacles to the ship. This leaves the other eight ten-
tacles free to go after the crew. When the squid has picked the 
ship clean it will crush the hull and drag the wreckage to its lair.
If the squid loses more than 3 arms it will release the ship and 
flee. As it does the monster will release ink in the water leaving 
a cloud 60ft deep by 80ft wide.
Special Features
Sectioned Armour Class. A giant squid’s body is protected by a 
tough shell (AC 3 [17]), but its tentacles and head are more 
vulnerable (AC 7 [13]).
Special Attacks
Tentacle. Each tentacle hits for 1d6 damage. With each success-
ful hit it grabs its victim and holds it. In addition to biting one 
held victim, with each successive round the tentacle does an 
additional 2d6 crushing damage until the victim is dead. It 
takes one round to eat its dead victim, then the tentacle is free 
to attack a new target. The only way to get a squid to release 
its victim is to sever the tentacle by causing 10 hp of slashing 
damage to it (this only damages the tentacle, not the squid).
When the tentacle hits, roll 1d100 to determine how well it is 
holding the victim.
01-25: Both arms are held and the victim cannot attack.
26-75: One limb is held and the victim can attack with a -3 
penalty. 
76-00: Both arms are free and the victim can attack with only 
a -1 penalty.
STIRGE
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 3d10
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 30ft; 180ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 8 [12]
HIT DICE 1+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d3)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 55%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 50
LOOT Hoard 4
EXPERIENCE 35 +2/hp
Stirges are bat-like flying mammals with a long, bloodsucking 
snout. They live in dark places and feed on blood.

===== PAGE 238 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 237
Special Attacks
Attacks as 4HD. A stirge attacks as a 4 hit dice creature.
Suck Blood. Once it has hit, the stirge has latched on to its victim 
and will continue draining the victim of 1d3 hp worth of blood 
per round, without needing any further rolls “to hit”, until the 
stirge has drunk 10–13 (9+1d4) hp worth of blood, at which 
time its hunger is satisfied and it will attempt to depart. Only 
death will prevent the stirge feeding once it has latched on.
STUNJELLY
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 30ft
ARMOUR CLASS 8 [12]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 tendril (2d4 + paralysis)
SENSES Special
LAIR CHANCE 95%
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 150 +4/hp
Stunjellies appear, at a distance, to be no more than a normal 
section of wall, but should any individual stray too close, they 
will turn translucent and attack. Most known examples occupy 
an area 10ft wide by 10ft high and are 2ft to 5ft thick, but larger 
versions have occasionally been reported.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Stunjellies surprise on a 1-5 on 1d6 if they have not 
been detected before the target moves into range.
Immunities and Resistances
Immunities. Stunjellies are immune to paralysis, as well as poly -
morph, electrical attacks and mind-influencing spells. They 
cannot be knocked prone.
Special Attacks
Paralysis. If the stunjelly makes a successful hit, the target must 
make a saving throw vs paralysis or be paralysed for 5d4 
rounds, which the Stunjelly uses to engulf its victim and begin 
the process of digestion.
SYLPH
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft; 360ft flying (AA:V)
ARMOUR CLASS 10 [10]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS Nil
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 60
LOOT Cache 3 (x10), Cache 10
EXPERIENCE 100 +3/hp
Sylphs are aerial fey creatures thought to be closely related to 
nymphs. Because a sylph’s territory is so vast it is unlikely they 
will be encountered anywhere near their lair.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (50%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 50% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; but 
if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Special Features
Spells. Sylphs are able to cast spells as if they are a 7th level 
magic-user (4—1st level, 3—2nd level, 2—3rd level, 1—4th lev -
el). 
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: invisibility.
1/Week: conjure air elemental.
Unarmed Combat.  Sylphs do not engage in grappling or over -
bearing attacks. They can be targeted this way. If not fended 
off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: 
ATK/DEF +10/-10. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0. 
Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
Language
Sylphs have their own language, speak the language of the fey, 
and can also understand common.

TICK, GIANT
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 3d4
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 30ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 50 +2/hp
These blood-sucking creatures are giant versions of the normal 
tick, but are 3-4ft in length. They typically dwell in ruins and up-
per dungeon levels, though some are found in forests as well. 
They usually seek to ambush their prey, jumping out from hid -
ing. 
Special Attacks
Suck Blood. The initial attack inflicts the damage indicated, and 
thereafter the giant tick drains 1d6 hp worth of blood automat-
ically per round (no further to-hit rolls needed). 
Disease. A character bitten by a giant tick must save vs poison or 
suffer a disease that will be fatal in 1d6 weeks.
Unarmed Combat. Giant ticks cannot be effectively targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
TIGER
TIGER SMILODON
FREQUENCY Uncommon Rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1d4 1d2
SIZE Large Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 120ft 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] 6 [14]
HIT DICE 5+5 7+2
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d6) and 1 
bite (1d10)
2 claws (1d8) and 1 
bite (2d6)
SENSES Keen Keen
LAIR CHANCE 7% 15%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE 75 80
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 250 +6/hp 525 +10/hp
Tigers are large, carnivorous and very cunning cats ranging 
from tropical climes to subarctic tundra. Tigers are ruthless and 
efficient predators, often hunting in pairs or family groups, sur-
rounding their prey and attacking as one. 
Smilodons (sabre-toothed tigers) are dangerous and aggres -
sive. These giant cats are generally found in “lost world” type 
areas with dinosaurs and cave men, but they are sometimes en-
countered in more standard (sword & sorcery) settings as well. 
Surprise and Initiative
Alert. Tigers and smilodons are surprised only on a roll of 1 on 
1d6.
Special Features
Movement. Tigers can climb trees as easily as walking on the 
ground. Tigers and smilodons can leap 40ft to attack from hid-
ing (treat as charge), move quietly at quarter speed in natural 
settings, and spring upwards a distance of 10ft.
Special Attacks
Rear Claws. If both claw attacks are successful the tiger (or Smi -
lodon) has grabbed its victim and gains two additional attacks 
at +4 “to hit” from its powerful rear legs (claw damage with a 
+2 bonus).
Smilodon Bite. Smilodons have a +2 to-hit bonus for their bite.
Unarmed Combat. Tigers and smilodons cannot be targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks. They may choose to over -
bear. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 
1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/
DEF +5/na.

===== PAGE 240 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 239
TITAN
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Huge (18ft or more)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic good
MOVE Depends on HD (see below)
ARMOUR CLASS Depends on HD (see below)
HIT DICE 17–22
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (See below)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Genius (17-18) or Supra–
Genius
MORALE Depends on HD (see below)
LOOT Hoard 5, Cache 3 (x10), Cache 
4
EXPERIENCE Variable
Titans are powerful creatures not native to the material plane, 
and are therefore affected by a protection from evil  spell (if it 
overcomes their innate magic resistance). They normally ap -
pear as massive humans, figured and possessed of an unearth-
ly beauty. By hit die, movement rate, armour class, damage, 
and morale is as follows:
• 17 HD: MV 210ft, AC 2 [18], DAM 7d6, Morale 130
• 18 HD: MV 210ft, AC 1 [19], DAM 7d6, Morale 135
• 19 HD: MV 150ft, AC 0 [20], DAM 7d6, Morale 140
• 20 HD: MV 150ft, AC -1 [21], DAM 7d6, Morale 145
• 21 HD MV 150ft, AC -2 [22], DAM 8d6, Morale 150
• 22 HD: MV 150ft, AC -3 [23], DAM 8d6, Morale 155
It is possible (20% chance) that a titan will be accompanied by 
a storm giant, for the two species are friendly with each other.
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (60%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 60% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mental Attacks.  Titans are immune to all mental at -
tacks (all spells and abilities affecting the mind), and may have 
mental powers if the GM makes use of psionics in the cam -
paign.
Special Features
Spell-Like Abilities.
At Will: levitate (per the level 2 magic-user spell).
2/day: become invisible or shift into the ethereal plane.
Spells. All titans have the ability to cast both clerical and mag -
ic spells. For each type of spell, roll 1d4+3. This number rep -
resents the maximum spell level the titan can cast for that type 
of spell. The titan can cast 2 spells of each level.
Example: The die rolls result in a 4 for magic spells and a 6 
for clerical spells. The titan would be able to cast 2 first level 
magic spells, 2 second level, 2 third level and 2 fourth level 
(because the die roll for maximum level of magic spells was 
4). It would also be able to cast 2 first level clerical spells, 2 
second level, 2 third level, 2 fourth level, 2 fifth level, and 2 
sixth level (because the die roll showed 6 as the maximum 
level for clerical spells).
Unarmed Combat.  Titans cannot be effectively targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
TITANOTHERE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d12
SIZE Huge
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14]
HIT DICE 12
MELEE ATTACKS 1 gore (4d4)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 105
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 2,000 +16/hp
A prehistoric ruminant, the titanothere (indricotherium) looks 
rather like its contemporary descendant the rhinoceros. If 
spooked, it will charge in an effort to trample. A 2 on the “Num-
ber Encountered” roll means a mated pair; a 3 means a mated 
pair with a juvenile.
Special Attacks
Charge. If the titanothere charges into combat and hits, it inflicts 
double damage.
Trample. The titanothere gains an extra attack against crea -
tures size Medium or smaller. The trample attack must roll to 
hit, and inflicts 2d6 hit points of blunt damage. Creatures size 
Large or larger cannot be attacked by trampling.
Unarmed Combat. Titanotheres cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.

TOAD, GIANT
GIANT GIANT 
POISONOUS
FREQUENCY Common Uncommon
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1d12 1d8
SIZE Medium Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 60ft; 60ft leaping 60ft; 60ft leaping
ARMOUR CLASS 6 [14] 7 [13]
HIT DICE 2+4 2
MELEE ATTACKS 2d4 1d4+1 + poison
SENSES Keen Keen
LAIR CHANCE Nil Nil
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE 60 55
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 20 +2/hp 50 +2/hp
Giant toads hunt any sort of prey, including humans. Poisonous 
giant toads are indistinguishable from normal giant toads.
Special Attacks
Leap. Giant toads can leap forward up to 60ft and attack in the 
same round (treat as a charge).
Venomous Bite (Poisonous Toad Only).  The giant poisonous toad 
has a venomous bite. Anyone successfully hit by the toad’s bite 
attack must make a saving throw vs. poison or die.
Unarmed Combat.  Giant toads cannot be effectively targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
TRAPPER
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 30ft
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 12
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (special)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 85%
INTELLIGENCE High (13-14)
MORALE 105
LOOT Hoard 7
EXPERIENCE 3,000 +16/hp
Trappers are underground predators, creatures with flat bodies 
like a manta ray and the ability to blend in almost perfectly with 
stone patterns (including flagstones). They are roughly square 
in shape, about 20ft by 20ft in size (larger and smaller trappers, 
with proportionally higher or lower hit dice have been encoun -
tered), but can alter their dimensions to a considerable degree. 
These dangerous creatures lie flat on dungeon floors, waiting 
for prey to step onto them. 
Surprise and Initiative
Undetectable. It cannot be surprised, and has a 95% chance to 
surprise for 1 segment.
Immunities and Resistances
Resistance (fire and cold).  Trappers take half damage from fire 
and cold, and no damage at all from such attacks if the mon -
ster is entitled to a saving throw and succeeds.
Special Attacks
Enfold. When potential prey steps onto the trapper, the monster 
whips upward to enfold all creatures standing on it. The trap -
per’s stone-hard skin and powerful muscles are deadly. Victims 
enfolded in the trapper cannot use weapons and will suffocate 
in 6 rounds. Moreover, before suffocating, victims will suffer 
6hp damage per round. Rescue must come from outside.
Unarmed Combat. Trappers cannot be effectively targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
TREANT
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d20
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Chaotic good
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 7–12 (1d6+6 if randomly determined)
MELEE ATTACKS 2 (fists). See below
SENSES Keen
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE See below
LOOT Cache 3 (x5), Cache 5
EXPERIENCE
7HD: 1,295 +8/hp 
8HD: 1,600 +10/hp 
9HD: 2,050 +12/hp 
10HD: 2,350 +13/hp 
11HD: 2,750 +14/hp 
12HD: 3,600 +16/hp
Treants are woodland creatures, reclusive to humankind but 
friendly with other good-aligned creatures of the woods. They 
resemble humanoid trees: their “arms” and “legs” are not eas-
ily seen as such until they begin moving. Treants are vigilant 
guardians of the woods, despising anyone of evil alignment and 
all who make reckless use of fire, regardless of alignment. Dam-
age and morale depend upon hit dice as follows:
===== PAGE 242 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 241
• 7–8 HD: 2d8 damage per fist; Morale 80 or 85
• 9–10 HD: 3d6 damage per fist; Morale 90 or 95
• 11–12 HD: 4d6 damage per fist; Morale 100 or 105
Surprise and Initiative
Alert. Treants cannot be surprised.
Immunities and Resistances
Vulnerability to Fire. Treants are quite vulnerable to fire: fire at -
tacks requiring a roll “to hit” gain a bonus of +4, a treant makes 
saving throws vs fire at -4, and any hit die of damage inflicted 
upon a treant gains a +1 to its result.
Special Features
Animate Trees. Treants can animate 1d2 trees to aid them (see 
“Tree, Animated”) within a range of 180ft. Separate experience 
is not awarded for killing such animated trees.
Unarmed Combat.  Treants cannot be effectively targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
TREE, ANIMATED
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 30ft
ARMOUR CLASS 0 [20]
HIT DICE 12
MELEE ATTACKS 2 (fists) (4d6)
SENSES Special
LAIR CHANCE 100%
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 1,300 +16/hp 
(nil if animated by a treant)
Animated trees are generally encountered when treants ani -
mate a normal tree (see “Treant”).
Immunities and Resistances
Plant Immunities. The animated tree doesn’t need to eat, drink, 
sleep, or breathe as an animal would. Magic can’t put it to 
sleep. Gases can’t harm it, and it is immune to poison and dis-
ease.
Unarmed Combat. Animated trees cannot be effectively targeted 
with grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
TRITON
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d6 (10–60)
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral good
MOVE 150ft; 150ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [15]
HIT DICE 3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (weapon)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE High+ (13-14+)
MORALE 60
LOOT Hoard 3, Cache 4, 5, 6, 10
EXPERIENCE
Warrior 110 +2/hp 
Leader (4HD) 145 +3/hp 
Leader (5HD) 190 +4/hp 
Leader (6HD) 280 +6/hp 
Leader (9HD) 1,000 +12/hp
Tritons resemble humans, but have two fish-like tails instead of 
legs. These aquatic creatures can live in any depth of water, and 
are found in the elemental plane of water as well as in the prime 
material plane. Groups of tritons are composed as follows:
• 90% chance to be mounted on hippocampi (65%) or giant sea 
horses (35%)
• Per 10 tritons, 1 leader with 4-6 hit dice (1d3+3)
• Per 10 tritons, 10% chance of 1d4 tritons with magic-user abil-
ity of level 1d6.
• Per 20 tritons, 1 leader with 7-8 hit dice (1d2+6)
• Any number of tritons over 50, 1 leader with 9 hit dice
• A triton leader (9 HD) will carry a shell horn with the following 
abilities:
• Summon allies: 5d4 hippocampi, 5d6 giant sea horses, or 1d10 
sea lions (depending upon how blown)
• Disperse sea animals:  all sea creatures of animal intelligence 
must save vs spells at -5 or flee for 3d6 turns
Tritons are civilised, dwelling in castles beneath the waves (80% 
chance) or elaborate caverns of their own excavation (20% 
chance). These lairs contain additional tritons:
• 60 normal triton warriors plus leaders as described above
• 1 magic-user level 1d2+6
• 1 cleric level 1d4+7
• 4  clerics level 1d4+5
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (90%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 90% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Unarmed Combat.  Tritons cannot be effectively targeted with 
grappling or overbearing attacks, and do not use these tactics.
===== PAGE 243 =====
242 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
TROGLODYTE
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 10d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d3) and 1 bite (1d4+1) or by 
weapon
SENSES Infravision 90ft
LAIR CHANCE 15%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 55
LOOT Hoard 1
EXPERIENCE 20 +2/hp
Troglodytes are a reptilian species that make their lairs deep 
underground. They have an unnatural hatred of humans, and 
will attack them on sight.
Raiding parties (10 or more) will be led by a troglodyte that has 
3 HD. War parties (20 or more) will be led by 2 troglodytes with 
4 HD each. In their lair a chief will be present (a 6 HD monster) 
as well as 2d4 guards (3 HD creatures).
Some troglodytes attack with their claws and teeth, and some 
use weapons that they have taken in previous battles. 
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise (Chameleon). Troglodytes have the ability to alter their 
colouration depending on the area around them. This ability 
allows them to surprise others on a 1–4 on 1d6. This surprise 
bonus does not work when they are emitting their odour.
Special Features
Emit Repulsive Odour.  They also have a special attack, once in 
combat they emit a sickening odour against which humans (as 
well as elves, half-elves, dwarfs, halflings, and gnomes) must 
save vs poison. If they fail, they lose 1 point of strength per 
round for 1d6 rounds. This effect lasts for 10 rounds after the 
last round it was active.
Unarmed Combat. Troglodytes can be targeted and may engage 
in overbearing and grappling. If not fended off, they score an 
unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Over-
bear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0. Grappling Result Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/-0.
TROLL
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d12
SIZE Large (9ft+ tall)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 6+6
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d4+4) and 1 bite (2d6)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 40%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 80
LOOT Hoard 4
EXPERIENCE 525 +8/hp
Trolls are large creatures, shaped like humans but with long 
arms and legs, rubbery flesh, and a feral, misshapen face. Their 
crouching gait is unmistakable to those who are familiar with 
the species, which is known for its ravenous hunger and its 
most notable attribute: regeneration.
Immunities and Resistances
Effect of Regeneration.  Trolls regenerate all damage except fire 
damage and corrosive damage (acid). Thus, a troll can only be 
permanently killed by fire or acid.

===== PAGE 244 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 243
Special Features
Regeneration. After three rounds of combat (even if the troll is 
dead), a troll’s innate regeneration ability will start working, 
recovering 3 hit points per round. Their ability to regenerate 
even allows detached limbs to reattach to their body. Severed 
limbs will even continue to attack foes independently. 
Unarmed Combat. Trolls do not engage in grappling or overbear-
ing attacks, but can be targeted this way. If not fended off, they 
score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF 
+8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +3/-3. Grappling Result 
Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2.
TROLL, GIANT
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d12
SIZE Large (10ft tall)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 8
MELEE ATTACKS 1 club (2d8) or 2 claws (1d6)
SENSES Infravision 90ft
LAIR CHANCE 33%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 85
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 750 +10/hp
These horrible creatures are the result of crossbreeding trolls 
with hill giants, resulting in a monster that looks mostly like a 
troll, but even larger.
Immunities and Resistances
Effect of Regeneration. Giant trolls regenerate all damage except 
fire damage and corrosive damage ( acid) while they are alive. 
Unlike true trolls, they do not regenerate once killed. It is thus 
possible to kill a giant troll without using acid or fire, but the 
creature will be regenerating damage up until the point it dies.
Special Features
Catch Missiles. Giant trolls have extremely fast reflexes and are 
able to snatch a missile from the air 25% of the time. If it is a 
missile suitable for throwing (example: spear, rock) the giant 
troll will throw it back as its next attack.
Regeneration. Giant trolls regenerate 2 hp per round but unlike 
their smaller cousins, they are not capable of rebonding sev -
ered limbs, and severed limbs will not attack independently.
Unarmed Combat. Giant trolls cannot be targeted with overbear-
ing or grappling attacks, and do not use these tactics.
TROLL, GIANT TWO–HEADED
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3
SIZE Large (10ft tall)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 10
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d6) and 2 bites (1d10)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 35%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 95
LOOT Hoard 4, Cache 3
EXPERIENCE 1,750 +15/hp
Giant two-headed trolls are the vicious offspring of trolls and et-
tins. In appearance they look most like trolls, though they have 
two heads like an ettin and prefer wearing filthy animal skins 
as ettins do. Giant two–headed trolls are nocturnal and prefer 
underground dwellings such as dungeons or caverns.
In combat the giant two–headed troll can direct claw attacks at 
two different opponents, but both bite attacks must be directed 
at the same opponent. 
Surprise and Initiative
Alert. Giant two-headed trolls can only be surprised on a die roll 
of 1 (on a 1d6).
Immunities and Resistances
Effect of Regeneration.  Giant two-headed trolls regenerate all 
damage except fire damage and corrosive damage (acid) while 
they are alive. Unlike true trolls, they do not regenerate once 
killed. It is thus possible to kill these creatures without using 
acid or fire, but the creature will be regenerating damage up 
until the point it dies.
Special Features
Regeneration. Giant trolls regenerate 1 hit point per round (ex -
cept acid and fire damage) but unlike their smaller cousins, 
they are not capable of rebonding severed limbs, and severed 
limbs will not attack independently.
Unarmed Combat. Giant two-headed trolls cannot be targeted 
with overbearing or grappling attacks, and do not use these 
tactics.
===== PAGE 245 =====
244 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
TROLL, ICE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d6
SIZE Large (9ft tall)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 8
HIT DICE 2
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d8)
SENSES Infravision 90ft
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 55
LOOT Hoard 4 (with no magic)
EXPERIENCE 45 +2/hp
An ice troll has the general form of a troll, but its cold, semi-trans-
parent body seems to have been chiselled from blue-tinged ice. 
Ice trolls prefer underground dwellings such as dungeons or 
caverns and will always lair near running water if possible or, 
failing that, the wettest spot they can find. 
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Immune to Cold. Ice trolls are immune to cold damage. 
Vulnerability to Fire. Fire inflicts double damage upon ice trolls.
Effect of Regeneration.  Ice trolls regenerate all damage except 
fire damage and corrosive damage (acid). Thus, an ice troll can 
only be permanently killed by fire or acid.
Special Features
Regeneration. These trolls regenerate 2 hp per round (except 
damage from fire and acid) and are capable of rebonding sev-
ered limbs, but only if the limb is immersed in water. A severed 
limb will move toward the nearest body of water if one is with-
in 30ft. Severed limbs will not attack independently.
Unarmed Combat. Ice trolls do not engage in grappling or over -
bearing attacks, but can be targeted this way. If not fended off, 
they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/
DEF +6/-6. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +3/-3. Grappling 
Result Mods: ATK/DEF +2/-2.
TROLL, SPECTRAL
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d2
SIZE Large (8ft tall)
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2
HIT DICE 5+5
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d3 + special) and 1 
bite (1d6)
SENSES Infravision 120ft
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Very (11-12)
MORALE 75
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 625 +6/hp
Long ago these creatures were created in some bizarre and 
perverted arcane ritual, merging the life essences of a troll and 
some extra-planar creature. Fewer than 50 of these creatures 
are believed to exist and the secret of their creation process 
died with the creators when the spectral trolls rose up in revolt 
and fed upon their life energy (see “claws drain strength”). 
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Resistance (30%).  Any magic spell or effect has a 30% 
chance (at caster level 11) to have no effect on this creature; 
but if it does, the magic takes full effect.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. This creature is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, and slashing damage from weapons and unarmed 
attacks, unless the weapon causing that damage has a magical 
bonus of +1 or more.
Effect of Regeneration. Spectral trolls regenerate all damage ex -
cept fire damage and corrosive damage ( acid). Thus, they can 
only be permanently killed by fire or acid.
Immunity (Cold). Spectral trolls are immune to cold damage.
Special Features
Natural Invisibility. Spectral trolls are invisible. Anyone able to 
see invisible will see a monster that appears very much like 
a shorter-than-normal troll with somewhat indistinct features 
and glowing amber eyes.
Regeneration. After three rounds of combat (even if the spectral 
troll is dead), its innate regeneration ability will start working, 
recovering 3 hit points per round. Their ability to regenerate 
even allows detached limbs to reattach to their body. Severed 
limbs will even continue to attack foes independently. 
Special Attacks
Claws Drain Strength. The spectral troll has two claw attacks that 
subtract 1d3 points from the victim’s strength ability score as 
well as inflicting damage. For example: a 17 strength fighter 
===== PAGE 246 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 245
struck for 2 hit points of damage will also be reduced to a 
strength of 15. Strength is recovered at a rate of 1 point every 
2d4 turns. If a victim’s strength is reduced to zero they die; if 
strength is reduced to 1 or 2 hit points, the victim will fall co -
matose to the ground and will not recover consciousness until 
enough strength is recovered to raise the score to 3 or higher.
Magic Bite. The bite attack inflicts 1d6 points of damage, but the 
troll adds its own hit point value as bonus damage to the attack. 
Example: a spectral troll with 38 hit points bites a cleric for 4 
points of damage. Total damage dealt to the unfortunate cleric 
would 4+38 = 42.
Unarmed Combat. Spectral trolls cannot be targeted with over -
bearing or grappling attacks, and do not use these tactics.
TURTLE, GIANT
GIANT SEA GIANT SNAPPING
FREQUENCY Uncommon Uncommon
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 1d3 1d4
SIZE Large Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 10ft; 150ft 
swimming
30ft; 120ft 
swimming
ARMOUR CLASS
2 [18] (shell) or 
5 [15] (head and 
flippers)
0 [20] (shell) or 5 
[15] (head and legs)
HIT DICE 15 10
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (4d4) 1 bite (6d4)
SENSES Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil Nil
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE 120 95
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 2,400 + 20/hp 1,500 +14/hp
GIANT SEA TURTLES
These are massive beasts, normally placid but ferocious when 
they feel they are under attack. They have a 90% chance of over-
turning a boat, and a 10% chance to overturn even a small ship. 
A sea turtle can withdraw its vulnerable head and flippers into 
its shell, but obviously cannot attack or move if it does so.
Special Features
Segmented Armour Class. The head and flippers of a giant sea 
turtle are AC 5 [15]. Most attacks will have to be directed at its 
shell, which is AC 2 [18]. Only a person directly in front of the 
turtle can attack its head.
GIANT SNAPPING TURTLES
These creatures are less massive than giant sea turtles, but they 
are aggressive predators, lying in wait for prey in the shallow 
waters of swamps, lakes, and coasts. The turtle’s lumpy shell 
is excellent camouflage, granting it an excellent chance to sur-
prise passing creatures. A giant snapping turtle can retract its 
legs and head into its shell, but when it is thus protected it ob -
viously cannot attack or move. Like their smaller cousins, giant 
snapping turtles have disproportionately long necks, and can 
snap at enemies as far as 10ft away.
Surprise and Initiative
Surprise. Giant snapping turtles surprise on a 1–4 on d6.
U.
UNICORN
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4+1
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Chaotic good
MOVE 240ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 4+4
MELEE ATTACKS 2 hooves (1d6) and 1 horn (1d12)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 5%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 70
LOOT Cache 10
EXPERIENCE 440 +4/hp
Unicorns avoid contact with all but woodland creatures, al -
though they may render assistance to maidens who are pure of 
heart, allowing themselves to be tamed as steeds. 
Surprise and Initiative
Alert. Unicorns are never surprised within 240ft.
Surprise. Unicorns surprise on a 1–5 on 1d6.
Immunities and Resistances
Saving Throws. Unicorns make saving throws as a level 11 mag -
ic-user.
Immunities. Unicorns are immune to charm, hold, death magic, 
and poison.
===== PAGE 247 =====
246 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Special Features
Neutralise Poison. A unicorn’s horn negates the effect of poison, 
with normally the merest touch being required.
Dimension Door (1/day). Unicorns can cast dimension door once 
per day at a range of 360ft.
Special Attacks
Charge. Unicorns normally charge into battle, attacking only 
with their horns but for double damage on the initial attack.
Unarmed Combat. Unicorns cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, but they may overbear. If not fended 
off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. Hit Mods: 
ATK/DEF +8/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF +5/na. 
V.
VAMPIRE
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d4
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 120ft; 180ft flying (AA:V)
ARMOUR CLASS 1 [19]
HIT DICE 8+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 strike (1d6+4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 90
LOOT Hoard 6
EXPERIENCE 3,810 +12/hp
The vampire is one of the most feared of the undead. Vampires 
must take refuge in a coffin or other hide-away during daylight, 
and must slumber on soil from their own grave once in a while 
to maintain their powers.
Vampires create others of their kind by draining humans or hu-
manoids of all life energy. The victim must be buried. After 1 day 
they will arise as a vampire. The victim will retain class abilities 
they had in life but will become a chaotic evil undead being. The 
new vampire is a slave to the vampire that created them, but 
becomes free willed if the master is killed.
There is a variant type of vampire from the east, which can -
not assume gaseous form at will (but will if reduced to zero hit 
points), nor does it have a charm gaze. However, it is invisible 
and foes that cannot detect invisibility suffer -4 to strike these 
vampires in combat.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Vampires are turned as type 10 Undead.
Effect of Regeneration.  Vampires regenerate all damage types, 
and can only be killed in one of the following ways. They are 
killed in 3 rounds of immersion in running water (each round 
reduces 1/3 of the creature’s hit points). If caught in sunlight 
a vampire becomes powerless and will die in 1 turn. Finally, a 
stake through the heart, coupled with decapitation, will destroy 
a vampire if holy sacraments or a blessed item is placed in the 
mouth after the head is removed. If a vampire is staked, it will 
appear to die, but unless also decapitated the vampire will re -
vive when the stake is removed.
Immune to Mundane Weapons. The vampire is immune to pierc-
ing, blunt, or cutting damage, unless the weapon causing that 
damage is magical.
Undeath. This creature is immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by sleep, gases, 
poison, or disease.
Resistances. Electricity and cold inflict only half damage on vam-
pires.
Vulnerabilities. In addition to the effect of running water, sun -
light, and turning, vampires are affected in a minor way by a 
lawful good holy symbol, a mirror, or garlic if they are present-
ed with confidence. Garlic causes a vampire to cringe for 1d4 
rounds, and a holy symbol or mirror will cause a vampire to 
take a position in which it cannot directly see the item. Holy 
water will inflict 1d6+1 points of damage per full vial which 
strikes.
Special Features
Regeneration. Vampires regenerate 3 hit points per round. They 
can only be killed by specific methods.
Shape Change. Vampires may shape change into a large bat at 
will, which grants them flying movement.
Summon Creatures. Vampires can summon 1d10×10 bats or rats 
when underground or 3d6 wolves when above. These crea -
tures will arrive in 2d6 rounds. 
Gaseous Form (At Will). Vampires may take gaseous form at will, 
and will be forced into this form if their hit points reach zero. 
They then attempt to reach their coffin to reform their bodies. 
If they do not reach the coffin within 12 turns and rest for 8 
hours, they are unable to re-form.
Special Attacks
Drain Life. A successful hit by a vampire drains two levels of ex -
perience from the victim, which consequently reduces all other 
traits linked to a loss in levels (attack ability, hit dice, etc.).
Charm Gaze. Any person meeting the gaze of a vampire must 
successfully save vs petrifaction with a penalty of -2 or be 
charmed (save each time the gaze is met) as per the charm 
person spell. This applies to anyone who is generally in front 
of the vampire and attacking it or casting spells at it. Attacking 
===== PAGE 248 =====
 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 247
the creature by looking at a reflection is not possible, because 
it casts no reflection.
Unarmed Combat. Vampires can be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks, but they are immune to temporary dam -
age. They may overbear and might grapple under certain con-
ditions. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ 
on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +8/-8. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +2/-2. Grappling Result Mods: ATK/DEF +0/-0.
VILSTRAAK
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d10
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 90ft
ARMOUR CLASS 2 [18]
HIT DICE 1d6  hit points
MELEE ATTACKS 1d4+1/1d4+1
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 50
LOOT Hoard 3
EXPERIENCE 5 +1/hp
Vilstraaks appear as a 6ft tall cross between a human and a bug, 
and have hides with the consistency of heavy rock. The fore -
arms are longer than a human’s and much more massive, end-
ing in two heavy fists.
Vilstraaks blend well with their subterranean habitat, and have 
an innate ability to merge with (and move through) rock. These 
creatures tend to lair in hollows of rock and earth. These lairs 
usually have entrances too small to enter without traveling 
through solid rock, but they can be detected and dug into if 
there is enough time and the right tools.
Surprise and Initiative
Stone Ambush. When merged with stone, vilstraaks surprise on 
1–4 on a 1d6.
Special Features
Travel Through Stone. These creatures can merge into stone and 
move through it, at half their normal movement rate.
Unarmed Combat. Vilstraaks cannot be targeted with overbear -
ing or grappling attacks, and do not use these tactics.
VOLT
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d12
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft levitating (AA:II)
ARMOUR CLASS 3 [17]
HIT DICE 2+1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d4 + shock)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 55
LOOT Nil
EXPERIENCE 50 +3/hp
Volts are peculiar, magical creatures that attack almost any -
thing that ventures near their lair. They appear as little balls of 
grey fur with two protruding eyes and a long, whip-like tail that 
crackles with electricity.
Volt lairs usually smell slightly of ozone.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Electricity.  Volts are immune to electricity and light -
ning damage.
Special Attacks
Latch, Bite, and Shock.  If the volt’s bite attack succeeds, it has 
latched onto its target and will do normal bite damage (1d4 
hp) automatically every round. At the same time it will lash its 
foe with its tail (not an automatic hit). The tail attack inflicts 
a strong electric shock (2d6 hp damage) with a hit. Only vic -
tims not wearing metal armour receive a saving throw (for half 
damage), and any victim wearing metal armour is treated as 
unarmoured (AC 10 [10]) for the purposes of the volt’s attack. 
Unarmed Combat. Volts cannot be targeted with overbearing or 
grappling attacks, and do not use these tactics.

===== PAGE 249 =====
248 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
VULCHLING
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d8
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 60ft; 30ft flying (AA:IV)
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [12]
HIT DICE 1
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d4+1) or 2 claws (1d4)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 30%
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE 50
LOOT Individual 1, 2, 3, 4 each
EXPERIENCE 10 +1/hp
Vulchlings are a type of intelligent bird that resemble a vulture, 
but with facial features that look rather like a human with a 
beak. Vulchlings are weak fliers, tending to swoop upon their 
prey with 2 claw attacks, then fighting on the ground (where 
they can only bite).
Vulchlings can be found in desolate areas or underground, and 
they have been known to ally themselves with harpies or Class 
A demons. Vulchlings are attracted to shiny objects and a care -
ful search of their nest usually yields a few coins.
Unarmed Combat. Vulchlings cannot be targeted with overbear-
ing or grappling attacks, and do not use these tactics.
W.
WALRUS
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 3d6
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft; 180ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 6
MELEE ATTACKS 3 (1d6/1d6/2d12)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 20%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1)
MORALE 75
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE 160 +6/hp
Walruses are large, flippered animals with long ivory tusks. They 
are carnivorous and very aggressive in defending their territory, 
and are made every more dangerous due to their great size and 
the fact several are usually encountered. The largest males can 
weight as much as 4,400 lbs.
In combat a walrus attacks with its clawed front flippers and its 
tusks. Males will primarily defend the herd from intruders but 
the females, who are also tusked, will fight if necessary.
Treasure: The ivory tusks of this animal can be sold for 1d4+1×100 
gp on the market.
WASP, GIANT
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d20; 1d20+20 in lair
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 60ft; 210ft flying (AA:V)
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 4
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d4) and 1 sting (2d4 + poison)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE 25%
INTELLIGENCE Non- (0)
MORALE N/A
LOOT Cache 3 (x20)
EXPERIENCE 300 +3/hp
Giant wasp nests may be mud-daubed or paper, depending on 
the type of wasp. Giant wasp wings are instantly burned away if 
they suffer a successful hit with a flaming object; burning away 
the wings in this manner does not cause hit point damage to 
the wasp, but prevents the creature from flying.
Although giant wasps are aerial agility level V, they can hover.
Special Attacks
Stinger. Giant wasps have a poisonous stinger that delivers a 
paralysing venom. Any creature hit by the stinger must make 
a saving throw vs poison or be paralysed for 1d4+1 days, fol -
lowed by death. The wasps generally carry paralysed victims 
to their nests, where the victims are kept to be eaten by larvae 
when eggs hatch.

WEASEL, GIANT
BIG BIGGER
FREQUENCY Uncommon Rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 2d6 1d8
SIZE Small Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 150ft 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13] 6 [14]
HIT DICE 1+1 3+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 (1d8) 1 (2d6)
SENSES Keen Keen
LAIR CHANCE 10% 10%
INTELLIGENCE Animal (1) Animal (1)
MORALE 50 65
LOOT See below See below
EXPERIENCE 40 +2/hp 75 +3/hp
Big and bigger giant weasels are typically encountered in forest-
ed areas, though some may also be found in dungeons. 
Their pelts are quite valuable. Big weasel pelts sell for 1d6×100 
gp, bigger weasel pelts for twice that amount or more.
Special Attacks
Drain Blood. The bite of a giant weasel drains blood (automatic 
damage after the first successful attack). The drain is 1d8 hp 
per round for “big” giant weasels, and 2d6 hp per round for 
the bigger version.
Unarmed Combat. Giant weasels cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks, and they do not use these tactics. 
WHALE
FREQUENCY Common
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d8
SIZE Huge
ALIGNMENT Neutral
MOVE 180ft to 240ft swimming
ARMOUR CLASS 4 [16]
HIT DICE 12 to 36
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite or 1 tail. 5–15 d4 (bite), 
1–5 d8 (tail)
SENSES Standard
LAIR CHANCE Nil
INTELLIGENCE Low (5-7)
MORALE Varies by HD
LOOT See below
EXPERIENCE Varies according to hit dice
As reflected in the statistics above, there is considerable varia -
tion between different species of whales and their relative sizes. 
The swallow whole ability of a purple worm may be used for 
larger whales, if desired, rather than the automatic spit-out de-
scribed here.
Treasure: Whale ambergris from a single whale is worth 
1d20×1,000 gp if properly collected. Whale meat is worth 100 
gp per hit die of the whale. Additionally, there is a 1% chance 
each of finding cp, sp, ep, pp, gems, jewellery, and magic 
items within the stomach of a whale. Coins found will number 
1d3×1,000 of the types found, gems and jewellery will number 
1d20 if found, and magic items will number 1d4.
Special Attacks
Swallow Whole.  Whales can swallow their prey (or attackers) 
whole. Digestive juices from the whale cause 1 hit point of 
damage per turn, and the whale will disgorge prey that attack 
from the inside, but it may choose to do so in deep water.
WIGHT
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d8
SIZE Medium
ALIGNMENT Lawful evil
MOVE 120ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 4+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 claw (1d4+level drain)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 70%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 70
LOOT Hoard 2
EXPERIENCE 590 +4/hp
Wights are undead corpses, risen with a twisted intelligence. Al-
though they are not damaged by sunlight, they loathe the rays 
of the sun and do not emerge by choice from their barrows and 
lairs during daylight. A human killed by a wight becomes a wight 
under the control of its maker.
Immunities and Resistances
Turned as Undead. Wights are turned as type 5 Undead.
Immune to Mundane Non-Silver Weapons. The wight is immune to 
piercing, blunt, or cutting damage, unless the weapon causing 
that damage is magical or silver. 
Immune (Cold). Wights are immune to cold damage.
Undeath. This creature is immune to all mind control (including 
charm) and hold spells. It cannot be affected by sleep, gases, 
poison, or disease.
===== PAGE 251 =====
250 | OSRIC 3.0 - PART TWO: MONSTERS 
Vulnerability to Raise Dead. Wights are destroyed if targeted with 
a raise dead spell.
Special Attacks
Level Drain. The undead power of wights is linked to the nega -
tive material plane, and thus they permanently drain a level of 
experience from victims when they score a hit in combat.
Unarmed Combat. Wights cannot be targeted with overbearing 
or grappling attacks, and do not use these tactics.
WILL–O–THE–WISP
FREQUENCY Uncommon
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1d3
SIZE Small
ALIGNMENT Chaotic evil
MOVE 180ft flying (AA:VI)
ARMOUR CLASS –8 [28]
HIT DICE 9
MELEE ATTACKS 1 electric shock (2d8)
SENSES Infravision 60ft
LAIR CHANCE 50%
INTELLIGENCE Exceptional (15-16)
MORALE 90
LOOT Cache 12
EXPERIENCE 1,400 +12/hp
Will-o-the-wisps are strange, fey creatures that resemble danc -
ing lights or torches. Living in desolate and dangerous locations, 
they seek to draw unwary travellers into bogs or quicksand. If a 
will-o-the-wisp is clearly faced with death in combat, it will bar-
gain the location of its treasure in exchange for freedom. 
Immunities and Resistances
Magic Immunity. Will-o-the-wisps are immune to all spells with 
the exception of protection from evil, magic missile, and maze.
Special Attacks
Electric Shock. The will o the wisp can deliver its electric shock at 
a range of up to 10ft.
WOLF
NORMAL DIRE
FREQUENCY Common Rare
NO. 
ENCOUNTERED 3d10 3d4
SIZE Small Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral Neutral
MOVE 180ft 180ft
ARMOUR CLASS 7 [13] 6 [14]
HIT DICE 2+2 3+3
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (1d4+1) 1 bite (2d4)
SENSES Keen Keen
LAIR CHANCE 10% 10%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4) Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 60 65
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 50 +2/hp 75 +3/hp
Wolves are predators that hunt in packs of up to 30. They are 
normally 26 to 30 inches tall at the shoulder, and males weigh 
80 to 100 lbs. A wolf pack will encircle prey, seeking to attack 
from behind. Once a pack has identified possible prey, they 
usually (75%) follow the group, waiting for the chance to attack.
Dire wolves are massive wolves, prehistoric ancestors of the 
normal species. Like normal wolves, they hunt in packs and 
their howl has a 50% chance to panic herbivores such as horses.
Special Features
Howl. A wolf pack’s howling may panic horses and other herbi-
vores (50% chance per animal).
Unarmed Combat. Wolves cannot be targeted with grappling or 
overbearing attacks. They may choose to overbear a lone op -
ponent. If not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ 
on 1d20. Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/na. Overbear Results Mods: 
ATK/DEF +0/na.
Unarmed Combat.  Dire wolves cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks. They may choose to overbear. If 
not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. 
Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF 
+2/na.

 CHAPTER TEN: MONSTER STATISTICS | 251
WOLF, WINTER
FREQUENCY Very rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 2d4
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
MOVE 180ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15]
HIT DICE 6
MELEE ATTACKS 1 bite (2d4)
SENSES Keen
LAIR CHANCE 10%
INTELLIGENCE Average (8-10)
MORALE 75
LOOT Hoard 9
EXPERIENCE 245 +5/hp
The winter wolf is a large, intelligent, and bad-tempered pred -
ator which is found only in the cold wastes. Its coat is typically 
silver or ivory in colour, and its eyes are ice blue.
Their coats are worth 5,000 gold pieces.
Immunities and Resistances
Immune to Cold. The wolf is immune to cold attacks.
Vulnerability to Fire.  Winter wolves take an extra point of dam -
age on each die rolled for a fire-based attack (example: a 5HD 
fireball inflicts +5 fire damage, which is also regardless of the 
wolf’s saving throw).
Special Attacks
Breathe Frost.  In addition to its bite, once every 10 rounds a 
winter wolf can breathe cold on all creatures within 10ft of its 
mouth. This icy breath causes 6d4 points of damage to the vic-
tim (half damage with a successful save vs. breath weapon).
Unarmed Combat. Winter wolves cannot be targeted with grap -
pling or overbearing attacks. They may choose to overbear. If 
not fended off, they score an unarmed hit with a 2+ on 1d20. 
Hit Mods: ATK/DEF +10/na. Overbear Results Mods: ATK/DEF 
+5/na.
Language
Winter wolves speak their own language, and are also able to 
talk to worgs.
WOLVERINE
NORMAL GIANT
FREQUENCY Uncommon Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 1 1
SIZE Small Medium
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil Neutral evil
MOVE 120ft 150ft
ARMOUR CLASS 5 [15] 4 [16]
HIT DICE 3 4+4
MELEE ATTACKS 2 claws (1d4) and 1 bite (1d4+1) 2 claws (1d6) and 1 bite (2d4)
SENSES Standard Standard
LAIR CHANCE 15% 15%
INTELLIGENCE Semi- (2-4) Semi- (2-4)
MORALE 60 70
LOOT Nil Nil
EXPERIENCE 125 +2/hp 235 +4/hp
Wolverines are deadly predators, resembling a cross between a 
huge weasel and a small bear. The creatures are just intelligent 
enough to be evil-aligned, and are intelligent enough to ambush 
prey. 
Giant wolverines are massive cousins to the normal wolverine, 
but they do not differ in terms of special abilities.
Special Attacks
Blinding Musk Spray. Wolverines can spray musk in a 60ft cone, 
which necessitates a saving throw vs poison or the victim will 
be blinded for 1d8 hours. Regardless of the saving throw’s re-
sult, the strength and dexterity scores of anyone caught in a 
musk cloud will effectively be halved.
Improved Attack Roll.  The savagery and speed of a wolverine 
grants them a +4 on all to-hit rolls.
Unarmed Combat. Wolverines cannot be targeted with grappling 
or overbearing attacks, and do not employ these tactics.

WORG
FREQUENCY Rare
NO. ENCOUNTERED 3d4
SIZE Large
ALIGNMENT Neutral evil
